member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Tools.AssemblyHandler
member.summary: Handles assembly-related operations such as loading, scanning for extensions, and managing driver configurations. Core partial class containing fields, properties, and constructor.
AssemblyHandler partial class - Driver Package Tracking Tracks which NuGet packages provide which drivers.
AssemblyHandler partial class - Helper Methods and Utilities
AssemblyHandler partial class - Assembly Loading Methods
AssemblyHandler partial class - NuGet Search, Download, and Package Operations Implements the new IAssemblyHandler NuGet members.
AssemblyHandler partial class - NuGet Source Management Manages custom NuGet package sources with persistence.
AssemblyHandler partial class - Assembly Scanning Methods
AssemblyHandler partial class - Load Statistics Tracks assembly loading performance and outcomes.

member.summary: Partial class for documentation, utilities, and advanced features
Enhanced ClassCreator with modular architecture using partial classes and helper pattern. Provides comprehensive class generation capabilities with separation of concerns.
Partial class for database-related class generation functionality
Partial class for DLL creation and compilation functionality
Entity class generation using EntitiesExtensions for type conversion
Partial class extending ClassCreator with POCO to Entity generation capabilities. Supports namespace scanning, runtime type loading, and datasource integration.
Partial class for testing and validation functionality
Partial class for Web API generation functionality

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.CacheManager
member.summary: Advanced cache manager with pluggable providers, stale-while-revalidate support, scoped provider access, and SLO-ready statistics. Supports multiple cache backends including In-Memory, MemoryCache, Redis, and custom providers.
Extension methods for cache operations to provide additional functionality.

member.summary: Gets a cached value or creates it using stale-while-revalidate semantics. Returns a (possibly stale) cached value immediately; if the entry is older than , a background refresh is triggered so the next caller will receive fresh data. The hard TTL () is when the item is fully evicted and the next call will block on the factory.
member.param: Async factory invoked on misses and background refreshes.
How long a value is considered fresh (serve without refresh).
How long the value stays in cache after softTtl passes.
Cancellation token (only used for blocking path).

An isolated, instance-based cache backed by a dedicated .
Use to obtain a scope. Each scope has its own provider (and therefore its own statistics, key namespace, and eviction policy), so components like ProxyDataSource can have per-datasource caches without sharing the global instance.

member.summary:
Fetch-or-create with stale-while-revalidate semantics.
If the key exists and is within , return it.
If the key is older than but the hard TTL () has not expired, return the stale value and trigger a background refresh.
On a hard miss, invoke synchronously.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheConnectionHelperExample
member.summary:
Comprehensive example demonstrating the usage of Cache Connection Helpers in the Beep Data Management Engine.
This class provides complete examples of: - Registering cache connection configurations - Creating cache data source connections - Working with different cache types (in-memory, distributed, hybrid) - Configuring connection properties for various cache systems - Best practices for cache configuration and usage
The Cache Connection Helpers support various caching technologies including: - Built-in Beep InMemoryCache - Microsoft Extensions Memory Cache - Redis distributed cache - Hazelcast in-memory data grid - Apache Ignite cache - Hybrid and multi-level caching systems

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample
member.summary:
Comprehensive example demonstrating the InMemoryCacheDataSource functionality.
This class provides complete examples of: - Initializing and configuring InMemoryCacheDataSource for in-memory data operations - Creating and managing entities with automatic schema discovery - Performing full CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete) on cached data - Advanced filtering and querying with multiple operators (equals, contains, greater than, etc.) - Paging support for large datasets with configurable page sizes - Entity structure management including custom entity creation - Integration with CacheManager for advanced caching scenarios - Performance testing and benchmarking of cache operations - Statistics collection and monitoring of cache performance - Proper resource cleanup and disposal patterns
The InMemoryCacheDataSource serves as a high-performance, thread-safe in-memory data store that implements the full IDataSource interface, making it compatible with the entire Beep data management ecosystem while providing microsecond-level response times.
Key Features Demonstrated: - Thread-safe concurrent operations using ConcurrentDictionary - Automatic entity structure inference from data objects - Support for complex filtering with multiple conditions - Integration with external cache providers for persistence - Memory-efficient data storage and retrieval - Comprehensive error handling and logging

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample._cacheDataSource
member.summary: The main InMemoryCacheDataSource instance used for all demonstration operations. Provides in-memory data storage with full IDataSource interface compatibility.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample._dmeEditor
member.summary: Data Management Editor interface providing access to configuration, logging, and other core Beep framework functionality.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample._logger
member.summary: Logger interface for recording operations, errors, and performance metrics throughout the example execution.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.IDMEEditor,TheTechIdea.Beep.Logger.IDMLogger)
member.summary:
Initializes a new instance of the CacheMemoryDataSourceExample class.
Sets up the example environment with the provided editor and logger instances, preparing for demonstration of InMemoryCacheDataSource capabilities.
member.param: The data management editor for framework integration
The logger for recording operations and events

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.RunBasicExample
member.summary:
Runs a comprehensive basic example demonstrating core InMemoryCacheDataSource functionality.
This method orchestrates a complete demonstration including: 1. Data source initialization and connection establishment 2. Sample data creation with multiple entity types (Users, Products) 3. Full CRUD operations with error handling and validation 4. Advanced filtering and querying capabilities 5. Entity management and structure discovery 6. Proper resource cleanup and disposal
The example uses realistic business data (users, products, orders) to show practical usage patterns and best practices for the InMemoryCacheDataSource.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.InitializeCacheDataSource
member.summary:
Initializes and configures the InMemoryCacheDataSource for demonstration.
This method: - Creates a new InMemoryCacheDataSource instance with proper configuration - Sets up error handling and logging integration - Establishes the connection to the in-memory cache - Validates the connection status and reports results
The data source is configured for in-memory cache operations with automatic entity discovery and thread-safe concurrent access.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.CreateSampleData
member.summary:
Creates comprehensive sample data to demonstrate various data operations.
This method populates the cache with: - User entities with diverse attributes (name, email, age, department) - Product entities with pricing and inventory information - Realistic business data for testing filtering and querying
Each insert operation is validated and logged, demonstrating proper error handling and success verification patterns.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.DemonstrateCrudOperations
member.summary:
Demonstrates comprehensive CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations.
This method showcases: - Reading all entities from a collection - Updating existing entities with new information - Deleting entities by primary key - Verifying changes with subsequent read operations - Proper error handling and status validation
Each operation is logged and verified to ensure data integrity and demonstrate proper usage patterns for the InMemoryCacheDataSource.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.DemonstrateQuerying
member.summary:
Demonstrates advanced filtering and querying capabilities.
This method showcases: - Single-condition filtering (department equals "Engineering") - Multi-condition filtering (category and price range) - Various filter operators (equals, less than, contains, etc.) - Paged result retrieval with configurable page sizes - Complex query patterns for business scenarios
The filtering system supports all standard comparison operators and can handle multiple conditions with implicit AND logic.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.DemonstrateEntityManagement
member.summary:
Demonstrates entity management and structure discovery capabilities.
This method showcases: - Listing all available entities in the data source - Inspecting entity structures and field definitions - Creating custom entity structures programmatically - Setting primary keys and field properties - Adding data to newly created entities
The entity management system supports automatic schema discovery from data objects as well as explicit entity structure definition.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.Cleanup
member.summary:
Performs proper cleanup and resource disposal.
This method demonstrates: - Proper connection closure - Resource disposal patterns - Exception handling during cleanup - Logging of cleanup operations
Following proper cleanup patterns is essential for memory management and preventing resource leaks in production applications.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.RunAdvancedExample
member.summary:
Demonstrates advanced cache operations with external cache provider integration.
This method showcases: - Custom cache configuration with specific settings - Integration with CacheManager for advanced scenarios - Direct cache provider access and manipulation - Performance testing and benchmarking - Statistics collection and analysis - Memory usage optimization techniques
The advanced example shows how to leverage the full power of the caching system for high-performance, production-ready applications.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.DemonstrateCacheIntegration
member.summary:
Demonstrates integration between InMemoryCacheDataSource and CacheManager.
This method shows: - Dual access to cached data through different interfaces - Cache key naming conventions and patterns - Cross-system data accessibility - Integration verification and validation
The integration allows data stored through InMemoryCacheDataSource to be accessible through the CacheManager API and vice versa.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.PerformanceTest
member.summary:
Performs comprehensive performance testing and benchmarking.
This method: - Measures insert performance for large datasets (1000+ records) - Tests filtering performance with complex conditions - Collects and reports cache statistics - Analyzes hit/miss ratios and memory usage - Provides performance metrics for optimization
Performance testing is essential for understanding the scalability and efficiency characteristics of the caching system.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Providers.HybridCacheProvider
member.summary:
Intelligent hybrid cache provider combining multiple caching layers for optimal performance.
This provider implements a sophisticated multi-tier caching strategy that automatically orchestrates between different cache providers to deliver optimal performance, reliability, and cost-effectiveness across diverse application scenarios:
**Multi-Tier Architecture:** - L1 Cache: Ultra-fast in-memory cache for hot data (microsecond access) - L2 Cache: Distributed cache layer for shared data across instances - L3 Cache: Persistent storage layer for long-term data retention - Intelligent data promotion and demotion between tiers - Automatic tier selection based on access patterns and data characteristics
**Smart Data Management:** - Heat-based data placement using access frequency analysis - Automatic data promotion for frequently accessed items - Intelligent cache warming and pre-loading strategies - Size-aware data placement optimization - TTL cascade management across cache tiers - Conflict resolution and consistency management
**Performance Optimization:** - Sub-millisecond access for L1 cached data - Intelligent read-through and write-through patterns - Batch operation optimization across tiers - Network request minimization through smart caching - CPU usage optimization through tier-appropriate algorithms - Memory usage balancing across cache layers
**Reliability & Consistency:** - Automatic failover between cache tiers - Data consistency guarantees across distributed nodes - Graceful degradation when tier providers fail - Conflict resolution for concurrent updates - Transaction-like semantics for multi-tier operations - Data integrity validation across cache boundaries
**Use Cases:** - Enterprise applications requiring both speed and scale - Multi-region deployments with local and global caching needs - Applications with diverse data access patterns - Systems requiring high availability with cost optimization - Microservices architectures with varying caching requirements - Applications transitioning between caching strategies
**Intelligent Features:** - Machine learning-based access pattern prediction - Automatic cache strategy optimization - Dynamic tier configuration based on system load - Predictive cache warming and eviction - Cost-aware data placement decisions - Performance bottleneck identification and resolution
**Scalability:** - Horizontal scaling through distributed tier management - Elastic scaling based on demand patterns - Cross-region data replication and synchronization - Load balancing across multiple cache instances - Partition tolerance and network split handling
**Monitoring & Analytics:** - Comprehensive tier performance analytics - Data access pattern analysis and visualization - Cost analysis and optimization recommendations - Real-time performance dashboards - Predictive capacity planning and scaling recommendations
**Configuration Flexibility:** - Declarative tier configuration and management - Runtime tier addition and removal - Policy-based data placement rules - Custom eviction and promotion strategies - Integration with existing cache infrastructure
**Enterprise Integration:** - Support for enterprise monitoring and alerting systems - Integration with cloud provider caching services - Compliance and auditing capabilities - Multi-tenancy support with isolated cache namespaces - API gateway and service mesh integration

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Providers.HybridCacheProvider.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.ICacheProvider,TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.ICacheProvider,TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.CacheConfiguration)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the HybridCacheProvider class.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Providers.InMemoryCacheProvider
member.summary:
High-performance in-memory cache provider with advanced features.
This provider is engineered for high-throughput, production-grade applications requiring sophisticated caching capabilities and optimal performance characteristics:
**Advanced Features:** - True LRU (Least Recently Used) eviction using LinkedList for O(1) operations - Dual data structure design (ConcurrentDictionary + LinkedList) for optimal access patterns - Advanced compression support for large cached objects (reduces memory footprint) - Sophisticated access order tracking with minimal performance impact - Enhanced statistics collection including access patterns and performance metrics - Fine-grained concurrency control with minimal lock contention
**Performance Optimizations:** - O(1) cache access and LRU updates using linked list nodes - Efficient memory usage through optional compression - Optimized for high-frequency read/write operations - Scalable to large datasets (100,000+ items) - Minimal allocation overhead with object pooling patterns
**Enterprise Features:** - Comprehensive performance monitoring and diagnostics - Advanced eviction policies with configurable thresholds - Support for cache warming and pre-loading scenarios - Integration hooks for external monitoring systems - Detailed access pattern analysis for optimization
**Use Cases:** - High-traffic web applications and APIs - Real-time data processing systems - Applications with strict performance SLAs - Systems requiring detailed cache analytics - Microservices architectures with high throughput requirements - Gaming and real-time applications
**Scalability:** - Designed for multi-core systems with high CPU counts - Efficient handling of concurrent read/write operations - Optimized memory access patterns for NUMA architectures - Supports horizontal scaling patterns
**Memory Management:** - Intelligent memory allocation strategies - Compression support for large objects - Proactive garbage collection optimization - Memory pressure detection and adaptive behavior
**Monitoring & Diagnostics:** - Real-time performance metrics - Access pattern analysis - Memory usage optimization recommendations - Performance bottleneck identification

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Providers.InMemoryCacheProvider.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.CacheConfiguration)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the InMemoryCacheProvider class.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Providers.MemoryCacheProvider
member.summary:
Enhanced memory cache provider with advanced memory management and size-aware operations.
This provider focuses on intelligent memory usage and provides enterprise-grade features for applications requiring precise memory control and advanced caching strategies:
**Memory Management Excellence:** - Size-based eviction policies with configurable memory limits - Real-time memory usage tracking and reporting - Intelligent compaction algorithms to optimize memory utilization - Memory pressure detection with adaptive eviction strategies - Configurable size limits with automatic enforcement - Memory-aware item prioritization and retention policies
**Advanced Caching Features:** - Hybrid LRU eviction based on both access time and memory consumption - Size estimation algorithms for accurate memory accounting - Configurable compaction percentage for memory optimization - Smart eviction targeting least valuable items first - Multiple eviction triggers (size, time, access patterns)
**Enterprise Capabilities:** - Precise memory limit enforcement with soft and hard boundaries - Advanced statistics including memory efficiency metrics - Integration with system memory monitoring - Proactive eviction to prevent out-of-memory conditions - Support for memory-constrained environments
**Use Cases:** - Memory-constrained server environments - Cloud applications with memory limits (containers, serverless) - Applications requiring predictable memory usage - Systems with varying memory availability - Embedded systems with limited resources - Multi-tenant applications with memory quotas
**Performance Characteristics:** - Optimized for memory efficiency over raw speed - Intelligent trade-offs between memory usage and performance - Adaptive behavior based on available system memory - Efficient handling of varying object sizes - Minimal memory fragmentation
**Memory Optimization:** - Dynamic size calculation for all cached objects - Memory usage forecasting and planning - Efficient memory reclamation strategies - Support for memory pressure callbacks - Integration with .NET garbage collection optimization
**Monitoring & Control:** - Real-time memory usage dashboards - Memory efficiency reporting and analysis - Configurable memory thresholds and alerts - Memory usage trending and capacity planning - Integration with application performance monitoring (APM) tools
**Configuration Flexibility:** - Granular memory limit configuration - Adjustable compaction strategies - Customizable eviction policies - Integration with system memory management

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Providers.MemoryCacheProvider.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.CacheConfiguration)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the MemoryCacheProvider class.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Providers.RedisCacheProvider
member.summary:
Distributed Redis cache provider for enterprise-scale, multi-node caching solutions.
This provider enables distributed caching across multiple application instances using Redis as the backing store, providing enterprise-grade scalability and reliability:
**Distributed Caching Excellence:** - Seamless integration with Redis clusters and standalone instances - Automatic failover and high availability support - Cross-application cache sharing and coordination - Network-optimized serialization and compression - Connection pooling and management for optimal performance - Support for Redis Sentinel and Cluster modes
**Enterprise Features:** - Distributed lock mechanisms for cache coordination - TTL (Time To Live) management with Redis native expiration - Pub/Sub integration for cache invalidation notifications - Support for Redis streams and advanced data structures - Authentication and SSL/TLS security integration - Multi-database support within Redis instances
**Scalability & Performance:** - Horizontal scaling across multiple Redis nodes - Connection pooling with configurable pool sizes - Pipelining support for batch operations - Compression algorithms optimized for network transfer - Efficient serialization protocols (JSON, MessagePack, Protocol Buffers) - Load balancing across Redis cluster nodes
**Use Cases:** - Multi-instance web applications requiring shared cache - Microservices architectures with distributed state - High-availability applications with zero-downtime requirements - Applications requiring cache persistence across restarts - Global applications with geographically distributed deployments - Session state management across load-balanced environments
**Reliability & Availability:** - Automatic connection recovery and retry mechanisms - Circuit breaker patterns for fault tolerance - Fallback strategies when Redis is unavailable - Data persistence options (RDB snapshots, AOF logs) - Master-slave replication support - Cluster sharding and partitioning
**Network Optimization:** - Intelligent connection management and pooling - Compression for large cached objects - Efficient serialization protocols - Network timeout and retry configuration - Bandwidth usage optimization
**Security Features:** - Redis AUTH authentication support - SSL/TLS encryption for data in transit - Access control and permission management - Secure connection string handling - Integration with enterprise security frameworks
**Monitoring & Diagnostics:** - Redis server statistics integration - Network latency and throughput monitoring - Connection health and status tracking - Detailed error reporting and diagnostics - Integration with Redis monitoring tools (Redis Insights, etc.)
**Configuration & Management:** - Flexible Redis configuration options - Support for multiple Redis endpoints - Database selection and namespace management - Connection string encryption and secure storage - Environment-specific configuration profiles

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Providers.RedisCacheProvider.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.CacheConfiguration)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the RedisCacheProvider class.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Providers.SimpleCacheProvider
member.summary:
Simple, working in-memory cache provider optimized for .NET 8/9.
This provider serves as the foundational, lightweight caching solution with essential features:
**Core Capabilities:** - Thread-safe operations using ConcurrentDictionary for high concurrency - Automatic expiration with configurable TTL (Time To Live) settings - JSON-based serialization for universal data type support - Background cleanup timer for expired item removal - Comprehensive statistics tracking (hits, misses, memory usage) - Simple LRU (Least Recently Used) eviction when capacity limits are reached
**Performance Characteristics:** - Optimized for moderate-scale applications (up to ~10,000 items) - Low memory overhead with efficient data structures - Fast access times for cached items (microsecond range) - Minimal CPU overhead for background maintenance
**Use Cases:** - Development and testing environments - Small to medium applications with basic caching needs - Fallback provider for more complex caching scenarios - Applications requiring simple, reliable caching without external dependencies - Microservices with lightweight caching requirements
**Configuration Options:** - Default expiry time for cached items - Maximum item count before eviction - Cleanup interval for expired item removal - Key prefix for namespacing - Statistics collection enable/disable
**Thread Safety:** All operations are thread-safe and can handle high concurrency scenarios without requiring external synchronization mechanisms.
**Memory Management:** Uses efficient memory allocation patterns and provides automatic cleanup of expired items to prevent memory leaks in long-running applications.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.FileDataSource
member.summary: Connection lifecycle partial — open, close, transactions, query routing. All format-specific work is delegated to .
CRUD partial — Insert, Update, Delete, bulk UpdateEntities. All file write operations are delegated to .

Format-agnostic file data source. The actual file parsing is delegated to an resolved at connection-open time via using .
Supported formats (built-in): CSV, TSV, JSON, Text. Additional formats can be registered with .
Diagnostics / validation partial — ValidateRow, CanConvert, dead-letter helpers.
Governance partial — access policy enforcement, row-level security, field masking. Phase 7 implementation.
Query / read partial — GetEntity, paging, async, GetScalar. Delegates all file parsing to the set in Connection.cs. Filter evaluation is format-agnostic (works on projected string values).
Reader host surface for listing/switching file readers at runtime.

Schema inference partial — GetEntityStructure, CreateEntityAs, CreateEntities. Delegates header reading and type inference to . Phase 3: uses confidence-scored for per-column type and quality statistics.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.Registry.FileReaderRegistry
member.summary:
Discovers, caches, and auto-registers implementations that are decorated with .
Uses IDMEEditor.assemblyHandler.ConfigEditor.FileReaderClasses for discovery — the same mechanism as [AddinAttribute] connectors and [PipelinePluginAttribute] ETL plugins.
Calling also calls for each found reader so that all existing code that calls FileReaderFactory.GetReader(type) continues to work transparently.
uses for core readers.
Typical usage:
var registry = new FileReaderRegistry(editor); registry.Discover(); // finds all [FileReader(…)]-attributed classes var reader = registry.Create(DataSourceType.Parquet);

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.Registry.FileReaderRegistry.RegisterAttributedReadersFromAssembly(System.Reflection.Assembly)
member.summary: Registers all attributed file readers from a given assembly. Used by before an editor exists.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.Registry.FileReaderRegistry.Discover
member.summary:
Scans all assemblies loaded by AssemblyHandler and registers every concrete class that implements and carries a .
Each discovered reader is also registered in so existing code that calls FileReaderFactory.GetReader(type) works without changes.
Safe to call multiple times — existing descriptors are updated (re-discovery).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.Registry.FileReaderRegistry.Create(TheTechIdea.Beep.Utilities.DataSourceType)
member.summary: Creates a fresh instance of the reader registered for . The reader is created with its default (no-argument) constructor — readers do not require IDMEEditor.
member.exception: No plugin was discovered for the given .

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.Registry.FileReaderRegistry.GetAll
member.summary: Returns all currently discovered descriptors.

member.summary: Returns true if a plugin for is registered.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.DataView.DataViewDataSource
member.summary: Represents a data source for a data view.
Partial: Entity Lifecycle operations — move, rename, duplicate, reorder, get by name.
Partial: Field/Column management — add, remove, get, refresh schema.
Partial: Filter Management — per-entity WHERE filters stored in EntityStructure.CustomBuildQuery.
Partial: Join Graph Intelligence — auto-detect joins, shortest path, adjacency list.
Partial: Query and execution — preview, cache status, async materialize, execution plan.
Partial: Validation and health-check operations.
Partial: View-level utilities — clone, merge, datasource list, summary.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Audit.FileDistributedAuditSink
member.summary: Durable that appends audit events as JSON-lines to a rolling daily file: {directory}/distributed-audit-YYYYMMDD.jsonl.

remarks.para: Implementation notes:

item.description: Writes happen from a bounded background queue so the hot path never blocks on disk I/O.
is applied to the , , and fields so secrets embedded in SQL / exceptions never land on disk.
The sink owns a background writer thread that is torn down deterministically by .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Catalog.IShardCatalog
member.summary: Thread-safe registry of records. Implementations MUST allow concurrent / reads with in-flight / writes without throwing, and the snapshot returned MUST be a stable copy safe to enumerate after subsequent mutations.
member.remarks: The catalog is the single source of truth for "which shard IDs exist right now". The references shards by ID; validates the plan against the live catalog and raises for any unknown shard id.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Catalog.Shard
member.summary: Immutable identity record for a single distributed shard. Each shard wraps an existing (the HA pool that actually owns the rows) plus weight / tag metadata used by the router and rebalancer.
member.remarks: The cluster reference itself is not owned by the shard — the caller that constructed the cluster is responsible for its lifetime. only stores references and never disposes them on its own.

member.remarks: The catalog does not own the instances it stores — disposing the catalog does NOT dispose the clusters. Lifetime is the responsibility of the caller that built the clusters (typically the same code that constructs ).

Loading requires a factory because instances cannot be reconstructed from config alone — they own nodes, transports, and credentials that must be wired up by the host application.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSource
member.summary: partial — Phase 13 audit + access policy surface. Exposes an accessor, convenience RaiseAuditEvent emitters used by sibling partials, and an helper that throws when the configured policy denies a caller.

First-class that distributes entities (tables) and rows across multiple physical datasources. Each shard is itself an HA pool implemented by an existing , so per-shard failover, circuit breaking, and load balancing are reused from the Proxy/ tier rather than reinvented.

remarks.para: This file holds the partial-class root: private fields, constructors, identity properties, and the topology accessors. Behaviour is split across sibling partials:

list.item: DistributedDataSource.Plan.cs — plan management and shard-catalog validation (Phase 02).
DistributedDataSource.IDataSource.cs — stubs that throw in Phase 01 and are filled in by Phase 05+.

member.summary: partial — events declared by and plus internal Raise* helpers used by sibling partials.
member.remarks: All Raise* helpers swallow handler exceptions: the distributed datasource sits on the read/write hot path and a faulty subscriber must never be able to break a query. Failures are surfaced through with a well-known message so they remain observable.
member.summary: partial — placeholder implementations of the data-access surface. Phase 01 deliberately throws from every method, naming the phase that will fill it in. This guarantees a callable but honest skeleton: existing consumers can compile against the new type immediately, and any premature runtime use surfaces a clear error rather than silently succeeding.
member.remarks:
Phase mapping:
Phase 02 — entity / metadata accessors back the catalog plumbing.
Phase 06 — read methods (GetEntity*, RunQuery, GetScalar*).
Phase 07 — write methods (InsertEntity, UpdateEntity*, DeleteEntity).
Phase 09 — transaction methods (BeginTransaction, Commit, EndTransaction).
Phase 12 — schema methods (CreateEntityAs, CreateEntities, RunScript, GetCreateEntityScript).
member.summary: partial — connection lifecycle (, ) and orchestration across the composed shard clusters.

remarks.para: walks every shard cluster and requests Openconnection; the aggregate state is only when all shards opened successfully, otherwise it falls back to . When is false the method short-circuits and reports immediately so shards are opened lazily by the executors in later phases.

is idempotent: a second call is a no-op. Disposal walks every shard cluster, swallows any individual failure (so a misbehaving cluster cannot block the others), and surfaces the aggregated message via .

member.summary: partial — Phase 13 observability surface: metrics aggregator, hot-shard/hot-entity events, and scoping.
member.remarks: The aggregator is lazy-built on first access so tests that opt out of metrics allocate nothing. All calls are resilient to subscriber exceptions — the hot path is never broken by a faulty listener.
member.summary: partial — Phase 14 capacity engineering surface: global + per-shard concurrency gates, per-shard token-bucket rate limiter, adaptive timeouts, and hot-shard read shedding.

remarks.para: The gate, limiter, and mitigator are lazy-built on first use so tests that opt out of Phase 14 allocate nothing. The mitigator attaches itself to the metrics aggregator exactly once per lifetime.
All helpers return safely when is false so the executors can unconditionally call into this partial without branching every line.

member.summary: partial — distribution plan management and validation against the live shard catalog. Extracts the plan-related concerns out of the constructor partial so Phase 11 (resharding) can extend behaviour here without touching identity, lifecycle, or the surface.
partial — Phase 08 query planner entry points. Exposes the richer -based read API without disturbing the Phase 06 IDataSource stubs in DistributedDataSource.Reads.cs.

remarks.para: Callers that want cross-shard ORDER BY, TOP, GROUP BY, or AVG semantics should build a and call . The lower-level GetEntity / RunQuery calls in DistributedDataSource.Reads.cs continue to use the Phase 06 union-merge path for backward compatibility.

A caller-supplied shard executor delegate converts the per-shard sub-intent into an call (for example, by rendering the intent to shard-native SQL). Callers that do not need aggregate pushdown can use , which dispatches each shard's sub-intent via .

member.summary: partial — Phase 06 read dispatch. Each read method builds a via the Phase 05 router, hands the decision to the , and returns the merged result.

remarks.para: Dispatch rules:

list.item: GetEntity(name, filters) — routes via . Single-shard, scatter, or replicated path is chosen from the decision flags.
RunQuery(sql) — has no parsed filters in Phase 06, so it always routes by entity metadata (broadcast / replicated / scatter). SQL-parsed key extraction is Phase 08.
GetScalar(sql) — same policy as RunQuery; the basic merger sums per-shard scalars, matching the typical COUNT / SUM use-case.

remarks.para: RunQuery / GetScalar currently require the caller to tag the originating entity via or via a separate routing hook. Without an entity hint the executor falls back to a broadcast across every live shard, which matches the "safe default" Phase 06 ships.

member.summary: partial — Phase 11 resharding surface. Exposes the , the in-memory , and async wrappers for add-shard, remove-shard, move-entity, repartition, and apply-plan calls. Every operation runs through the same Shadow → DualWrite → Cutover → Off window protocol so the router and write executors stay in lockstep while data moves.

partial — Phase 10 resilience wiring. Owns the and instances, forwards their events through the datasource's safe-invoke raise helpers, and exposes hot-path helpers the read / write executors use to filter unhealthy shards.

remarks.para: Lifetime contract:

list.item: The monitor + circuit breaker are constructed in 's ctor.

Polling is started inside when
is true.
The monitor itself is disposed in .

member.summary: partial — entity-name placement resolution and routing diagnostics. Wraps the Phase 03 with safety checks (disposal, unmapped policy enforcement) and event emission (, ).
partial — Phase 12 schema surface. Exposes the orchestrator and forwards the DDL members (originally stubbed in DistributedDataSource.IDataSource.cs) into that service.

remarks.para: The service is created lazily through . Callers that only need the high-level surface should prefer the typed methods (, , etc.).
The classic overloads (, , , ) preserve their synchronous signatures by blocking on the async service; Phase 12 logs the blocking call through so mixed-mode callers remain discoverable.

member.summary: partial — adapter that exposes the private shard map and event raisers to the Phase 06 read executor via the contract. Defined as a nested type so the executor can live in its own test assembly without the datasource leaking private state.

partial — Phase 09 transaction surface. Implements the transaction triple (BeginTransaction / Commit / EndTransaction) as a thin wrapper around the and exposes the richer distributed-scope API for callers that need 2PC or saga semantics.

remarks.para:
The triple only targets a single shard (derived from or, when the datasource owns exactly one shard, that shard). This matches Oracle Forms / classic Beep expectations: plug the distributed datasource into existing IDataSource consumers without behaviour changes as long as their work lives on one shard. Multi-shard transactions MUST go through the explicit
API so the caller opts into atomic 2PC or saga semantics.

The single-flight model keeps one implicit per datasource instance: a second BeginTransaction without a prior commit/rollback is rejected. The explicit distributed API returns a fresh scope token and is therefore safe to use concurrently when callers own their own scopes.

member.summary: partial — Phase 07 write dispatch. Each write method routes a via the Phase 05 router, hands the decision to the , and converts the resulting into an . Partial failures (quorum met but a replica diverged) surface via .

remarks.para: Dispatch rules — strictly derived from :

list.item: Single target shard, no scatter → single-shard write (quorum implicit).
Replicated / broadcast fan-out → parallel fan-out with quorum resolved from and per-call .
Scatter sharded write (key missing, multiple shards, == true) → opt-in scatter delete path (requires ).

remarks.para:
ExecuteSql requires an entity hint via
(future phase) or a caller-supplied . Until the Phase 08 SQL parser lands, ExecuteSql without a hint broadcasts across every live shard under — safe for idempotent DDL-ish "catch-all" statements and noisy for anything else. Phase 12 replaces this with a dedicated DDL broadcast helper.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSource.GetCurrentPlan
member.summary: Returns the currently active plan. Equivalent to but exposed as a method to match Phase 02 wording in the design doc.

remarks.para: Synchronous plan swap: validates the target plan against the live shard catalog and replaces the active plan reference without moving any data. Use this overload when the caller knows the delta requires no data movement (pure version bumps, new empty entities, or additions to broadcast placements).

For live topology changes that require data migration (moves, repartitions, shard drains), call
instead — it routes the diff through the Phase 11 resharding pipeline.

member.summary: Validates the supplied plan against the live shard map and raises for every detected problem. Validation never throws — violations are surfaced via events so callers can decide whether to proceed with a degraded plan or roll back.
member.param: Plan to validate; is treated as valid.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSource.ExecuteQueryIntent(TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.QueryIntent)
member.summary:
Executes using a default shard executor that calls
with the pushdown filters from the sub-intent. Aggregates are folded by the merger from the raw rows each shard returns.
member.remarks: The default executor is deliberately conservative: it pushes only filters to each shard, which means SUM / MAX / AVG are computed on the coordinator side from every candidate row. For large rowsets prefer the overload that accepts a custom shard executor so aggregates can run on each shard's native engine.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions
member.summary: Tuning knobs for a instance. Phase 01 ships only the timeout / parallelism / disposal defaults. Phase 06 adds MaxScatterParallelism and ScatterFailurePolicy; Phase 07 adds MaxFanOutParallelism; Phase 10 adds MinimumHealthyShardRatio; Phase 14 adds the performance/back-pressure block.
member.remarks: All fields are public settable POCO properties so the options instance can be loaded from ConfigEditor JSON without custom converters. New fields must be appended; consumers must tolerate unknown values to keep persisted configs forward compatible.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.DefaultPerShardTimeoutMs
member.summary: Default per-shard timeout applied to read and write operations when the caller does not specify one. A value of 0 disables the per-shard timeout.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.DefaultReadDeadlineMs
member.summary: Total deadline applied to a distributed read that scatters across multiple shards (Phase 06). A value of 0 disables the deadline.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.MaxScatterParallelism
member.summary: Maximum degree of parallelism for scatter reads. Phase 06 honours this cap when issuing per-shard reads.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.MaxFanOutParallelism
member.summary: Maximum degree of parallelism for replicated / broadcast write fan-out. Phase 07 honours this cap.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.DisposeDrainTimeoutMs
member.summary: Timeout applied to while waiting for in-flight operations on each shard to drain. Defaults to 5 seconds.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.EagerOpenShards
member.summary: When true (default) the distributed datasource opens every shard cluster eagerly during . When false shards are opened lazily on first use.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.UnmappedPolicy
member.summary: Behaviour when the active contains no placement matching an entity name. Defaults to so missing placements fail loudly during development.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.DefaultShardIdForUnmapped
member.summary: Shard id used by . Required when is set to that value; ignored otherwise. Validated when the resolver is built.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.AllowScatterWrite
member.summary: When false (default) a write against a entity that does not supply a partition-key value is rejected via . Set to true to allow scatter writes (fan out the same payload to every live shard); useful for reference / lookup tables that happen to live in a sharded family.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.ScatterFailurePolicy
member.summary: Policy applied when one or more shards in a scatter read fail or time out. Defaults to so a single slow shard does not take down the whole query; flip to or for strict reads. Consumed by the Phase 06 read executor.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.ReplicatedReadPolicy
member.summary: Policy used by the Phase 06 read executor to pick a shard for a read that resolves to more than one live shard. Defaults to (the first id in the placement's ordered list) which keeps reads deterministic; flip to to spread load.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.PreferSagaOverTwoPhaseCommit
member.summary: When true, the Phase 09 always picks for multi-shard work, even when every shard advertises . Defaults to false (prefer 2PC when available).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.TransactionDeadlineMs
member.summary: Total deadline applied to a distributed transaction scope from BeginTransaction through Commit / Rollback. V1 surfaces the value for observability (logged at scope open); Phase 13 enforces it via cancellation. A value of 0 disables the deadline.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.EnableInMemoryTransactionLog
member.summary: When true (default) the coordinator keeps an in-memory transaction log for observability. Disable only for tests that need to compare memory allocation baselines — durability is a Phase 13 feature either way.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.ShardDownPolicy
member.summary: Per-mode configuration plus the distributed circuit-breaker knobs. Never null; replaced atomically via at runtime.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.MinimumHealthyShardRatio
member.summary: Minimum ratio of healthy shards required to execute a scatter read or broadcast write. When the observed ratio falls below this threshold the executor raises and throws instead of returning partial data. Defaults to 0.5; a value of 0 disables the gate entirely.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.HealthMonitorIntervalMs
member.summary: Poll interval for the background . A value of 0 disables polling (the monitor still tracks hot-path successes and failures). Defaults to 5 seconds.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.EnableHealthMonitor
member.summary: When true (default) the distributed datasource starts the in and disposes it in . Disable for tests that drive health updates manually.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.IdentityColumnPolicy
member.summary:
Policy applied by
when a entity carries a database-generated identity column. Defaults to so existing schemas keep working; switch to
once a distributed sequence provider is configured.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.SequenceProvider
member.summary: Optional used by Phase 07 sharded inserts to generate collision-free ids when the entity's primary key would otherwise rely on per-shard identity. null leaves id generation to the shard provider (the default).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.EnableDistributedMetrics
member.summary: When true (default) the Phase 13 metrics aggregator is installed and executors record per-request samples; disable for tests that need byte-for-byte allocation baselines.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.MetricsAggregator
member.summary: Optional custom metrics aggregator. When null a default is constructed against the live shard map.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.HotShardP95ThresholdMs
member.summary: p95 latency (ms) above which a shard is flagged hot. Defaults to 500 ms; set 0 to disable hot-shard detection (the detector still tracks samples).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.HotShardConsecutiveWindows
member.summary: Number of consecutive windows above the p95 threshold required before OnHotShardDetected fires. Defaults to 3.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.HotEntityThresholdRps
member.summary: Requests-per-second threshold that trips OnHotEntityDetected; defaults to 500.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.AuditSink
member.summary: Audit sink wired to every distribution-tier decision point. Defaults to ; swap in (or a bridge to ) for production.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.RedactAuditFields
member.summary: When true (default) audit events carry redacted partition keys and messages; disable only when writing to an already-trusted sink.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.AccessPolicy
member.summary: Optional access policy gating every read/write/DDL call. Defaults to so existing deployments keep working.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.DurableTransactionLogDirectory
member.summary: Optional directory for the durable . When null the coordinator keeps the Phase 09 in-memory log (subject to ).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.Performance
member.summary: Phase 14 back-pressure and capacity controls. Never null; the default instance preserves existing Phase 06 / 07 behaviour with generous caps.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedDataSourceOptions.RateLimiter
member.summary: Optional external rate limiter. When null the builds a from .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedExecutionContext
member.summary: Per-call context that follows a distributed operation through resolution, execution, and observability hooks. Carries a stable correlation id so a single request can be stitched together across multiple emissions, executor logs, and (Phase 13) audit records.

remarks.para: The context is intentionally tiny and value-style: copy it across executor boundaries instead of mutating it in place. Use to derive a new context with an extra tag.
Phase 03 only attaches contexts to the routing partial; later phases (read/write executors, transactions) carry the same instance forward so all events share the same correlation id.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedExecutionContext.CorrelationId
member.summary: Stable id used to correlate every event emitted for this operation.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedExecutionContext.OperationName
member.summary: Logical operation name (e.g. "GetEntity", "InsertEntity").

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedExecutionContext.EntityName
member.summary: Entity targeted by the operation; may be empty for plan-level calls.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedExecutionContext.IsWrite
member.summary: True for write-side operations; false for reads.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedExecutionContext.StartedUtc
member.summary: UTC timestamp captured at context creation.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedExecutionContext.Tags
member.summary: Read-only tag bag; use to derive an enriched context.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedExecutionContext.New(System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a new context with a freshly generated correlation id.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedExecutionContext.FromCorrelation(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a context with a caller-supplied correlation id (e.g. from an upstream trace).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedExecutionContext.WithTag(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns a new context with an additional tag. The current instance is left unchanged so the context can be safely shared across executor boundaries.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributionMode
member.summary: Per-entity placement / replication strategy used by . The mode is recorded inside the EntityPlacement attached to a DistributionPlan in Phase 02; the router and executor partials introduced in later phases consult the mode to decide whether to route to a single shard, scatter, fan-out, or broadcast.
member.remarks: New modes must be appended to keep persisted plans backward compatible. Numeric values are explicit so a stored plan from an earlier version always resolves to the same mode after upgrade.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributionMode.Routed
member.summary: Entity-level placement: the entire entity lives on exactly one shard. Reads and writes are routed to that shard; no row-level partitioning is performed.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributionMode.Sharded
member.summary: Row-level sharding: rows of the entity are split across N shards via the entity's partition function (Phase 04). Point reads use the partition key; range/scatter reads visit the candidate shards reported by the partition function.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributionMode.Replicated
member.summary: Replicated: the entity exists on every shard. Writes fan out to all shards (Phase 07 quorum policy), reads can be served by any healthy shard.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributionMode.Broadcast
member.summary: Broadcast (reference data): reads can be served by any shard, writes go to every shard. Distinguished from only by the read policy: broadcast reads pick the closest healthy shard rather than respecting any consistency window.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.DegradedModeEventArgs
member.summary:
Raised when the distribution tier detects that the healthy shard ratio has fallen below
(Phase 10). The datasource continues to honour single-shard routed calls that target a healthy shard, but scatter reads and broadcast writes are throttled or rejected until the ratio recovers.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.DegradedModeEventArgs.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.Double,System.Double,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new degraded-mode event.
member.param: Snapshot of currently healthy shard ids. Must not be null.
Snapshot of currently unhealthy shard ids. Must not be null.
Healthy ratio observed when the event was raised, in [0.0, 1.0].
Optional human-readable reason (e.g. "scatter aborted", "broadcast write throttled").

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.DegradedModeEventArgs.HealthyShardIds
member.summary: Shards classified as healthy at the moment of the event.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.DegradedModeEventArgs.UnhealthyShardIds
member.summary: Shards classified as unhealthy at the moment of the event.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.DegradedModeEventArgs.HealthyRatio
member.summary: Healthy ratio observed (0.0 to 1.0 inclusive).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.DegradedModeEventArgs.Threshold
member.summary: Configured minimum healthy ratio.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.DegradedModeEventArgs.Reason
member.summary: Optional reason captured by the raiser.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.DegradedModeEventArgs.TimestampUtc
member.summary: UTC timestamp the event was raised.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PartialBroadcastEventArgs
member.summary: Raised when a broadcast write was issued with one or more shards excluded for being unhealthy (Phase 10). The write itself may still have satisfied its quorum — this event is strictly informational so operators (or a Phase 13 reconciler) can replay the missed shards after they recover.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PartialBroadcastEventArgs.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new partial-broadcast event.
member.param: Shards the executor actually fired against.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PartialBroadcastEventArgs.EntityName
member.summary: Entity the broadcast targeted.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PartialBroadcastEventArgs.Operation
member.summary: Operation name (e.g. "InsertEntity", "ExecuteSql").

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PartialBroadcastEventArgs.AttemptedShardIds
member.summary: Shards the executor fired against.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PartialBroadcastEventArgs.SkippedShardIds
member.summary: Unhealthy shards that were skipped.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PartialBroadcastEventArgs.Reason
member.summary: Optional reason captured by the raiser.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PartialBroadcastEventArgs.TimestampUtc
member.summary: UTC timestamp the event was raised.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PartialReplicationFailureEventArgs
member.summary: Raised by after a replicated / broadcast write satisfied its quorum but one or more target shards still failed. Operators subscribe to reconcile the divergent replicas (e.g. replay the missing write, invalidate a stale cache, alert).

remarks.para: Full failure (quorum NOT satisfied) is reported via the normal return path and does NOT raise this event — this signal is specifically about the "quorum met but not every replica ack'd" case that benefits from a lightweight reconciliation hook rather than a hard error.
The full per-shard breakdown is exposed via ; subscribers should not mutate the outcome.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PartialReplicationFailureEventArgs.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.WriteOutcome)
member.summary: Creates a new partial-failure event.
member.param: Write outcome produced by the executor. Must have == true.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PartialReplicationFailureEventArgs.Outcome
member.summary: Full write outcome — per-shard results, quorum state, first error.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PartialReplicationFailureEventArgs.RaisedUtc
member.summary: UTC timestamp captured when the event was raised.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PartialReplicationFailureEventArgs.EntityName
member.summary: Convenience accessor for .

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PartialReplicationFailureEventArgs.Operation
member.summary: Convenience accessor for .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PlacementViolationEventArgs
member.summary: Raised when a routing or plan-application step encounters a configuration that violates the active distribution plan — for example a missing entity placement, a placement that points to an unknown shard ID, or a row whose partition key cannot be extracted. Phase 02 is the first phase to actually raise this; Phase 01 only declares the event so observability/log wiring can be set up early.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PlacementViolationEventArgs.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PlacementViolationSeverity)
member.summary: Initialises a new placement-violation event payload.
member.param: Logical entity name involved in the violation. Never null.
Shard that was requested but missing / invalid; may be null if the entity itself is unmapped.
Human-readable description suitable for logs and audit.
Severity classification used by listeners to decide between log / alert / fail-fast.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PlacementViolationEventArgs.EntityName
member.summary: Logical entity name involved in the violation.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PlacementViolationEventArgs.ShardId
member.summary: Shard identifier that the plan referenced, when applicable. null when the entity itself has no placement.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Events.PlacementViolationEventArgs.Reason
member.summary: Human-readable description of the violation.

member.summary: Default Phase 06 : concatenates rows and sums scalar / count values. Zero-knowledge of SQL semantics — suitable for GetEntity(filters) fan-outs and COUNT(*)-style scalars. Phase 08 replaces it with the query-aware merger for ORDER BY / LIMIT / AVG / MIN / MAX.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.DistributedReadExecutor
member.summary: Phase 06 implementation of . Partial-class root holds the ctor, shared fields, and helpers that would otherwise be duplicated across every behaviour partial.

remarks.para:
Files in this partial family:
DistributedReadExecutor.cs — ctor, dispatch helpers, guards.
DistributedReadExecutor.SingleShard.cs — single-shard path.
DistributedReadExecutor.Scatter.cs — scatter fan-out + merger.
DistributedReadExecutor.Replicated.cs — replicated read + failover.
The executor is stateless once constructed (the options snapshot is captured at build time). The parent rebuilds the executor only when options themselves change, not on every plan swap.

member.summary:
partial — replicated read path. Picks a single shard from according to
and fails over to the next live shard when the chosen one throws.

remarks.para: Each failed replica is reported via so operators see why a hot replica is being skipped. The final exception is rethrown only when every replica failed.
Per-shard HA (node-level failover inside a cluster) is already handled by ; this method adds a cross-cluster failover so a degraded cluster can be skipped entirely.

member.summary: partial — scatter / fan-out read path. Issues the same operation against every live shard in , applies the configured , then defers shape-specific merging to the .

remarks.para:
Parallelism is capped by
via a ; synchronous scatters run each shard on its own so the cap applies uniformly to sync and async paths.

The whole scatter is subject to
when the caller did not supply a stricter token.

member.summary: partial — single-shard path. No fan-out, no merging; the executor delegates straight to the resolved . Per-shard HA (node failover, retries, load balancing) is the cluster's job.

member.summary: Creates a new executor bound to the supplied shard map and merger.
member.param: Result merger used by scatter paths; defaults to .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.DistributedWriteExecutor
member.summary: Phase 07 implementation of . Partial-class root holds the ctor, shared fields, quorum derivation, and helpers shared by the behaviour partials.

remarks.para:
Files in this partial family:
DistributedWriteExecutor.cs — ctor, quorum resolution, helpers.
DistributedWriteExecutor.SingleShard.cs — single-shard path.
DistributedWriteExecutor.FanOut.cs — replicated / broadcast fan-out.
DistributedWriteExecutor.ScatterDelete.cs — opt-in scatter writes.
Stateless once constructed; the parent rebuilds the executor only when the options snapshot changes.

member.summary: Fan-out write path for . Covers replicated writes and broadcast writes (which are replicated writes with TargetShardIds = full catalog). Quorum is resolved via so both plan-derived and caller-overridden policies are honoured.

remarks.para: Per-shard calls run in parallel, bounded by . The implementation uses so the per-shard IDataSource calls (which are typically synchronous) don't block the caller's thread. Failures never cancel siblings — callers that want fail-fast semantics pick and treat any non-quorum outcome as failure.
The returned always contains one leg per target shard, even if the quorum was satisfied early; this keeps audit / reconciliation telemetry honest.

member.summary: Scatter write path for . Used for sharded "delete-by-filter" (and similar) operations when the partition key is missing but the caller explicitly wants the write broadcast to every shard.

remarks.para:
Opt-in only: the caller MUST set to true (or
must be true). This prevents accidental mass writes when a key was just forgotten; the message identifies the missing opt-in so the caller knows what to flip.
Scatter writes always require — a partial scatter-delete is almost never what the caller wanted. Use replicated/broadcast writes when you want quorum semantics.

member.summary: Single-shard write path for . Used for sharded writes that routed to exactly one shard via the partition function. Quorum is implicitly "the one shard must ack."

member.summary:
Derives the effective quorum policy + required ack count from and . Follows these rules:
Caller override on wins.
Otherwise use from the placement: 0 means "all", anything else implies .

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.DistributedWriteExecutor.CollectSkipped(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String})
member.summary: Computes the set of shards present in but absent from , preserving the original attempted order so operators see deterministic payloads.

Runs the fan-out while synthesising a skipped-shard for every shard filtered out by the resilience tier. Quorum evaluation sees the full attempted set so stays honest when a partial broadcast cannot make quorum.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.DistributedWriteOptions
member.summary: Per-call write hints consumed by the Phase 07 . Long-lived knobs live on ; these override behaviour for a single call without mutating the shared options instance.

remarks.para: Callers create a fresh instance per write. All properties are nullable so an unset property means "inherit the datasource default." Implementations MUST treat null properties as "no override" — never fall back to default(T).

Use when you just want the plan's quorum and no scatter delete. The type is deliberately a class (not a struct) so callers can build and share a pre-configured instance across a loop of related writes.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.DistributedWriteOptions.Default
member.summary: Shared "use defaults" singleton.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.DistributedWriteOptions.AllowScatterWrite
member.summary: When true a write against a entity that is missing a partition key is allowed to scatter across every live shard. Typical use-case: "delete rows matching this filter everywhere." Opt-in because the default path would otherwise throw . Per-call variant of ; null inherits from the datasource option.

Override the quorum policy for this call.
null — use the default derived from .
— strict.
— tolerate one outage with RF=3.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.DistributedWriteOptions.EntityNameHint
member.summary: Optional entity-name hint for ExecuteSql or similar "bring your own SQL" write paths. When omitted the write executor errors out with a routing exception because it cannot decide which shard(s) own the statement. Phase 12 extends this with a SQL parser that infers the entity from the statement body.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.IDistributedReadExecutor
member.summary: Turns a into the physical read calls against one or more shards and merges the per-shard results into a single logical result for consumers.

remarks.para: The executor never calls the router itself. Callers () resolve routing first, then hand the decision here. This separation makes the executor trivially testable against a fake without wiring a real plan.
Phase 06 ships only the read path. Writes live in Phase 07's IDistributedWriteExecutor; transactions in Phase 09.

member.summary: Phase 14 hook. Returns an adaptive deadline (ms) for a per-shard call, blending the caller's with the shard's observed p95. Default returns unchanged.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.NullDisposable
member.summary: Sentinel returned by capacity hooks that are not wired; disposing it is a no-op.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.IDistributedWriteExecutor
member.summary: Turns a write-side into the physical write calls, applies the quorum policy, and produces a the datasource can convert into .

remarks.para: Unlike the Phase 06 read executor the write executor does not throw on per-shard failure — every outcome is collected and reported via . The datasource inspects and to decide between Errors.Ok, partial success (event-only), and Errors.Failed.

The executor intentionally does NOT know about write methods directly. Callers pass a describing the per-shard operation; this keeps the executor usable from future Phase 12 DDL broadcast paths without API churn.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.IDistributedWriteExecutor.ExecuteSingleShard(TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Routing.RoutingDecision,System.Func{TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.IProxyCluster,TheTechIdea.Beep.ConfigUtil.IErrorsInfo},TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributedExecutionContext)
member.summary: Executes the supplied against the exactly one shard named in . Quorum is implicitly AtLeastN=1; the returned outcome carries one leg.
Fans out across every shard in using . Applies the effective quorum policy (derived from the decision + options + caller override) to produce a . Typical caller: replicated or broadcast writes.
Executes a sharded-scatter write (e.g. delete-by-filter) across every shard in . Requires (or the datasource-wide flag) to be true; otherwise throws . Quorum is always All for scatter writes.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.IResultMerger
member.summary: Strategy for combining per-shard read results produced by the Phase 06 scatter executor into a single logical result handed back to the caller.

remarks.para: Phase 06 ships which concatenates row sequences and sums scalar / count values. Phase 08 introduces a query-aware merger (QueryAwareResultMerger) that honours ORDER BY, aggregate functions, and LIMIT; callers swap mergers via construction.
Implementations should be stateless and thread-safe: a single merger instance is shared across concurrent reads.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.IResultMerger.MergeRows(TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Routing.RoutingDecision,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Object}})
member.summary: Merges the row-enumerable results produced by each shard in order of .
member.param: Routing decision that produced the shard fan-out.
Per-shard enumerables. Missing shards (best-effort drops) are represented by null entries.
member.returns: Combined row sequence; never null.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.IResultMerger.MergePaged(TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Routing.RoutingDecision,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{TheTechIdea.Beep.DataBase.PagedResult},System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Merges the paged results returned by each shard. Total record counts are summed; the caller-supplied page metadata from is preserved on the merged response.
member.param: Routing decision that produced the shard fan-out.
Per-shard values; null when the shard dropped out.
Page number originally requested by the caller.
Page size originally requested by the caller.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.IResultMerger.MergeScalar(TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Routing.RoutingDecision,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Double})
member.summary: Merges scalar values returned by GetScalar / GetScalarAsync. The default contract is "sum across shards" because those methods are overwhelmingly used for COUNT / SUM style queries. Query-aware mergers override when the originating SQL is an aggregate the basic merger cannot decompose (AVG, MIN, etc.).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.QuorumPolicy
member.summary: How the Phase 07 write executor decides whether a multi-shard write succeeded. Applied to and writes; single-shard sharded writes always require All (the one shard must ack).

remarks.para: The effective quorum numbers are derived from the in the active plan, but callers may override per-call via .

Quorum checks the count of shards that succeeded; partial failures are reported via
so operators can reconcile the divergent replicas.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.QuorumPolicy.All
member.summary: Every target shard must ack the write. The safest / default policy when the plan does not explicitly set a quorum.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.QuorumPolicy.Majority
member.summary: A simple majority (> N/2) of target shards must ack. Suitable for odd replication factors (3, 5, 7).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.QuorumPolicy.AtLeastN
member.summary: At least target shards must ack. Defaults to when not overridden. Use when the placement explicitly encodes the required ack count (e.g. 2 of 3).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.ReplicatedReadPolicy
member.summary: How the Phase 06 read executor picks a single shard for a read when the placement resolves to multiple live shards.
member.remarks: Load-aware / latency-aware variants are reserved for later phases; Phase 06 ships the two deterministic options below. Chosen via .

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.ReplicatedReadPolicy.First
member.summary: Always pick the first live shard in the placement order. Deterministic — easiest to trace. Default.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.ReplicatedReadPolicy.Random
member.summary: Pick a live shard uniformly at random on every call. Distributes read load across replicas at the cost of trace determinism.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.ScatterFailurePolicy
member.summary: How the Phase 06 read executor treats per-shard failures while it is fanning a single read out across multiple shards.

remarks.para: Chosen via . The policy only affects scatter reads (reads whose or is true); single-shard reads always propagate the originating exception.
Writes use the separate / Phase 07 quorum policy and do not consult this enum.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.ScatterFailurePolicy.BestEffort
member.summary: Return whatever the healthy shards produced. Failed shards are logged via but do not propagate. Default — safest for read-heavy workloads.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.ScatterFailurePolicy.FailFast
member.summary: The first failure cancels the remaining shard calls and rethrows the originating exception. Use when a partial result is worse than no result.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.ScatterFailurePolicy.RequireAll
member.summary: Every shard must succeed. A single failure aggregates every error into an and throws. Use for reconciliation / audit reads.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.WriteFanOutResult
member.summary: Per-shard outcome of a single write leg inside a fan-out. One is produced for every target shard, even when the whole fan-out ultimately succeeds, so audit / telemetry can show which replicas were contacted and how they responded.
member.remarks: Values are immutable. is derived from being null; the factories below keep construction symmetric and explicit.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.WriteFanOutResult.ShardId
member.summary: Target shard id.

member.summary: null on success; the thrown exception otherwise.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.WriteFanOutResult.Duration
member.summary: Wall-clock duration of the per-shard call.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.WriteFanOutResult.Succeeded
member.summary: true when is null.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.WriteFanOutResult.Success(System.String,System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Records a successful per-shard write.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.WriteFanOutResult.Failure(System.String,System.Exception,System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Records a failed per-shard write.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.WriteOutcome
member.summary: Aggregate outcome of a distributed write: the per-shard s, whether the quorum was satisfied, and the first error observed (for quick IErrorsInfo conversion).

remarks.para: The write executor always produces a — it never throws on per-shard failure. The caller inspects and decides whether to treat the write as success, partial success, or failure.

distinguishes "everything succeeded" from "quorum met but some replicas failed" so the datasource can raise
for the latter case.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.WriteOutcome.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.WriteFanOutResult},System.Int32,TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Execution.QuorumPolicy)

member.summary: Entity the write targeted.

member.summary: true when is >= .
