member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.IDistributedDataSource
member.summary:
Distributed-tier datasource contract that exposes a single surface over a set of physical shards. Each shard is itself an HA pool implemented by an existing , so per-shard failover, circuit breaking, and load balancing are reused from the Proxy/ tier rather than reinvented.
Phase 01 only defines the contract and the partial-class skeleton; routing, execution, transactions, and observability are filled in by later phases (see DistributedDatasource/DistributedPlans/MASTER-TODO-TRACKER.md).
member.remarks: Implementations MUST remain additive against consumers — callers that only need an never need to cast to .

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.IDistributedDataSource.DistributionPlan
member.summary: Read-only snapshot of the distribution plan currently in effect. Replace with a new plan via .

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.IDistributedDataSource.Shards
M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.IDistributedDataSource.ApplyDistributionPlan(TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributionPlan)
member.summary: Atomically replaces the active distribution plan. Phase 02 validates the plan against the current shard map; Phase 11 extends this method with reshard-on-apply behaviour. Phase 01 only validates non-null and stores the reference.
member.param: New plan to install. Must not be null.

member.name: E:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.IDistributedDataSource.OnShardSelected
member.summary: Raised after the router resolves an entity / key pair to a shard. Phase 05+.

member.name: E:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.IDistributedDataSource.OnPlacementViolation
member.summary: Raised when a routing or plan-application step finds a placement violation. Phase 02+.

member.name: E:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.IDistributedDataSource.OnReshardStarted
member.summary: Raised when a reshard operation begins. Phase 11+.

member.name: E:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.IDistributedDataSource.OnReshardCompleted
member.summary: Raised when a reshard operation completes (successfully or otherwise). Phase 11+.

member.name: E:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.IDistributedDataSource.OnReshardProgress
member.summary: Raised as a reshard copy loop makes progress. Phase 11+.

member.name: E:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.IDistributedDataSource.OnPartialReplicationFailure
member.summary: Raised when a replicated or broadcast write satisfied its quorum but at least one target shard still failed. Phase 07+. Full quorum failures surface via the normal return path and do not raise this event.

member.name: E:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.IDistributedDataSource.OnTransactionInDoubt
member.summary: Raised when a two-phase commit scope reaches : every shard voted PrepareOk but at least one shard failed the commit round. Operators (or the Phase 13 reconciler) must decide how to resolve the orphaned state. Phase 09+.

member.name: E:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.IDistributedDataSource.OnShardDown
member.summary: Raised when the classifies a shard as unhealthy for the first time since the last recovery. Phase 10+.

member.name: E:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.IDistributedDataSource.OnShardRestored
member.summary: Raised when a previously unhealthy shard recovers. Phase 10+.

member.name: E:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.IDistributedDataSource.OnDegradedMode
member.summary:
Raised when the healthy-shard ratio falls below
and a scatter / broadcast call had to be rejected or degraded. Phase 10+.

member.name: E:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.IDistributedDataSource.OnPartialBroadcast
member.summary: Raised when a broadcast write excluded one or more unhealthy shards but still satisfied its quorum, so the call succeeded with a missing-replica gap that operators (or a Phase 13 reconciler) must replay. Phase 10+.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Observability.DistributedActivitySource
member.summary: Shared for the distribution tier. Callers that want OpenTelemetry traces register a listener for ; the distribution datasource starts a root activity around every router-scoped operation with the tags listed below.

remarks.para: Standard tags written on each activity: beep.shard.ids, beep.entity, beep.mode, beep.match.kind, beep.partition.key, beep.correlation.id, beep.operation.
No listener ⇒ zero overhead: skips the allocation entirely when nothing is subscribed.

member.summary: Thread-safe implementation of . Records each request via and, on , merges distribution-tier counters with the shard clusters' output.

remarks.para: Cardinality guard: bounds the per-entity map with a simple LRU eviction so a runaway workload cannot blow up memory. The same bound is applied to the per-shard map via .
Latency rolling windows are delegated to ; the detector's event is forwarded as .

member.summary: Detection window size in seconds (default 10).

Optional custom hot-shard detector; a default-configured instance is created when null.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Observability.DistributionMetricsSnapshot
member.summary: Immutable point-in-time snapshot aggregated by . Captures distribution-tier counters plus a merged view of every shard cluster's .

remarks.para: Snapshots are produced on demand via ; each call returns a fresh instance so callers can store it for diffing, export to OpenTelemetry, or rendering in a dashboard without risk of mutation from concurrent writes.
Per-entity and per-shard maps are capped by an LRU (MaxObservedEntities / MaxObservedShards) so metric cardinality stays bounded in multi-tenant workloads.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Observability.HotShardDetector
member.summary: Rolling-window p95 latency detector used by . A shard whose p95 latency stays above for successive windows is flagged as "hot" and reported via .

remarks.para: The detector keeps a bounded ring of recent latencies per shard ( samples). When the window is full it computes an approximate p95 by sorting a copy of the buffer; for realistic per-shard cardinalities (≤ few dozen shards and ≤ 2048 samples) the cost is a few hundred microseconds per sample and safely runs on the hot path.
The detector never throws: a faulty event handler cannot break the recording path.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Observability.HotShardDetector.WindowSize
member.summary: Samples kept per shard (default 256).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Observability.HotShardDetector.P95ThresholdMs
member.summary: p95 threshold (ms) that triggers breach counting.

member.summary: Consecutive-window breaches required before firing.

member.summary: Raised when a shard breaches the threshold.

member.summary: Creates a new detector with explicit tuning.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Observability.IDistributedMetricsAggregator
member.summary: Aggregates distribution-tier counters, latency samples, and shard-cluster metrics into a single . Instances are thread-safe and designed to live for the full lifetime of a .
member.remarks: Implementations are free to retain rolling windows for latency percentiles and hot-shard detection; concrete keeps a bounded token-bucket-style window to keep memory flat even under sustained load.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Observability.IDistributedMetricsAggregator.Record(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Double,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Records a completed request. Called by the read/write/DDL executors on the hot path; implementations MUST be allocation-cheap and MUST NOT throw.
member.param: Distribution mode () encoded as a string so callers can pass the enum's ToString() without referencing its numeric value.
Wall-clock latency observed at the distribution tier (includes routing, wait, and shard round-trip).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Observability.IDistributedMetricsAggregator.Snapshot

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Partitioning.CompositePartitionFunction
member.summary: Combines two or more inner instances. Each inner function resolves the input independently; the composite returns the deduplicated union of every inner result. Useful for tenant + key sharding where a row should land on every shard chosen by either function.

remarks.para: Output ordering is intentionally undefined; documented as "union, deduplicated, no order guarantee" so callers must not depend on the order in which shard ids are returned.
The composite's is the deduplicated union of the inner functions' columns, in the order they were first observed.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Partitioning.HashPartitionFunction
member.summary: Consistent-hash partition function. Builds a virtual-slot ring (default 150 slots per shard) at construction and resolves each row to the shard owning the first slot ≥ hash(key). Uses the shared so its distribution matches the proxy-tier ConsistentHashRouter bit-for-bit.

remarks.para: The ring is immutable once built; rebuild a new function when the placement shard list changes (typically driven by Phase 11 resharding).
Adding one shard to an N-shard ring re-routes only ~1/(N+1) of the keys — the property that makes this implementation suitable for online resharding.

member.param: Columns whose values combine to form the hash input. At least one is required.
Shards that participate in the ring. Must contain at least one entry.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Partitioning.IPartitionFunction
member.summary: Contract for a row-level partition function. Implementations take a (entity name + key values) and return the shard ids a row should be placed on.

remarks.para: All implementations MUST be pure: no I/O, no state mutation, no time- or thread-dependent behaviour. The same input must always produce the same output for a given function instance. This is what makes the resharding logic in Phase 11 cheap — only inputs whose hash slot moves need to be rewritten.
Most functions return exactly one shard id. The contract uses to allow to return the union of multiple inner-function outputs without boxing.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Partitioning.ListPartitionFunction
member.summary: Discrete-value partition function. Maps each enumerated key value to an explicit shard id (e.g. Region = "EU" → "shard-eu"). Falls back to for values not in the map; returns an empty list when no default is set.

remarks.para: Lookups use so a stored string "42" can match an int 42 coming from a row. The original mapping is preserved verbatim for diagnostics via .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Partitioning.MurmurHash3Helper
member.summary: Bit-for-bit port of the 32-bit x86 MurmurHash3 implementation originally embedded in Proxy/ProxyCluster.NodeRouting.cs's ConsistentHashRouter. Extracted into a shared helper so the Phase 04 partition functions and the existing proxy-tier router both call the same algorithm — required for the "ConsistentHash tests still pass after refactor" verification criterion.

remarks.para: MurmurHash3 is a non-cryptographic hash; do NOT use it for security-sensitive scenarios. It is chosen here for its strong distribution properties and identical behaviour across .NET runtimes and target frameworks.
Constants and finalisation steps mirror the original implementation exactly:

list.item: UTF-8 encoding for string inputs, little-endian block reads.

member.returns: 32-bit unsigned hash code.

member.summary: Computes the 32-bit MurmurHash3 of .
member.param: Input buffer. null is treated as empty.
member.returns: 32-bit unsigned hash code.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Partitioning.PartitionKeyCoercer
member.summary: Best-effort typed coercion / comparison helper used by the partition functions. Allows a string "42" stored in a list-partition map to match an int 42 coming from a row, and vice versa for range boundaries.

remarks.para: Coercion is intentionally conservative: failure returns the original value rather than throwing, so a partition function can fall back to or on the stringified form. This keeps the row-routing path resilient to schema drift.
All comparisons use the invariant culture so partition results are deterministic regardless of the calling thread's locale.

member.summary: Returns a deterministic string representation of suitable for hashing. null is rendered as empty so it hashes consistently.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Partitioning.RangePartitionFunction
member.summary: Range-based partition function. Routes a row to the shard whose half-open boundary [lo, hi) contains the key value. Boundaries are sorted at construction; resolution uses binary search.

remarks.para: The boundary list MUST be supplied in ascending order of . The constructor validates the ordering and contiguousness so callers cannot accidentally introduce gaps.
A boundary with MaxExclusive = null represents the open-ended upper segment (matches every key >= the previous boundary's max). Exactly one such boundary is allowed and it must be the last entry.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Partitioning.RangePartitionFunction.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Partitioning.RangePartitionBoundary})
member.summary: Initialises a new range function.
member.param: Key columns whose values are compared against the boundaries.
Sorted, contiguous boundaries. The last entry may have MaxExclusive = null for the open-ended segment.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.AdaptiveTimeoutCalculator
member.summary: Produces a per-call deadline from a shard's observed p95 latency so a slow shard gets its timeout shortened (fail fast) while a healthy shard keeps its full budget.

remarks.para: Formula: deadline = clamp(factor * p95, minDeadline, maxDeadline) where factor / min / max come from . When no metrics are available the calculator returns the supplied fallback so callers keep their pre-Phase-14 behaviour.
The calculator is intentionally stateless apart from the metrics aggregator reference; it re-reads p95 on every call so plan swaps / aggregator replacements take effect immediately.

member.param: Delegate that returns the live aggregator (never cached so hot-swaps take effect).
Performance options; must not be null.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.BackpressureException
member.summary: Thrown when a Phase 14 capacity control (concurrency gate, rate limiter, or shard-permit) rejects a caller instead of letting the request queue unboundedly. Carries a hint so callers can back off without guessing a delay.

remarks.para: Backpressure is explicit rather than silent: the distribution tier would rather fast-fail with a structured exception than let a slow shard pull the whole datasource down. Higher-level frameworks (queues, schedulers) can catch this and re-submit after .
The exception is serializable so it survives crossing AppDomain or process boundaries when routed through Beep's proxy / remoting layers.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.DistributedConcurrencyGate
member.summary: Semaphore-backed concurrency cap that throws when a permit cannot be acquired within the caller's wait budget. Holds both a global permit (all distributed calls) and per-shard permits so a single slow shard does not drain the datasource-wide budget.

remarks.para: Acquired permits implement ; the caller must dispose the permit in a finally block to avoid permanently leaking capacity. Permits are safe to dispose from any thread.
The gate is allocation-light on the hot path — the only allocations are the permit struct boxed as and the per-shard lazy-created once per shard id.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.HotShardMitigator
member.summary: Maintains the set of shards currently flagged "hot" by the Phase 13 and answers the executor's question "may I still dispatch this read to shard X?". Hot shards are read-shed only for replicated / broadcast placements; single-shard (sharded) reads are never shed so no partitioned data gets orphaned.

remarks.para:
The mitigator subscribes to
and maintains a cooldown per shard so a single flap does not yank the shard in and out. When no aggregator is attached the mitigator is a no-op ( returns false).
Callers use / to bounce the mitigator without re-subscribing to events, and to wipe the hot-shard set after a plan swap.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.IDistributedRateLimiter
member.summary: Per-shard rate limiter consulted by the read / write executors before dispatching a call. Implementations cap throughput and surface when the caller cannot be admitted within the supplied wait budget.

remarks.para: The limiter layers on top of 's own capacity controls. When both are configured the distributed limiter runs first so hot-path callers see a single, coherent instead of two stacked guards.
Implementations must be safe to call from many threads simultaneously. State (e.g. token counters) is keyed by so a misbehaving shard cannot starve healthy neighbours.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.IDistributedRateLimiter.TryAcquire(System.String)
member.summary: Attempts to acquire one permit for without waiting. Returns true when the permit was granted; false when the limiter is at capacity.

member.summary: Asynchronously acquires one permit for , throwing when the wait budget expires before a permit becomes available.
member.param: Maximum time to wait for a permit. = try-only.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.IDistributedRateLimiter.Configure(System.Double,System.Int32)
member.summary: Replaces the per-shard configuration at runtime. Implementations should avoid re-allocating existing buckets so in-flight callers are unaffected.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.PerformanceOptions
member.summary: Per-instance tuning record that controls Phase 14 capacity engineering: cross-shard parallelism caps, per-shard rate limits, adaptive timeouts, and hot-shard read-shedding.

remarks.para: All caps default to safe, generous values so existing Phase 06 / 07 / 13 deployments keep running unchanged; operators opt in to stricter limits as they collect production telemetry.
Layering with : the distributed gate runs before any cluster-tier semaphore. Both must be satisfied for a call to proceed.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.PerformanceOptions.MaxConcurrentDistributedCalls
member.summary: Maximum simultaneous distribution-tier calls in flight across every entity and every caller. Acts as a global circuit breaker against runaway parallelism. Defaults to 256; set to 0 to disable the gate entirely.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.PerformanceOptions.DistributedPermitWait
member.summary: Maximum time a caller will wait to acquire the global distributed permit before is thrown. Defaults to 2 seconds.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.PerformanceOptions.MaxConcurrentCallsPerShard
member.summary: Maximum simultaneous calls routed to a single shard from the distribution tier. Defaults to 32; set to 0 to disable the per-shard gate.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.PerformanceOptions.ShardPermitWait
member.summary: Maximum time a caller will wait to acquire a per-shard permit. Defaults to 1 second.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.PerformanceOptions.ShardRateLimitPerSecond
member.summary: Token-bucket steady-state rate per shard (calls/sec). Defaults to 0 = rate-limiting disabled.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.PerformanceOptions.ShardRateLimitBurst
member.summary: Token-bucket burst capacity per shard. Defaults to 32; ignored when is 0.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.PerformanceOptions.AdaptiveDeadlineFactor
member.summary: Multiplier applied to a shard's observed p95 latency to produce an adaptive deadline (DeadlineFactor * p95). Defaults to 4.0.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.PerformanceOptions.MaxAdaptiveDeadlineMs
member.summary: Hard ceiling for adaptive deadlines, in milliseconds. Defaults to 60_000 (1 minute).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.PerformanceOptions.MinAdaptiveDeadlineMs
member.summary: Minimum adaptive deadline in milliseconds; applied even when p95 is zero or negligible. Defaults to 250 ms.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.PerformanceOptions.EnableHotShardReadShedding
member.summary: When true (default), reads against entities with replicated / broadcast placements skip shards flagged hot by the Phase 13 detector; single-shard reads are never shed (to avoid orphaning partitioned data).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.PerformanceOptions.EnableCapacityGates
member.summary:
When true (default), executors call
and the per-shard acquire before dispatching. Set to false for tests that want to observe pre-Phase-14 behaviour.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.PerformanceOptions.Clone
member.summary: Returns a deep copy so runtime tweaks by one caller do not leak into other datasources.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Performance.TokenBucketRateLimiter
member.summary: Per-shard token-bucket implementation of . Each shard gets its own bucket so a bursty shard cannot drain tokens from neighbours.

remarks.para: Buckets refill continuously at tokens/sec up to . The bucket state is maintained via a simple spin-lock on each bucket so the hot path stays allocation-free.
Pass ratePerSecond <= 0 to effectively disable the limiter — every TryAcquire succeeds and AcquireAsync returns immediately.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Placement.EntityPlacementMap
member.summary: Runtime, fast-lookup view of a . Splits placements into exact and prefix buckets so resolution is O(1) for exact matches and O(prefix-count) for the longest-prefix scan. Inspired by the Proxy/EntityAffinityMap resolver.

remarks.para: Prefix patterns are encoded by an entity name ending in * (case-insensitive). For example, an with EntityName = "Audit_*" matches every entity whose name starts with "Audit_"; the stored prefix key is "Audit_".
The map is immutable once returns; the dictionaries are concurrent only to allow safe enumeration from multiple readers without re-allocating a snapshot per resolve.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Placement.EntityPlacementResolver
member.summary: Combines an , a live-shard supplier, and an into a single call that returns a ready for the executors.

remarks.para: The resolver always queries the live-shard supplier when expanding placements (and when applying the fallback) so that shards added after the plan was built are included automatically — see Phase 03 risk note.

Shards listed by a but missing from the live snapshot are filtered out. When that filtering removes every shard the resolver still returns a non-null resolution but with an empty — callers must check or the TargetShardIds.Count == 0 case before dispatching.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.DistributionPlan
member.summary: Versioned, immutable placement spec consumed by . Maps each entity name to a single ; mutations always produce a new instance with incremented by 1.

remarks.para: The plan does NOT validate that referenced shards exist in the catalog — that responsibility lives with which raises for unknown shard ids.
Equality is value-style: two plans with the same name, version, and placement set compare equal. Phase 11 resharding diffs plans by enumerating placement deltas.

member.summary: Folds per-shard partial aggregate values into a running total for one . Used by the Phase 08 while walking grouped rows from every shard.

remarks.para: The accumulator is deliberately untyped — partial values may arrive as int, long, decimal, double, , or . SUM / COUNT are folded in when the inputs are integral/decimal and in otherwise. MIN / MAX use -style comparisons via .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.BroadcastJoinRewriter
member.summary: Detects the narrow Phase 08 "broadcast-side join" case: a query that joins a sharded entity with a broadcast entity can run entirely inside each shard because every shard holds a complete copy of the broadcast table. The rewriter flags those intents so the planner can leave them alone instead of rejecting them as an unsupported cross-shard join.

remarks.para:
The rewriter is intentionally conservative in v1:
It inspects only one "other" entity name supplied by the caller (the typical hand-authored join has a single driving table).
It rewrites nothing inside the intent text / filters; the expectation is that each shard's data source already executes the join locally against its broadcast replica.
Full distributed joins between two sharded entities are out of scope; a future phase will add a broadcast-hash rewriter.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.BroadcastJoinRewriter.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Placement.EntityPlacementResolver)
member.summary: Creates a rewriter that queries to classify sibling entities as broadcast or sharded.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.BroadcastJoinRewriter.CanRewrite(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns true when a join between and can run locally on each shard because at least one side is broadcast.
member.returns: true when a rewrite is safe — the caller should fan out the join to each shard without any cross-shard coordination. false when both sides are sharded (v1 cannot plan that case) or both are broadcast (trivial, pick either shard).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.BroadcastJoinRewriter.Rewrite(TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.QueryIntent,System.String)
member.summary: Inspects against the hint and returns a describing whether the caller can continue without a distributed-join rewrite.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.IQueryAwareResultMerger
member.summary: Extends the Phase 06 with the entry point that honours a produced by the Phase 08 planner (top-N, sort-merge, group-aggregate, …).

remarks.para: remains the contract the Phase 06 executor talks to. The query-aware merger implements both interfaces so callers can register a single implementation and let upgrade to the richer entry point when a plan is present.
Implementations MUST cope with missing shard outputs (null entries in ) and produce a deterministic result even when the routing decision's shard order differs from the enumerable order.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.IQueryAwareResultMerger.MergePlan(TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.QueryPlan,TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Routing.RoutingDecision,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Object}})
member.summary: Merges per-shard row outputs according to .
member.param: Routing decision that produced the fan-out (always equal to ; passed for convenience).
Per-shard row enumerables; null entries represent dropped / failed shards.
member.returns: Merged row enumerable; never null. Rows are shaped as (with keys) when the plan produces a group-aggregate, otherwise shards' native row objects are passed through in the requested order.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.IQueryPlanner
member.summary: Converts a high-level + a Phase 05 into a (per-shard sub-intents + merge spec).

remarks.para: The default implementation is . Callers may swap in a richer planner via without touching the read path; the executor only needs a .
The planner MUST leave the incoming untouched — routing is owned by Phase 05 and should only be produced by the router. This keeps routing policy (hooks, unmapped behaviour) in one place.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.MergeOperation
member.summary: Shape of the cross-shard merge the Phase 08 planner attached to a . The value decides which code path in runs against the per-shard results.
member.remarks: The planner picks exactly one operation per plan. Complex intents (e.g. GROUP BY + ORDER BY + TOP) are expressed as a merge that keeps sort/top information on the and applies them after the group merge finishes.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.MergeOperation.Union
member.summary: Concatenate per-shard rows in the order shards returned them. No sort, no dedupe. Matches the v1 Phase 06 behaviour for filter-only reads.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.MergeOperation.TopN
member.summary: K-way merge the per-shard outputs by and stop after rows. Each shard is expected to return at most TopN pre-sorted rows.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.MergeOperation.SortMerge
member.summary: K-way merge the per-shard outputs by with no row limit. Used for ORDER BY without TOP / LIMIT.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.MergeOperation.GroupAggregate
member.summary: Group per-shard partial aggregates by and fold their across shards. AVG is rewritten upstream to a SUM/COUNT pair and divided after merging to avoid an "average of averages" error.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.MergeSpec
member.summary: Declarative description of the cross-shard merge step the runs after every shard has returned its partial result. Attached to .

remarks.para: A is produced by the Phase 08 planner alongside the per-shard sub-intents. Keeping the merge description separate from the planner lets the runtime swap out the planner while reusing the same merge engine (useful when a future phase layers a smarter planner on top of the basic one).
drives the behaviour; all other properties are optional and consumed only by the operations that need them ( only for , / only for , etc.).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.PartialAggregate
member.summary: Describes one partial aggregate the merger expects on every shard's result row. Lives next to because the planner produces these as a byproduct of AVG rewriting.

remarks.para: For plain aggregates (, , , ) the merger folds values under and emits the result under .

For the planner emits two entries with = original alias: one for the SUM side ( == ) and one for the COUNT side ( == ). The merger detects the pair via and divides after folding to produce the final average.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.QueryAwareResultMerger
member.summary: Phase 08 implementation. Dispatches on to Union, Top-N k-way merge, SortMerge, or GroupAggregate paths. Falls back to behaviour when asked through the plain surface (no plan present).

remarks.para: The merger is stateless; one instance can serve all concurrent reads on the distributed data source. Worker helpers live in partial class files (QueryAwareResultMerger.Sorting.cs, QueryAwareResultMerger.Grouping.cs) so each file does one job.
When a shard's enumerable is null the merger logs nothing — the Phase 06 executor is responsible for partial failure reporting via / shard-level events. The merger merely skips the entry.

member.summary: Partial class carrying the path. Responsible for combining per-shard partial aggregate rows into a single globally-aggregated row per group, then re-pairing AVG sum/count components and applying any outstanding ORDER BY / TOP from the merge spec.

Partial class carrying the Top-N and SortMerge paths. The implementation is a simple list-sort + offset/limit; a full heap-based k-way merge can replace it transparently once shard results are streamed lazily.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.QueryIntent
member.summary: Structured description of a read request the Phase 08 planner can reason about: entity name, pushdown filters, column list, group/aggregate, order-by, and top/offset. Kept intentionally small — full SQL parsing remains out of scope for v1.

remarks.para: Callers typically build a either by hand (for programmatic read APIs) or via the lightweight (Phase 08) that scans an incoming RunQuery string for ORDER BY / TOP keywords. The intent is immutable once constructed — modifying it after planning would defeat the plan cache.
An intent with = empty and = empty and no / is treated by the planner as a plain filter read (matches ).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.QueryPlan
member.summary: Immutable plan produced by the Phase 08 query planner: the per-shard sub-intent each shard should execute, the cross-shard , and the that pinned down the target shards.

remarks.para: The plan is a read-only snapshot — running the same logical read twice yields two fresh plans because the shard set and placement may have changed. Callers MUST NOT mutate the returned dictionary; the planner builds a defensive copy.
When has exactly one id, is true and the merge spec is always . The executor short-circuits single-shard plans to a straight pass-through call with no merge overhead.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.QueryPlanner
member.summary: Default Phase 08 . Pushes filters, columns, order-by, and top-N down to every shard, splits into a SUM/COUNT pair so the merger can compute total_sum / total_count, and produces a single describing how the per-shard outputs should be combined.

remarks.para:
Planning rules:
Single-shard decision → trivial plan with a pass-through merge and the unchanged intent.
Simple filter/projection intent → push the full intent to every shard and merge with .
Intent with order-by / top-N but no aggregates → push order-by + (offset + top) cap per shard and merge with / .
Intent with aggregates → push group-by + partial aggregates (with AVG split) to every shard and merge with .
The planner is deliberately stateless — no caching in v1 — so plan construction stays cheap and deterministic.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Query.RowValueExtractor
member.summary: Reads named column values out of the loosely-typed row objects returned by shard data sources. Supports dictionary rows, , and plain POCOs (via cached reflection).
member.remarks: The merger deals with heterogeneous shard payloads — some shards might return strongly-typed entity classes, others might return DataTables, and a few might return plain dictionaries. Wrapping field access through this helper keeps the merge path agnostic and avoids littering the grouping code with as chains.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resharding.DualWriteCoordinator
member.summary: Default in-memory . Stores one window per entity in a concurrent dictionary so the router's read/write hot path pays only a dictionary lookup when no migration is active.
member.remarks: Persistence is out of scope for v1 — if the process restarts mid-reshard the coordinator starts empty and the is responsible for replaying the last checkpoint. Phase 13 extends this with a persisted window store backed by ConfigEditor.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resharding.DualWriteState
member.summary: Phase 11 dual-write window lifecycle states. Drives and the router's write-path consultation of the so reads stay consistent while an entity is being moved from one placement to another.

remarks.para: Lifecycle: → → → → . Every transition is one-way and logged so operators can audit a reshard end-to-end.
Semantics per state:

list.item: : no migration in flight; router uses the active plan only.
: target placement has been announced; reads/writes still flow only to the source.
: writes fan out to both source and target placements while the copy runs.
: reads are being flipped to the target; writes still dual-hit during this short window.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resharding.DualWriteState.Off
member.summary: No migration in flight.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resharding.DualWriteState.Shadow
member.summary: Target placement announced; no write fan-out yet.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resharding.DualWriteState.DualWrite
member.summary: Writes fan out to both source and target placements.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resharding.DualWriteState.Cutover
member.summary: Reads are flipping to the target placement; writes still dual-hit.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resharding.DualWriteWindow
member.summary: Per-entity dual-write window tracking a running Phase 11 reshard operation. Stores the entity name, current , and both placements (source + target) so the router can fan writes out to both sets while a move/repartition is in progress.

remarks.para: Windows are created by and registered with an . The router consults the coordinator on every write to augment the decision's shard list whenever a window is in or .
State transitions are enforced here: illegal transitions throw rather than silently corrupting the migration. and are thread-safe via a per-window lock.

member.summary: Upper bound on rows copied per second. 0 disables throttling. Default: 0 — operators explicitly opt in so unit tests and dev boxes run at full speed.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resharding.EntityCopyOptions.CheckpointInterval
member.summary: Minimum delay between checkpoint persistences. The copy loop debounces checkpoint writes by this interval so a fast source shard does not flood the checkpoint store. Default: 1 second.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resharding.EntityCopyOptions.PageRetryCount
member.summary: Maximum retry attempts for a single failed page before the copy loop surfaces the error to the caller. Default: 3.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resharding.EntityCopyService
member.summary:
Default — pages rows out of the source cluster with
and inserts each row into the target via . Persists checkpoints between pages so a restart can resume mid-reshard.

remarks.para: Idempotency: the copy loop relies on the target shard's conflict-handling behaviour (upsert / unique-constraint tolerance). The service records the last copied page number in the checkpoint; on resume it reads the checkpoint, advances pageNumber past the recorded value, and continues.

Throttling: when is positive, the loop sleeps between batches to stay under the target rate. The calculation is intentionally simple — a linear sleep based on the page size — which is accurate enough for operational throttling while keeping the implementation free of timers.

Checkpoint store; an is used when null.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resharding.IDualWriteCoordinator
member.summary: Thread-safe registry of active instances keyed by entity name. Consulted by the router on every write so an in-flight migration can transparently fan writes out to both source and target placements during and .

remarks.para: Implementations MUST be safe for concurrent readers (router hot path) and a single writer (the reshard orchestrator). Windows are reference-stable: a window returned by can be observed to change state while the caller holds the reference, but will not be swapped out from under the caller.

Only one active window per entity is supported in v1 — attempts to register a second window throw. Callers that need to chain migrations must complete (or cancel) the first window before starting the next.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resharding.IEntityCopyService
member.summary: Bulk-copies rows from one shard to another in paged batches with durable semantics. Consumed by during to bring the target placement up to parity before cutover.

remarks.para: The service is decoupled from the concrete datasource — it consumes the already-resolved endpoints so unit tests can supply stubs. All calls are cancellable; a cancelled run leaves the latest checkpoint in place so a resume call can pick up where it left off.

Row shape is whatever the source shard returns from
— typically IDictionary or a POCO. The service hands each row verbatim to the target's ; callers wanting to transform rows (e.g. decrypt, mask) should compose a decorator around the source cluster.

Target shard id (used for checkpoint identity). Required.
Optional filter applied to the source read; null copies every row.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resharding.IReshardingService
member.summary: Phase 11 resharding orchestrator. Exposes the four topology primitives — add shard, remove shard, move entity, repartition entity — plus a plan-apply helper that translates a into concrete migration calls.

remarks.para: Every primitive follows the same Shadow → DualWrite → Cutover → Off window lifecycle:

item.description: Register a in .
Transition to and copy rows via .
Transition to , swap the plan, and unregister the window.
Close the window (Off) and drop any source-side state when applicable.

remarks.para: Every method is cancellable. On cancel the service forces the active window to , leaves the source plan untouched, and returns a with set to true.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resharding.PlanDiff
member.summary: Plan-to-plan diff calculator. Produces the minimal ordered list of steps required to migrate into .

remarks.para: Step ordering: removes run first (to free capacity), then adds, then repartition/move steps. The actual apply order is the 's responsibility; the diff merely classifies and presents the work.

Equality is Phase-02 value-style: two placements compare equal when their mode, shard list, partition function, RF, and quorum match exactly. A entry is never added for an unchanged placement; the result list only contains actionable work.

member.summary: Drain plan supplied to . Specifies which shard is being removed and which shards inherit the entities it owned.

remarks.para: Two modes of operation:

item.description: populated — every entity declared on must have an explicit target shard mapping. Entities without a mapping cause the remove to abort before any data is touched.
empty and populated — each entity is redistributed across the fallback shards (used for hash-partitioned removals).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resharding.ReshardingService
member.summary: partial — AddShardAsync. Adding a shard is a no-copy operation: the new shard is registered in the catalog but no placements point at it until a later or repartition call brings it into service.
Default — orchestrates every topology change (add/remove shard, move/repartition entity, apply a new plan) through the Shadow → DualWrite → Cutover window protocol.

remarks.para: The service is decoupled from through a small set of delegates supplied at construction time. This keeps the partial class focused on orchestration while making every side effect (plan swap, shard catalog mutation, event raise) testable in isolation.
Implementation is split across partials:

member.summary: partial — MoveEntityAsync. Moves a single entity from one shard to another using the Shadow → DualWrite → Cutover window protocol.

partial — RemoveShardAsync. Drains the specified shard by moving every placement that references it to either an explicit redirect or the plan's fallback shard list, then unregisters the shard from the catalog.

partial — RepartitionEntityAsync. Repartitions a sharded entity using a new partition function and/or a new shard set by dual-writing across every old → new shard pair until the target placement is fully populated.
Initialises a new resharding service.
member.param: Delegate that returns the currently-active plan.
Delegate that atomically replaces the active plan.
Delegate that returns the for a shard id (or null).
Delegate that adds a new shard to the catalog.
Delegate that removes a shard from the catalog.
Delegate used to raise OnReshardStarted (reshardId, reason, affected).
Delegate used to raise OnReshardCompleted (reshardId, reason, affected, error).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.DistributedCircuitBreaker
member.summary: Distribution-tier circuit breaker keyed by shard id. Thin wrapper over (Phase 2 of the Proxy tier) that adds per-shard isolation and an -compatible surface.

remarks.para: The per-node inside is never bypassed — this breaker only adds a second guard rail at the distribution tier so the executors can trip a whole shard off the routing set even when the cluster itself reports healthy (the "returns 200s but semantically wrong" scenario).
Configuration is driven by , , and . A failure threshold of 0 disables the breaker — every call is allowed through and the underlying is never instantiated.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.IShardHealthMonitor
member.summary: Aggregates each shard's health into a distribution-tier view used by the Phase 10 read / write executors, the , and the enforcement pipeline.

remarks.para: Implementations MUST be thread-safe: every accessor may be called concurrently by the executors while the background polling loop (or direct RecordSuccess / RecordFailure calls from the hot path) updates the state.

The monitor never short-circuits a shard on its own. It emits and when the health classification flips, and the executors decide whether / how to route around the shard by consulting and the configured .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardDownPolicy
member.summary: Strategy the distribution tier applies when a routed call lands on a shard that is currently classified as unhealthy (Phase 10). Each can map to a different policy through .

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardDownPolicy.FailFast
member.summary: Throw a (or the mode-appropriate error) immediately. Preferred for routed reads / writes where the data only lives on the target shard and no replica exists.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardDownPolicy.SkipShard
member.summary: Drop the unhealthy shard from the candidate set and continue. Used by replicated reads (pick another replica) and broadcast writes (raise , carry on with the survivors).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardDownPolicy.UseFailover
member.summary: Route the call to the designated failover shard configured on . Typically used for routed reads against a read-through cache or warm replica set. Falls back to when no failover shard is configured or the failover shard is also unhealthy.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardDownPolicy.DegradeScatter
member.summary: Only for sharded scatter reads — execute against the known healthy subset and accept the degraded view. The scatter entry gate still enforces .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardDownPolicyOptions
member.summary: Per-mode configuration plus the distributed circuit-breaker tuning knobs (Phase 10). Owned by ; may be swapped at runtime via .

remarks.para: Defaults are chosen to match the scope in the phase plan:
All values are plain POCO properties so the options instance can be serialized alongside the rest of without custom converters.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardDownPolicyOptions.RoutedPolicy
member.summary: Policy applied when a routed entity lands on an unhealthy shard.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardDownPolicyOptions.ReplicatedPolicy
member.summary: Policy applied when a replicated entity lands on an unhealthy shard.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardDownPolicyOptions.BroadcastPolicy
member.summary: Policy applied when a broadcast write targets an unhealthy shard.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardDownPolicyOptions.ShardedPolicy
member.summary: Policy applied when a sharded scatter hits unhealthy shards.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardDownPolicyOptions.FailoverShardId
member.summary: Shard id used by . Ignored when the current policy is not or when the failover shard itself is unhealthy (the resolver then falls back to ).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardDownPolicyOptions.CircuitFailureThreshold
member.summary: Consecutive failures that trip the distributed circuit breaker for a shard. Defaults to 5; set to 0 to disable the distributed circuit (only the per-cluster breakers remain).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardDownPolicyOptions.CircuitResetTimeout
member.summary: Time a tripped distributed circuit stays open before transitioning to half-open. Defaults to 30 seconds.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardDownPolicyOptions.CircuitSuccessThreshold
member.summary: Consecutive successes required in half-open state before the distributed circuit closes again. Defaults to 2.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardHealthMonitor
member.summary: Default implementation. Uses a lightweight to poll each shard's and aggregates hot-path failure counts reported via .

remarks.para: The monitor is deliberately additive on top of each cluster's internal health machinery (, per-node watchdog, per-node circuit breaker). It never tries to reproduce those checks — it simply observes the cluster's top-level state and trusts the Proxy tier for the finer-grained decisions.

A shard is flipped to unhealthy when its consecutive failure counter reaches . A single successful observation (polling tick or hot-path call) flips it back, mirroring the >= 1 contract of the underlying .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Resilience.ShardHealthSnapshot
member.summary: Immutable point-in-time view of a single shard's health as seen by the distribution tier. Produced by and consumed by the read / write executors plus the resilience event pipeline (Phase 10).

remarks.para: Per-node HA (connection retries, replica fail-over, per-node circuits) belongs to and is never duplicated here. This snapshot describes the cluster as a whole — if the cluster itself is unreachable or every node has tripped its circuit, the snapshot is marked unhealthy so the distributed tier can apply a decision for the affected shard.
All values are captured at the moment the snapshot was built; subscribers must re-fetch via rather than caching the instance.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Routing.IShardRouter
member.summary: Public contract for the Phase 05 shard router. Combines the Phase 03 placement resolver with the Phase 04 partition functions to turn an incoming call into a concrete .

remarks.para: All members are pure with respect to the active distribution plan: a router instance is constructed once per plan version and re-built atomically when the plan changes (Phase 11). Callers who want to override routing without touching the plan should install an .
Implementations MUST raise for routing-specific failures (e.g. scatter writes when scatter is disallowed) and MAY surface upstream for placement failures forwarded from the resolver.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Routing.IShardRoutingHook
member.summary: Pluggable override surface for . The router invokes after producing its baseline ; the hook may return the baseline unchanged, return a modified decision, or return null to indicate "use the baseline".

remarks.para: Hooks MUST be deterministic and non-blocking — they run on the request hot path. Use them to pin debug sessions, force a shard for a specific tenant, or implement custom canary routing. Anything heavier (rate limiting, RBAC) belongs in a dedicated middleware layer.

Returning a decision whose is false is allowed but discouraged: callers cannot tell the override happened. The convention is to construct any override decision with hookOverridden: true so observability tooling can surface the customisation.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Routing.RoutingDecision
member.summary: Concrete instruction produced by : "given this entity and these key values, send the operation to these shards with this quorum and fan-out flag." Phase 06+ executors consume the decision; the router never executes anything itself.

remarks.para: Decisions are immutable so they can be safely captured into closures by the read / write executors. Construction is validated: may be empty only when indicates an explicit failure mode the caller is expected to handle.

records the partition-key values that were actually consulted (or null/empty for scatter reads / unkeyed broadcasts). The dictionary is exposed for observability — Phase 13 audit attaches it to the trace event so a slow read can be correlated with the key it was issued for.

true when this decision was produced for a write call.
true when no partition key was supplied for a sharded read and the executor must fan out across .
true when the operation must touch more than one shard (replicated / broadcast write or sharded write resolved to multiple shards).
Quorum carried over from ; 0 means "all listed shards must ack".
Partition-key columns used for routing; null is normalised to empty.
true when an replaced the baseline decision.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Routing.ShardRouter
member.summary: Phase 05 shard router. Combines the with the partition functions from to turn an entity name + key values into a concrete .

remarks.para: Lifecycle: a router is constructed once per version and replaced atomically whenever the plan changes (Phase 11). The internal partition- function cache is keyed by reference; a new plan produces new placement instances and the cache is naturally discarded.

Liveness handling: hash partition functions are built from the placement's full shard list (so the consistent-hash ring stays stable across transient liveness churn). The router then intersects the function's output with the resolver's live- filtered shard list. If the intersection is empty, is raised with Reason = "NoLiveShard".
Implementation is split across partials:

list.item: ShardRouter.cs — root: ctor, fields, route methods, hook plumbing.

member.summary: partial that augments write-path routing decisions with the additional target shards demanded by active Phase 11 dual-write windows.

remarks.para: Reads are never rerouted by the dual-write coordinator — the reshard source is still canonical for query traffic. Writes are augmented only when a window is in and the routed shard set intersects the window's source shard list; in that case the target shards from the window are union-merged into the decision so every INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE lands on both the old and the new placement.

The coordinator is optional. When null (default), the router behaves exactly like it did before Phase 11 — no additional shards are added. When provided, dual-write fan-out is layered on top of the baseline routing decision without altering any other routing semantics.

member.summary: partial — partition-key extractors for the three call shapes used across the surface: List<AppFilter> (query filters), positional PK arrays (GetEntity(string, object[])), and entity instance writes (InsertEntity/UpdateEntity/DeleteEntity).

remarks.para: All extractors return null when no key column has a usable value — the router treats null as "no key" and either scatters or rejects the call depending on direction and the AllowScatterWrite option.
Reflection accessors are cached per via so per-call cost is a dictionary lookup once the first record of a type has been routed.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Routing.ShardRoutingException
member.summary: Raised when the cannot turn an incoming request into a workable : scatter writes when scatter is disallowed, partition functions resolving to zero live shards, or a hook returning an empty / contradictory override.

remarks.para:
This exception is intentionally distinct from the raised by
for unmapped placements: callers and observability tooling can treat routing failures (key extraction / scatter rejection / hook errors) separately from the upstream placement failures.
The and properties are populated for every throw so structured logging can correlate failures back to a specific entity without regex-parsing the message.

remarks.para: The record is intentionally closed (no subclassing) because providers must pattern-match on . Use the static factory methods (AddColumn, DropColumn, etc.) instead of the raw constructor to guarantee the payload for each kind is fully populated.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Schema.DistributedSchemaService
member.summary: partial — AlterEntity broadcast. Renders each into a minimal ANSI-SQL fragment (so non-RDBMS providers can still pattern-match on the message) and dispatches it to every owning shard via .

remarks.para: V1 uses a deliberately conservative SQL rendering (double- quoted identifiers, plain ANSI ADD COLUMN / DROP COLUMN / ALTER COLUMN / CREATE INDEX / DROP INDEX). Providers that need dialect-specific rendering should override via the options surface in a follow-up — Phase 12 does not block on that.
DDL is not atomic across shards. Callers that require eventual consistency should pair with a follow-up and a manual repair step on any shard listed in .

member.summary: partial — CreateEntity broadcast. Handles mode-specific target resolution and the check for Sharded entities.
Default implementation. Composed of partial files for each operation (Create, Alter, Drop, DriftDetection) so every file owns one concern in the Phase 12 fan-out / drift pipeline.
member.remarks: The service is intentionally decoupled from via delegates so it can be unit-tested against fake shard maps. The Phase 12 DistributedDataSource.Schema.cs partial constructs a default instance that forwards and friends into this surface.
member.summary: partial — drift detection. For every entity in the active plan, samples the per-shard , picks a reference shard, and flags any shard whose structure disagrees with the reference.
member.remarks: The reference is deterministic: the first successfully-sampled shard in the placement's ordered list. When every shard fails sampling the report surfaces those exceptions via and contains no drift entries for the entity.
member.summary: partial — DropEntity broadcast. Renders a conservative DROP TABLE statement and dispatches it to every owning shard. Uses the same helper as CreateEntity / AlterEntity so the audit surface stays consistent.

member.summary: Returns the ordered list of shard ids targeted by a DDL operation for the supplied placement. For Routed the list has exactly one entry; for Sharded / Replicated / Broadcast every listed shard is targeted.

Runs sequentially against every shard in and aggregates successes / failures into a . Sequential execution keeps DDL logs deterministic and avoids overloading central-metadata systems that serialise DDL anyway.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Schema.HiLoSequenceProvider
member.summary: HiLo that allocates ids in blocks from a supplied . Each (, ) pair gets its own lo counter in-process so callers can maintain independent sequences per column when required.
member.remarks: Thread-safe. When the in-process lo counter reaches the provider requests the next block via the allocator delegate. The allocator is responsible for the persistent hi state (e.g. a single-row counter table on a central shard).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Schema.IdentityColumnPolicy
member.summary: Policy applied by when an marked for placement contains a database-generated identity column.
member.remarks: Per-shard identity sequences collide on a distributed insert path because nothing coordinates the generator across shards. Phase 12 therefore surfaces this mismatch explicitly: either log a warning and let the operator accept the risk, or fail loudly and force the caller to switch to a distributed sequence provider (Snowflake / HiLo) or remove the identity column.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Schema.IdentityColumnPolicy.WarnOnly
member.summary: Log a warning via OnPlacementViolation but proceed with the create. Intended for dev / test flows that rely on per-shard identity for local-only tests.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Schema.IdentityColumnPolicy.RejectShardedIdentity
member.summary: Reject the create with a descriptive exception when a Sharded entity carries an identity column. Default for production-grade deployments.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Schema.IDistributedSchemaService
member.summary: Phase 12 orchestrator for DDL operations across a . Coordinates create / alter / drop against every shard that the active says owns the entity, and exposes drift detection so operators can repair heterogeneous schemas.

remarks.para: The service sits on top of the Phase 07 write-executor surface and per-shard DDL methods. Callers typically do not invoke it directly; the Phase 12 DistributedDataSource.Schema.cs partial forwards / / into this surface.
Every public method emits an audit-friendly that carries the per-shard success / error state; drift detection returns a .

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Schema.IDistributedSchemaService.CreateEntityAsync(TheTechIdea.Beep.DataBase.EntityStructure,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Creates the entity on every shard that the active plan assigns to it, honouring the distribution mode:
Routed → create on exactly one shard.
Sharded → create on every member shard.
Replicated / Broadcast → create on every listed shard.
When the entity carries an identity column and the mode is Sharded, the
option decides whether to proceed with a warning or reject.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Schema.IDistributedSequenceProvider
member.summary: Client-side distributed sequence provider consulted by Phase 07 writes when the active placement has an identity column that cannot rely on per-shard auto-increment.

remarks.para: Implementations MUST be thread-safe and collision-free across every node that calls them, typically by combining a unique node id with a monotonic counter (see SnowflakeSequenceProvider) or by reserving a batch of ids via a centralized allocator (HiLoSequenceProvider).
The provider is opt-in; the default options wiring leaves it null, which means Phase 07 writes inherit the shard's native identity behaviour.

member.summary: Terminal outcome of a DDL broadcast operation performed by . Captures the entity, the set of shards targeted, and the per-shard success / error state so callers (and Phase 13 audit) can diagnose partial failures.
member.remarks: DDL cannot be rolled back atomically across heterogeneous shards (see Phase 12 risks). Operations therefore aim for "best-effort with detectable drift": success for every listed shard is reported as = true; per-shard failures populate and surface in the next drift scan.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Schema.SnowflakeSequenceProvider
member.summary:
Snowflake-style . Produces 63-bit ids composed of:
41 bits — milliseconds since .
10 bits — node id (supplied at construction, 0..1023).
12 bits — per-ms sequence counter (0..4095).
member.remarks: Thread-safe. Ids are monotonically increasing within a single process / node id pair. When two threads race for the same millisecond the counter increments; when the counter exhausts the generator spins until the next millisecond boundary.

member.param: Node identifier in the range [0, 1023]. Must be globally unique across all producers.
member.exception: Thrown when is out of range.

member.summary: Simple per-entity ACL policy. For each entity the caller registers the set of principals allowed to perform Read, Write, and DDL operations respectively. A request is allowed when the principal appears in the matching set, the wildcard "*" is present, or the caller opted into a fallback.

remarks.para: The ACL is thread-safe and supports runtime edits via and ; inspection is lock-free against reads. Unknown entities fall back to which defaults to false (deny-by-default) to keep mis-configurations loud during development.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Security.IDistributedAccessPolicy
member.summary: Optional per-entity access policy evaluated by the distribution tier before any executor call. Implementations return true to allow, false to deny; denied calls surface as at the boundary so no shard is ever touched.

remarks.para: Keep implementations allocation-light: the policy is consulted on every read/write/DDL hop. Anything more expensive than a dictionary lookup should be cached by the implementation itself.

is the caller identity when available, otherwise an empty string. Callers may layer a richer authentication context on top by supplying a policy that reads from Thread.CurrentPrincipal or a scoped accessor.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.DistributedTransactionCoordinator
member.summary: Default implementation of . Root partial: ctor, scope registry, shared helpers. Strategy-specific work lives in DistributedTransactionCoordinator.SingleShard.cs, .TwoPhaseCommit.cs, and .Saga.cs.

remarks.para: The coordinator is intentionally decoupled from DistributedDataSource: it receives the shard map via a delegate () and the in-doubt event via an . That way the coordinator can be used in tests with synthetic clusters without a full datasource.

Scopes are tracked in an in-memory registry keyed by correlation id. The registry is only used to reject stale CommitScope/RollbackScope calls (scope already closed) — it is not authoritative for recovery, which is Phase 13's job.

member.summary: partial — saga runner. Executes the caller-supplied list in order against their target shards; on any failure, runs the successful steps' compensations in reverse and finalises the scope as aborted.
member.remarks: V1 compensations run sequentially without retries. Phase 13 introduces retry with exponential back-off and persists the step log so a crashed coordinator can resume compensations.
member.summary: partial — single-shard fast path. When every enlisted entity maps to the same shard the coordinator hands off BeginTransaction / Commit / EndTransaction to that shard's cluster verbatim, so there is zero added round-trip versus calling the cluster directly.

partial — two phase commit across the enlisted shards. The coordinator runs a prepare round (every shard must ack) followed by a commit round. If any prepare fails, an abort round rolls back every prepared shard. If every shard acked prepare but at least one commit fails, the scope is declared and the configured handler is invoked.
member.remarks: V1 uses the existing transaction triple — BeginTransaction is assumed to have been issued per shard by ; Commit stands in for the commit phase and EndTransaction stands in for rollback. Drivers that do not model an explicit prepare still benefit from the fan-out ordering (commit only after every shard's BeginTransaction succeeds), but fail-stop atomicity is best-effort without a durable prepare log (Phase 13).

member.summary: Creates a new coordinator.
member.param: Returns the live shard map. Called on every scope open so the coordinator always sees the current topology — no stale snapshots.
Callback invoked when a 2PC scope enters . Typical binding is DistributedDataSource.RaiseTransactionInDoubt. May be null to silently log only.
Optional log instance. Defaults to a new .
Default policy used when the caller does not override it at time.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.DistributedTransactionStatus
member.summary: Lifecycle state of a tracked by the Phase 09 coordinator.
member.remarks: State transitions are linear: Active → Preparing → Prepared → Committing → Committed (2PC success) or Active → Preparing → Aborting → Aborted (2PC prepare failure). Single-shard uses Active → Committing → Committed. Sagas use Active → Committing → Committed on success and Active → Compensating → Aborted on failure. A state is reached only when a commit round produced mixed acks after the prepare round succeeded.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.DistributedTransactionStatus.Active
member.summary: The scope is open and accepting work.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.DistributedTransactionStatus.Preparing
member.summary: 2PC prepare round is in progress.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.DistributedTransactionStatus.Prepared
member.summary: 2PC prepare round completed successfully.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.DistributedTransactionStatus.Committing
member.summary: Single-shard or 2PC commit round is in progress.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.DistributedTransactionStatus.Compensating
member.summary: Saga compensation sequence is in progress.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.DistributedTransactionStatus.Aborting
member.summary: 2PC abort round is in progress (prepare failure).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.DistributedTransactionStatus.Committed
member.summary: Terminal: the scope committed successfully.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.DistributedTransactionStatus.Aborted
member.summary: Terminal: the scope rolled back.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.DistributedTransactionStatus.InDoubt
member.summary: Terminal: at least one shard acked prepare but failed the commit phase; resolution requires operator intervention (or the Phase 13 recovery log). The coordinator raises OnTransactionInDoubt when it enters this state.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.FileTransactionLog
member.summary: Durable file-backed introduced in Phase 13. Each entry is appended as a single JSON-line record under {directory}/{correlationId}.tx.jsonl with a terse ClosedCorrelationIds manifest used to reconstruct in-doubt scopes after a crash.

remarks.para: Design points:

item.description: Append semantics are preserved with ; concurrent writers to the same scope synchronise on the in-memory list lock.
is derived by scanning the directory for *.tx.jsonl files not present in the closed manifest so a process restart can re-surface in-doubt transactions.
renames the scope file into the closed/ sub-folder so the open set stays small; callers that need long-term retention can back up the folder.

member.summary: Creates a new durable log.
member.param: Optional I/O error callback. Defaults to silent so the hot path is never interrupted by disk flakes.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.FileTransactionLog.LoadOpenScopes
member.summary: Convenience helper for crash-recovery code: returns every entry for every currently-open correlation id.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.IDistributedTransactionCoordinator
member.summary: Phase 09 orchestrator for distributed transactions. Owns the lifecycle of a , picks the right via , and runs the prepare / commit / rollback / saga sequences across the enlisted shards.

remarks.para: The coordinator is read-only from the caller's perspective: once a scope is opened it is threaded through or verbatim. All state the coordinator needs lives either on the scope token or in the attached .
Strategies are implemented as partials of the concrete DistributedTransactionCoordinator class: SingleShard.cs, TwoPhaseCommit.cs, Saga.cs.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.IDistributedTransactionCoordinator.Log
member.summary: Log used for prepare / commit / rollback entries.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.IDistributedTransactionCoordinator.Begin(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Opens a new scope that enlists . The coordinator picks the strategy at this moment; the choice is frozen on the returned scope.
member.param: Override policy: when true, force saga for multi-shard work. Single-shard scopes always use the fast path regardless.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.IDistributedTransactionCoordinator.CommitScope(TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.DistributedTransactionScope)
member.summary: Commits the given scope according to the strategy captured at begin time. For saga mode, the caller must supply the ordered step list via BEFORE invoking this method — a saga scope commit without prior step execution is a no-op returning success.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.IDistributedTransactionCoordinator.RollbackScope(TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.DistributedTransactionScope)
member.summary: Rolls back the given scope. For single-shard / 2PC this translates to a cross-shard rollback/abort. For saga mode, the stored compensations (registered via ) are replayed in reverse order.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.IDistributedTransactionCoordinator.RunSaga(TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.DistributedTransactionScope,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.SagaStep})
member.summary: Runs a saga: executes each step's in order against its shard; on any failure, runs the completed steps' compensations in reverse. Only valid on scopes whose is .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.IDistributedTransactionLog
member.summary: Append-only log consumed by the Phase 09 . V1 ships an in-memory implementation () that is suitable for diagnostics but not for crash recovery; Phase 13 will introduce a durable file-based log that swaps in behind the same contract.
member.remarks: Implementations MUST be safe for concurrent appends: the coordinator writes entries from the prepare / commit / rollback fan-outs without serialising on its own.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.IDistributedTransactionLog.Append(TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.TransactionLogEntry)
member.summary: Appends one entry.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.IDistributedTransactionLog.Read(System.String)
member.summary: Returns a snapshot of every entry recorded for the given correlation id, ordered by .

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.IDistributedTransactionLog.Close(System.String)
member.summary: Drops the log for the given correlation id once the scope has reached a terminal state. Implementations may keep the tail for a short retention window (useful for support / audit) or discard immediately.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.IDistributedTransactionLog.OpenCorrelationIds
member.summary: Returns a snapshot of every scope currently in a non-terminal state — used by recovery to spot scopes that survived a crash. V1 returns only scopes still live in memory.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.InMemoryTransactionLog
member.summary: V1 implementation of : an in-process concurrent map of correlation id → list of log entries. Intended for diagnostics and unit tests — Phase 13 introduces a durable file-based log for crash recovery.

remarks.para: The log is deliberately simple: append is lock-free against other scopes (different keys), and synchronised within a single correlation id to keep stable. No eviction policy is enforced — callers are expected to terminated scopes.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.SagaStep
member.summary: Immutable descriptor for one step in a saga-mode . Each step pairs a forward operation with an idempotent compensation that undoes the forward on failure.

remarks.para: The coordinator treats as the shard against which and are executed. Both delegates receive the resolved for that shard so callers don't need to look it up themselves.

Compensations MUST be idempotent and tolerant of being called after a partial forward — the runner cannot tell whether a step partially succeeded before it threw. V1 runs compensations once; Phase 13 adds retry with back-off.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.SagaStep.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Func{TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.IProxyCluster,TheTechIdea.Beep.ConfigUtil.IErrorsInfo},System.Func{TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.IProxyCluster,TheTechIdea.Beep.ConfigUtil.IErrorsInfo})
member.summary: Creates a new step.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.SagaStep.Name
member.summary: Unique (within the scope) step name — appears in log entries.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.SagaStep.ShardId
member.summary: Shard the step targets.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.SagaStep.Forward
member.summary: Forward delegate — runs during the commit phase.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.SagaStep.Compensation
member.summary: Compensation delegate — runs during rollback in reverse order.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.TransactionDecisionResolver
member.summary: Stateless helper that maps (enlisted shards, per-shard capabilities, policy) → . The Phase 09 coordinator delegates every strategy decision to this resolver so the selection rules stay in one place and are unit-testable without touching the coordinator.
member.remarks:
Selection table:
Zero shards → throws (nothing to enlist).
One shard → .
Multiple shards, every involved cluster reports = true, and is false → .
Multiple shards, any involved cluster is saga-only OR the caller prefers saga → .
The resolver never reaches into the cluster pool for I/O; it only reads the opt-in capability flag. If a caller's shard id is missing from the supplied shard map the resolver falls back to saga to preserve correctness.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.TransactionDecisionResolver.Resolve(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.IProxyCluster},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Resolves the strategy for the given enlisted shards.
member.param: Unique shard ids enlisted in the scope.
Live shard map used to read capabilities.

When true, always prefer saga for multi-shard work even when every shard supports 2PC. Useful when the caller knows the workload is tolerant of eventual consistency and wants to avoid the prepare round's blocking window.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Distributed.Transactions.TransactionDecisionResolver.Every2PcCapable(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.IProxyCluster})
member.summary: Returns true when every enlisted shard is present in and has = true. Missing shards are treated as not-capable so the caller cannot silently end up in 2PC mode against a cluster the coordinator can't see.
