member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.Mapping.RelationshipToEntityMapper
member.summary:
Converts relationship analysis results from NavigationPropertyDetector and RelationshipInferencer into the existing BeepDM relationship classes (RelationShipKeys, ChildRelation).
This adapter bridges the new navigation property analysis with the existing EntityStructure relationship model, enabling automatic population of Relations and ChildRelations lists when converting POCOs to EntityStructure.

member.summary: Converts analysis of a navigation property into a RelationShipKeys object representing the foreign key relationship.
member.returns: RelationShipKeys configured for this relationship, or null if not applicable

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.Mapping.RelationshipToEntityMapper.CreateChildRelation(TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.Analysis.NavigationPropertyInfo,System.String,System.String,System.Type,System.Type)
member.summary: Converts analysis of a navigation property into a ChildRelation object for one-to-many relationships where the FK is on the child side.
member.returns: ChildRelation for one-to-many, or null if not applicable

member.summary: Extracts all relationships from a POCO type and populates EntityStructure.Relations and EntityStructure.ChildRelations with appropriate entries.

member.summary: Analyzes a POCO type and returns relationship summary for documentation/UI.
member.returns: Dictionary mapping relationship names to cardinality descriptions

member.summary: Gets the primary key property name for a type.

member.summary: Finds the foreign key property on a child type that references a parent type. Looks for properties named {ParentEntityName}Id or {ParentType.Name}Id.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.MongoDBHelpers.MongoDBHelper
member.summary:
MongoDB-specific helper for query generation and operations. MongoDB uses BSON documents and aggregation pipelines instead of SQL. This helper generates MongoDB query syntax and aggregation pipelines.
Note: MongoDB doesn't support traditional JOINs. Use $lookup for embedded aggregations. Transactions are supported for multi-document operations (v4.0+).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.MongoDBHelpers.MongoDBHelper.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.IDMEEditor)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the MongoDBHelper class.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.MongoDBHelpers.MongoDBHelper.GetSchemaQuery(System.String)
member.summary: MongoDB doesn't have traditional schemas, but collections can be listed. Returns a query to retrieve collection names.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.MongoDBHelpers.MongoDBHelper.GetTableExistsQuery(System.String)
member.summary: Gets a query to check if a collection exists.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.MongoDBHelpers.MongoDBHelper.GetColumnInfoQuery(System.String)
member.summary: Gets a query to retrieve field information for a collection. MongoDB is schema-less, so this returns a sample document structure.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.MongoDBHelpers.MongoDBHelper.GenerateCreateTableSql(TheTechIdea.Beep.DataBase.EntityStructure,System.String,System.Nullable{TheTechIdea.Beep.Utilities.DataSourceType})
member.summary: MongoDB collections are created implicitly when first document is inserted. This method acknowledges that Ddl is not needed for collection creation.

member.summary: Generates MongoDB command to drop a collection.

member.summary: Generates MongoDB command to remove all documents from a collection.

member.summary: Generates MongoDB command to create an index.

member.summary: MongoDB is schema-less, so adding columns is not applicable. Returns success since no action is needed.

member.summary: MongoDB is schema-less, so dropping columns is not applicable. Returns success since no action is needed.

member.summary: Generates MongoDB command to rename a collection.

member.summary: MongoDB doesn't have traditional primary key queries. The _id field is the primary key.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.RdbmsHelpers.RdbmsHelper
member.summary:
Partial class for RdbmsHelper providing constraint and relationship Ddl operations (PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE, CHECK constraints).
Each method generates database-agnostic SQL, then adapts for specific RDBMS syntax.

Unified RDBMS helper implementing IDataSourceHelper interface for all RDBMS databases. Provides a single entry point for all RDBMS query generation and operations.
Supported Databases (9): - SQL Server, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQLite, DB2, Firebird - Azure SQL, AWS RDS
This implementation delegates to the legacy helper classes: - DatabaseSchemaQueryHelper - Schema and metadata queries - DatabaseObjectCreationHelper - Ddl operations - DatabaseDMLHelper - DML operations (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, SELECT) - DatabaseEntityHelper - Entity analysis and validation

Partial class for RdbmsHelper providing transaction control operations.
Supports BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK for ACID compliance across RDBMS databases. Syntax varies by RDBMS (SQL Server uses BEGIN TRANSACTION, MySQL uses START TRANSACTION).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.RdbmsHelpers.RdbmsHelper.GenerateBeginTransactionSql
member.summary: Generates SQL to begin a transaction. Syntax varies by RDBMS: - SQL Server: BEGIN TRANSACTION - MySQL/PostgreSQL/SQLite/Firebird: BEGIN TRANSACTION or START TRANSACTION - Oracle: implicit (transactions start with first DML statement)

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.RdbmsHelpers.RdbmsHelper.GenerateCommitSql
member.summary: Generates SQL to commit a transaction. Standard SQL syntax: COMMIT Also supported: COMMIT TRANSACTION (SQL Server), COMMIT WORK (PostgreSQL)

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.RdbmsHelpers.RdbmsHelper.GenerateRollbackSql
member.summary: Generates SQL to rollback (abort) a transaction. Standard SQL syntax: ROLLBACK Also supported: ROLLBACK TRANSACTION (SQL Server), ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT (PostgreSQL)

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.RdbmsHelpers.RdbmsHelper.GenerateSavepointSql(System.String)
member.summary: Generates SQL to create a savepoint within a transaction. Useful for partial rollback in complex transaction scenarios. Syntax: SAVEPOINT savepoint_name (PostgreSQL, Oracle, MySQL 5.7+) SQL Server uses: SAVE TRANSACTION savepoint_name

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.RdbmsHelpers.RdbmsHelper.GenerateRollbackToSavepointSql(System.String)
member.summary: Generates SQL to rollback to a specific savepoint. Useful for undoing part of a transaction without committing or rolling back entirely. Syntax: ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT savepoint_name (PostgreSQL, Oracle) SQL Server uses: ROLLBACK TRANSACTION savepoint_name

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.RdbmsHelpers.RdbmsHelper.GetTransactionIsolationLevelQuery
member.summary: Gets the current transaction isolation level for the RDBMS. Returns SQL query to retrieve isolation level information. Different RDBMS store this in different system views/functions.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.RdbmsHelpers.RdbmsHelper.GenerateSetTransactionIsolationLevelSql(System.String)
member.summary: Generates SQL to set the transaction isolation level. Controls how transactions interact with concurrent access. Levels: ReadUncommitted to ReadCommitted to RepeatableRead to Serializable

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.RedisHelpers.RedisHelper
member.summary:
Redis-specific helper for query generation and operations. Redis is an in-memory key-value store with support for various data structures. This helper generates Redis commands and Lua scripts for operations.
Note: Redis doesn't have traditional schemas. Key patterns and data structures are used. Lua scripts provide atomic multi-command operations. TTL (Time-To-Live) is a first-class feature.

member.summary: Gets a command to retrieve information about a key's type and TTL.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Helpers.UniversalDataSourceHelpers.RestApiHelpers.RestApiHelper
member.summary:
Generic REST API helper for query generation and operations. Works with standard REST endpoints that follow REST conventions.
Note: REST APIs have limited server-side capabilities. No transactions, joins, or aggregations support. Query parameters and JSON payloads are used for operations.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Json.JsonDataSource
member.summary: JsonDataSource provides CRUD operations on a JSON file acting as a data source. It implements IDataSource and manages entities derived from JSON objects/arrays. This class supports reading, writing, updating, and deleting JSON objects from a file, and includes the ability to generate EntityStructures dynamically.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.CircuitBreaker
member.summary: Thread-safe circuit breaker that supports three states (Closed, Open, HalfOpen), severity-weighted failure accumulation, and a configurable consecutive-success threshold before closing from HalfOpen.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.CircuitBreaker.CanExecute
member.summary: Returns true when a request may proceed through the circuit.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.CircuitBreaker.RecordSuccess
member.summary: Records a successful call. Closes circuit after enough consecutive successes in HalfOpen.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.CircuitBreaker.RecordFailure(TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyErrorSeverity)
member.summary: Records a failed call. Critical/High severity failures accumulate extra weight, accelerating the transition to Open.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.CircuitBreaker.Reset
member.summary: Forces the circuit to Closed state. Use only for manual recovery or tests.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.EntityAffinityMap
member.summary: Thread-safe map from entity (table) name → owning node ID. Supports exact-match and prefix-match lookups (longest prefix wins). Inspired by Vitess VSchema shard/tablet routing and Kafka partition leader election.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.EntityAffinityMap.FallbackNodeId
member.summary: When no exact/prefix match exists, fall back to this node. Null means "no static fallback — use the cluster router".

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.EntityAffinityMap.MapEntity(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Map an exact entity name to a node.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.EntityAffinityMap.MapPrefix(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Map all entity names starting with to a node.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.EntityAffinityMap.UnmapEntity(System.String)
member.summary: Remove an exact entity mapping.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.EntityAffinityMap.UnmapPrefix(System.String)
member.summary: Remove a prefix mapping.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.EntityAffinityMap.Resolve(System.String)
member.summary: Resolve the preferred nodeId for . Returns null when no mapping is found and is null.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.EntityAffinityMap.ReassignAfterFailure(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String})
member.summary: Reassign all entity/prefix mappings that point to to a new node chosen round-robin from . Returns the reassignment map (keys are "entity:<name>" or "prefix:<name>").

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.EntityAffinityMap.GetAllMappings
member.summary: Returns all current exact and prefix mappings for observability.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.IProxyCluster.SupportsTwoPhaseCommit
member.summary: Indicates whether every node in the cluster exposes a two-phase-commit-capable (explicit prepare + commit, durable participant state on crash). Defaults to false so existing cluster implementations keep their behaviour — concrete clusters opt-in by overriding this property once every node's underlying driver supports the pattern.
member.remarks:
The Phase 09 TransactionDecisionResolver uses this flag to pick between
and for multi-shard work. Clusters that return false force saga mode, which preserves correctness at the cost of atomicity across shards.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyCluster
member.summary: ProxyCluster — fault injection + per-node rate limiting partition (Phase 11.10).
ProxyCluster — request hedging + outlier detection partition (Phase 11.8).
ProxyCluster — node management partition. Implements: AddNode, RemoveNode, GetNodes, DrainNode/DrainNodeAsync, IProxyDataSource datasource-membership shims.

ProxyCluster — node probing partition. Runs a lightweight background timer that probes every node with and tracks consecutive failures / recoveries to implement IProxyDataSource watchdog semantics. Also owns the affinity TTL sweep timer.
ProxyCluster — query routing rules + traffic-split partition (Phase 11.7). Evaluates declarative and to override the default router for specific traffic patterns.
ProxyCluster — scatter-gather reads + replica lag awareness (Phase 11.9).

Nodes added with will automatically be saved to config. Call after construction to restore a previously saved cluster topology (local nodes only — remote nodes require their transport to be rebuilt; see ).

Policy governing routing, retries, probing, etc.

member.summary:

Rebuilds local nodes from ConfigEditor.DataConnections. For each stored record with ParameterList["ClusterName"] == clusterName and IsRemote == false, the method resolves the backing from and calls .
Remote nodes cannot be rehydrated automatically because their transport (HTTP client, credentials) cannot be reconstructed from config alone. Use to supply a factory for remote nodes.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyDataSource
member.summary: Phase 6 — per-source latency tracking for SLO computation and adaptive routing. Maintains a rolling window of the most recent latency samples and exposes p50/p95/p99 percentile summaries via .

Watchdog layer: active health probing of the write-primary on a tight cycle, automatic role promotion (Replica → Primary) when the current Primary fails, and demotion back to Replica when the original Primary recovers. Runs as a background and communicates via events.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyDataSource.StopWatchdog
member.summary: Stops the watchdog background loop.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyDataSource.GetWatchdogStatus

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ICircuitStateStore
member.summary: Abstraction over the circuit-breaker state storage layer. Implement this interface to replace the default in-process store with a distributed backend (e.g. Redis, SQL Server, Azure Table Storage).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ICircuitStateStore.CanExecute(System.String)
member.summary: Returns true when the circuit for allows a call to proceed (Closed or HalfOpen within reset window).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ICircuitStateStore.RecordSuccess(System.String)
member.summary: Records a successful call result. May close an open circuit.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ICircuitStateStore.RecordFailure(System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyErrorSeverity)
member.summary:
Records a failed call result. Severity weights the failure accumulation:
Critical — immediately breaches the failure threshold.
High — counts as two failures.
Medium / Low — counts as one failure.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ICircuitStateStore.Initialize(System.String,System.Int32,System.TimeSpan,System.Int32)
member.summary: Registers (or reinitialises) the circuit entry for a datasource. Called when a datasource is added to the proxy or when policy is applied.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ICircuitStateStore.Remove(System.String)
member.summary: Removes the circuit entry for a datasource. Called when a datasource is removed from the proxy.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ICircuitStateStore.GetState(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the current , or Closed if not tracked.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ICircuitStateStore.ForceOpen(System.String)
member.summary: Manually trips the circuit to Open (e.g. for maintenance windows).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ICircuitStateStore.Reset(System.String)
member.summary: Manually resets the circuit to Closed (e.g. after manual recovery).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.InProcessCircuitStateStore
member.summary: Default in-process circuit-state store backed by . Thread-safe; suitable for single-machine deployments. Replace with a distributed implementation for multi-replica or multi-region deployments.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.RedisCircuitStateStore
member.summary:
Redis-backed . All keys live under the prefix ":" so multiple clusters can share a single Redis instance without key collisions.
Requires StackExchange.Redis NuGet package. Add <PackageReference Include="StackExchange.Redis" Version="2.*" /> to the project file if not already present.
member.remarks:
State key format:
<prefix>:state:<dsName> → "Closed" | "Open" | "HalfOpen"
<prefix>:fails:<dsName> → integer (INCR)

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.RedisCircuitStateStore.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a store backed by the specified Redis connection. Falls back silently to in-process state when Redis is unreachable.
member.param: Cluster-unique prefix for all Redis keys.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyLogRedactor
member.summary: Scrubs PII patterns from strings before they reach the log. Used by via LogSafe() helpers. Disabled per-policy via .

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyLogRedactor.Redact(System.String)
member.summary: Returns a redacted copy of safe to write to the log. All PII patterns are replaced with [REDACTED]. Returns the original string unchanged if it is null or empty.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyLogRedactor.RedactException(System.Exception)
member.summary: Extracts and redacts the message from an exception chain, concatenating up to the inner-most non-null message.

member.summary: Distribute across nodes proportionally to their .

member.summary: Always pick the node with the lowest rolling average response time.

member.summary: Consistent hash ring keyed on .

member.summary: Declares the read/write role of a registered datasource.

member.summary: Controls how writes are distributed across Primary-role datasources.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyWriteMode.SinglePrimary
member.summary: Default: route write to the first available Primary only.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyWriteMode.FanOut
member.summary: Fan-out: attempt write on ALL Primary-role datasources concurrently. Fails if any Primary rejects.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyWriteMode.QuorumWrite
member.summary: Quorum write: succeed if at least Primaries acknowledge. Remaining writes are best-effort.

member.summary: When true (default), all proxy log messages pass through before reaching the DME logger. Set false in development to see raw values.

member.summary: Controls how writes are routed to Primary-role datasources.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyPolicy.WriteFanOutQuorum
member.summary: Minimum number of Primaries that must acknowledge a write for to report success. Ignored for other modes. Defaults to 1.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyPolicy.NodeRoutingStrategy
member.summary: Node-selection algorithm used by .

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyPolicy.NodeUnhealthyThreshold
member.summary: Consecutive probe failures before a node is marked down.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyPolicy.NodeHealthyThreshold
member.summary: Consecutive probe successes before a down node is restored.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyPolicy.NodeProbeIntervalMs
member.summary: How often (ms) the cluster probes each node for liveness.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyPolicy.NodeProbeTimeoutMs
member.summary: Timeout (ms) for a single liveness probe call.

member.summary: When true, sessions are pinned to a node for the affinity TTL duration.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyPolicy.NodeAffinityTtlSeconds
member.summary: How long (seconds) a session-affinity binding remains alive without activity.

member.summary: When positive, new nodes ramp up their effective weight over this period (milliseconds). Set 0 to disable slow-start.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyPolicy.MaxReplicaLagMs
member.summary: Maximum replication lag (ms) a replica may have before it is excluded from read routing. Set 0 to disable the lag guard.

member.summary: Static entity→node mappings. Null = entity affinity routing disabled.

member.summary: What to do when the affinity-pinned node is unavailable.

member.summary: Ordered routing rules (descending Priority). First match wins.

member.summary: Traffic-splitting rules for canary deployments.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.ProxyPolicy.OutlierDetection
member.summary: Outlier detection configuration. Null = disabled.

member.summary: Delay (ms) before issuing the hedge request.

member.summary: Time (ms) a queued request waits before timing out.

member.summary: What the cluster does when the affinity-pinned node is unavailable.

member.summary: Use the normal router strategy to pick any live node.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.EntityAffinityFallback.ThrowException
member.summary: Throw an exception — the entity cannot be served without its owner.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.EntityAffinityFallback.WaitForOwner
member.summary: Wait until the pinned node recovers (blocks up to NodeProbeIntervalMs).

member.summary: Strategy applied by a routing rule when no explicit TargetNodeId is given.

member.summary: Route to the primary node (role = Primary).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.HttpProxyTransport
member.summary:
HTTP/JSON implementation of .
Sends requests to {baseUrl}/proxy/execute as JSON POST and maps the JSON response back to .
Security: pass a pre-configured with mTLS, bearer token, or an API-key header already set in its default headers. Never log the raw request body — it may contain query results.
Connection reuse: pass a singleton (or one managed by IHttpClientFactory) from the outside. If the parameterless path is used, an internal client is created and disposed with this instance.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.HttpProxyTransport.#ctor(System.String,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a transport that manages its own .
member.param: Base URL of the remote worker, e.g. http://worker-b:5100. Do not include a trailing slash.
Optional API key sent as X-Proxy-Api-Key header. Must be treated as a secret — never log it.

Optional shared HMAC secret (UTF-8 encoded) used to sign every request. The worker must be configured with the same secret in . When omitted, request signing is disabled (not recommended for production). The secret must be at least 32 characters long.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.HttpProxyTransport.#ctor(System.String,System.Net.Http.HttpClient,System.Boolean,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Creates a transport backed by the supplied . The client is not disposed when this transport is disposed.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.HttpProxyTransport.FromConnectionProperties(TheTechIdea.Beep.ConfigUtil.ConnectionProperties)
member.summary:
Creates an from a node's as stored in ConfigEditor.DataConnections.
Reads: Url (base URL), ApiKey (X-Proxy-Api-Key header), Timeout (seconds), and ParameterList["HmacSecret"] (signing secret — treat as sensitive, never log).
member.param: A record whose IsRemote == true and DriverName == "BeepProxyNode".
member.exception: Thrown when config.Url is null or whitespace.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.IProxyTransport
member.summary:
Abstracts the network channel between a (client) and a worker's (server).
Implementations provided:
— HTTP/JSON over System.Net.Http (zero extra NuGet)
You can implement this interface to use gRPC, NATS, RabbitMQ, named pipes, or an in-process test double without changing any proxy logic.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.IProxyTransport.NodeAddress
member.summary: Human-readable address of the remote worker node, e.g. "http://worker-b:5100" or "grpc://worker-c:5200". Used in log messages and diagnostics only — never parsed by the proxy.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.IProxyTransport.SendAsync(TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRemoteRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Sends a to the remote worker and returns the worker's .
Transport exceptions (network unreachable, timeout) should be thrown rather than swallowed — let the caller (ProxyCluster's retry policy) decide how to handle them.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.IProxyTransport.PingAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Fires a lightweight liveness ping to the remote worker. Returns true when the worker is reachable and healthy. Must complete within a few hundred milliseconds so that the ProxyCluster probe loop runs on schedule.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRemoteOperations
member.summary: String constants that identify each remoted operation in the wire protocol. Both (client) and (server) reference these.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRemoteRequest
member.summary: Serializable envelope sent by over the transport to the worker's . All complex payload fields are JSON-encoded strings to keep the DTO fully serializable without coupling to domain type assemblies on the transport tier.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRemoteRequest.CorrelationId
member.summary: Unique per-request correlation ID for distributed tracing.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRemoteRequest.Operation
member.summary: One of the constants.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRemoteRequest.RequestTimestamp
member.summary: UTC timestamp when this request was created. Workers reject requests where |UtcNow − RequestTimestamp| > 5 minutes to prevent replay attacks. Set automatically by via when an HMAC secret is configured.

member.summary: HMAC-SHA256 signature of the canonical message {CorrelationId}|{Operation}|{RequestTimestamp:O} keyed with the shared hmacSecret. Left null when no secret is configured (insecure mode).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRemoteResponse.WorkerAvgLatencyMs

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRemoteRequestDispatcher
M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRemoteRequestDispatcher.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.IDMEEditor,System.String,System.String)
member.param: Local DMEEditor that resolves the backing IDataSource.
Optional default datasource name used when the request does not specify one. Typically the name registered via ConfigEditor.AddDataConnection.

Optional shared HMAC secret (UTF-8 encoded) used to verify every inbound request. Must match the secret configured in on the coordinator side.
When set, requests with a missing, invalid, or expired signature are rejected with HTTP-equivalent error code 401. This protects against unauthenticated callers and replay attacks.
Use null (default) only for local-loopback testing — never in production.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRemoteRequestDispatcher.DispatchAsync(TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRemoteRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Dispatches the to the appropriate / method and returns the wrapped result.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRequestSigner
member.summary:
Shared HMAC-SHA256 signing and verification helper for the proxy wire protocol.
Canonical message format: {CorrelationId}|{Operation}|{RequestTimestamp:O}
The message covers the fields that uniquely identify this request (correlation ID), what it does (operation), and when it was sent (timestamp). Per-field coverage prevents field-swap attacks while keeping the surface small and deterministic.
Replay protection: the worker rejects requests where |UtcNow − RequestTimestamp| > .

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRequestSigner.ClockSkewTolerance
member.summary: Maximum allowed difference between the coordinator's clock and the worker's clock. Requests outside this window are rejected as potential replays.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRequestSigner.Sign(TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRemoteRequest,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Signs in-place: sets RequestTimestamp to UtcNow and computes a fresh HMAC-SHA256 Signature.
member.param: Raw UTF-8 bytes of the shared secret. Must be at least 32 bytes for adequate security. Never log or transmit this value in clear text.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRequestSigner.Verify(TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRemoteRequest,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Returns a indicating whether the request's signature and timestamp are valid.
member.param: The server's copy of the shared secret.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.ProxyRequestSigner.CryptographicEquals(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Constant-time string equality to prevent timing attacks.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.SignatureVerificationResult
member.summary: The outcome of .

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.SignatureVerificationResult.Valid
member.summary: Signature is present and valid; timestamp is within tolerance.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.SignatureVerificationResult.MissingSignature
member.summary: The request carries no Signature or RequestTimestamp.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.SignatureVerificationResult.InvalidSignature
member.summary: The HMAC does not match the shared secret (tampered or wrong key).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.SignatureVerificationResult.ReplayAttack
member.summary: The RequestTimestamp is outside the ±5-minute tolerance window — likely a replayed or very-delayed request.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.RemoteProxyDataSource
M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.RemoteProxyDataSource.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.IProxyTransport,System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.IDMEEditor)
member.param: Logical name shown in metrics and logs.
Local DMEEditor (for logging only — data ops go remote).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Proxy.Remote.RemoteProxyDataSource.FromConnectionProperties(TheTechIdea.Beep.ConfigUtil.ConnectionProperties,TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.IDMEEditor)
member.summary: Creates a from a node's as stored in ConfigEditor (DriverName = "BeepProxyNode").

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinParsers.CsvColumnParser
member.summary: Parses CSV-style column-reference expressions of the form: column[N] or row.column[N] where N is a 0-based column index or column name. Examples: col[2], row.price[0], Amount[3].

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinParsers.FormulaParser
member.summary: Parses Excel/spreadsheet-style formula expressions into tokens. Recognizes: - Function calls: SUM(A, B), IF(cond, trueVal, falseVal) - Arithmetic ops: + - * / ^ - Comparison ops: < > <= >= = <> - String literals: "text" or 'text' - Number literals: 42, 3.14 - Cell references: A1, $B$2, Sheet1!C3 - Identifiers: bare names used as variables/parameters Resulting tokens are stored in the for downstream evaluation by .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinParsers.JsonRuleTreeParser
T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinParsers.NfelParser
member.summary:
NFEL — Natural Formula Expression Language parser. A lightweight, human-readable expression language inspired by MVEL/SpEL: - Arithmetic: price * qty, (a + b) / 2 - Comparison: age >= 18, name == 'Alice' - Logical: isActive && age > 0, !flag - Ternary: score > 50 ? 'pass' : 'fail' - Field refs: order.total - Null check: value != null
Tokens are built with correct values for downstream use by expression evaluation.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinParsers.SqlWhereParser
member.summary: Parses simplified SQL WHERE clause expressions into a . Recognizes: - Simple comparisons: field = 'value', field > 42 - IN list: field IN ('a','b','c') - NULL checks: field IS NULL, field IS NOT NULL - BETWEEN: field BETWEEN 10 AND 20 - AND / OR joins Returns a flat list of tokens representing the condition tree.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Compute.AgeFromDate
member.summary: Computes the elapsed time between a source date value and today (or a reference date). Parameters: Value — the date to measure from (parseable date string or DateTime). Unit — Years | Months | Days | Hours (default Years). ReferenceDate — date to measure to (default UTC now). Outputs: Result (numeric in requested unit), Years, Months, Days.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Compute.EvalExpression
member.summary: Evaluates a mathematical or string expression with {FieldName} token substitution. Uses for safe, eval-free arithmetic. Parameters: Expression — e.g. {Price} * {Qty} * (1 - {Discount}). Record — IDictionary<string,object> or "field=val;…" string. Individual field values may also be passed directly as keyed parameters. Outputs: Result (computed value), Expression (token-expanded expression string).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Compute.IfElseValue
member.summary: Returns TrueValue or FalseValue based on a single-field comparison. Parameters: ConditionField — field name to look up in Record (or directly in parameters). Operator — =, !=, >, <, >=, <=, contains, startswith, endswith (default =). CompareValue — value to compare against. TrueValue — value returned when condition holds. FalseValue — value returned when condition does not hold (default empty string). Record — or "field=val;…" string. Outputs: Result (chosen value), Condition (bool).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Compute.MapValueTable
member.summary: Maps an input value to an output value via a lookup table — equivalent to a SWITCH/CASE. Parameters: Value — input value to look up (case-insensitive match). Map — semicolon-separated "input=output" pairs. Use "*=default" for a catch-all entry. Example: "A=Active;I=Inactive;*=Unknown". Default — fallback string when no entry matches (overrides * entry). Outputs: Result (mapped value or default), Matched (bool — true when an exact key matched).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Date.AddDatePart
member.summary: Adds a specified amount of a date part to Value. Parameters: Value (DateTime or parseable string), Amount (int), Part ("years"|"months"|"days"|"hours"|"minutes"|"seconds"). Returns the resulting .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Date.DateDiff
member.summary: Computes the difference between Start and End dates. Parameters: Start (DateTime/string), End (DateTime/string), Part ("days"|"hours"|"minutes"|"seconds"|"totaldays"). Returns the difference as a double (fractional for sub-day parts).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Date.IsWithinBusinessHours
member.summary: Returns true if Value falls within business hours on a weekday. Parameters: Value (DateTime/string), StartHour (int 0-23, default 9), EndHour (int 0-23, default 17). Optional TimeZone (IANA/Windows timezone id, default = local). Optional IncludeWeekends (bool string, default "false").

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.DQ.IsInList
member.summary: Returns true when Value is contained in AllowedValues. Parameters: Value (object), AllowedValues (comma-separated string OR IEnumerable<object>). Optional IgnoreCase (bool string, default "false") — applies to strings.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.DQ.IsInRange
member.summary: Returns true when Value lies between Min and Max. Parameters: Value (IComparable), Min, Max, optional Inclusive (bool string "true"/"false", default true).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.DQ.IsNullOrEmpty
member.summary: Returns true when the input value is null, DBNull, empty string, or whitespace. Parameters: Value (object).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.DQ.IsUnique
member.summary: Checks whether Value is unique for FieldName in EntityName. Parameters: DataSourceName, EntityName, FieldName, Value, IDMEEditor.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.DQ.MatchesExpectedType
member.summary: Tries to parse Value as ExpectedType and returns true on success. Parameters: Value (object), ExpectedType (string: "int", "double", "decimal", "date", "datetime", "guid", "bool", "string"). Output dict also contains ParsedValue on success.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.DQ.MatchesRegex
member.summary: Returns true when Value matches the regex Pattern. Parameters: Value (string), Pattern (string), optional IgnoreCase (bool string, default "false").

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.DQ.ReferentialIntegrity
member.summary: Checks referential integrity: verifies that Value exists in LookupEntityName.LookupField. Parameters: DataSourceName, LookupEntityName, LookupField, Value, IDMEEditor. Output: bool + MatchedRecord (first matched row as dict).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Enrich.AddMetadataFields
member.summary: Enriches a record with computed metadata fields: __timestamp, __ruleKey, __version, and any user-defined tags. Parameters (all optional): RuleKey — source rule identifier to stamp (default: this rule's key). Version — schema/pipeline version string. Tags — semicolon-separated "key=value" tag string. TimestampField — output field name for timestamp (default "__timestamp").

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Enrich.LookupEnrich
member.summary: Looks up a record in LookupEntityName by LookupField==Value and copies the value of ReturnField into the output. Parameters: DataSourceName, LookupEntityName, LookupField, Value, ReturnField, IDMEEditor. Optional DefaultValue returned when no match found.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Enrich.MergeFields
member.summary: Merges all key-value pairs from SourceFields into the output dictionary, optionally applying a prefix to each key. Parameters: SourceFields (semicolon-separated "Key=Value" string OR any IDictionary<string,object> via the SourceFields parameter). Optional Prefix (string prepended to each key).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Flow.CircuitBreaker
member.summary: Implements the Circuit Breaker resilience pattern. States: Closed (normal) → Open (failing) → HalfOpen (probe) → Closed. Parameters: StateKey — unique string key per circuit. FailureThreshold — failures before opening (default 5). ResetSeconds — seconds before attempting half-open probe (default 30). CallSucceeded — bool string — report the outcome of the last protected call. Optional IRuleStateStore. Returns true when the circuit is Closed or HalfOpen (call is allowed). Returns false when Open (call should be rejected).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Flow.DeadLetterQueue
member.summary: Writes a failed record to a dead-letter entity table so it can be inspected and reprocessed later without losing data. Parameters: DataSourceName — name of the registered data source. EntityName — target dead-letter table / collection name. RecordId — identifier of the original record. FailureReason — human-readable error description. Payload — original payload (string or object; stored as ToString()). IDMEEditor — injected instance. Returns true on successful write, false on failure.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Flow.RetryWithBackoff
member.summary: Retries a downstream rule/action up to MaxRetries times with exponential backoff. In this implementation the rule records retry state and returns false when the maximum retry count is exceeded — the caller is responsible for re-invoking. Parameters: StateKey — unique key scoping retry state. MaxRetries — int (default 3). DelayMs — initial delay in ms (default 200); doubles each retry. LastResult — bool string — pass "true" when the downstream call succeeded. Optional IRuleStateStore for persistent counter. Returns true when retries are not yet exhausted and more attempts may be made.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.GetRecordCount.RuleText
member.summary: The textual expression that defines the rule's logic. For example:Rule Test Should be

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Notify.EmitAuditTrail
member.summary: Writes an audit trail record to the configured data source. Parameters: DataSourceName — target data source. EntityName — audit log entity/table name. Event — event label ("Validated", "Rejected", etc.). RecordId — identifier of the audited record. IDMEEditor — engine reference. Optional Details — free-text description. Optional ActorId — user/system that triggered the event.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Notify.LogMessage
member.summary: Logs a structured notification message to the BeepDM diagnostic log via IDMEEditor.AddLogMessage (if available) or falls back to . Parameters: Message — message template (supports {Key} tokens from parameters). Level — "Info"|"Warning"|"Error" (default "Info"). Source — log source label (default "RulesEngine"). Optional IDMEEditor — when present, uses native logging.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Notify.SendWebhook
member.summary: Sends an HTTP POST webhook with a JSON payload built from Payload parameters. Parameters: Url — target URL (required). Payload — semicolon-separated "key=value" pairs OR a JSON string. Token — optional Bearer token for Authorization header. TimeoutSeconds — request timeout (default "10"). Returns true on HTTP 2xx; writes StatusCode to output. NOTE: Uses HttpClient. For production, inject a shared client via IoC.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Numeric.Clamp
member.summary: Clamps Value between Min and Max. Parameters: Value (numeric), Min (numeric), Max (numeric). Returns the clamped double value.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Numeric.GetEntityAggregate
member.summary: Computes an aggregate (sum, avg, min, max, count) over FieldName in EntityName on DataSourceName. Parameters: DataSourceName, EntityName, FieldName, Function ("sum"|"avg"|"min"|"max"|"count"), IDMEEditor. Optional FilterField, FilterOperator, FilterValue for row pre-filtering.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Numeric.RoundNumeric
member.summary: Rounds Value to Decimals decimal places. Parameters: Value (numeric string), Decimals (int string, default "2"). Optional MidpointRounding ("AwayFromZero"|"ToEven", default "AwayFromZero").

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Query.RunParameterizedLookup
member.summary: Performs a single-key lookup against a data source entity and returns a named field from the first match. Designed for common "lookup a label/value by ID" patterns. Parameters: IDMEEditor — injected instance. DataSourceName — registered data source name. EntityName — entity/table to query. LookupField — field name to match against (e.g. CustomerId). LookupValue — value used to match (e.g. the ID). ReturnField — field from the matching row to return. DefaultValue — (optional) value returned when no row is found. Outputs: Result (field value or default), Found (bool).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Query.RunScalarQuery
member.summary: Runs a filtered query on a registered data source and returns the first field of the first matching row as a scalar value. Parameters: IDMEEditor — injected instance. DataSourceName — registered data source name. EntityName — entity/table to query. Filters — (optional) semicolon-separated "field:op:value" triples, e.g. "Status:=:Active;Score:>:50". OutputField — (optional) specific field name to return; defaults to first field. Outputs: Result (scalar), RowCount.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Query.SetFromQuery
member.summary: Runs a parameterized query on a data source and writes the result value into a named field of the supplied record. Parameters: IDMEEditor — injected instance. DataSourceName — registered data source name. EntityName — entity/table to query. Filters — (optional) semicolon-separated "field:op:value" triples. OutputField — field in the result set to read (defaults to first field). TargetField — field name in Record to write the result into. Record — or "field=val;…" string. Outputs: Record (updated), Result (value written), TargetField.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Record.ConditionalSetField
member.summary: Sets TargetField to TrueValue when a field condition is met, or to FalseValue when it is not (if supplied). Parameters: Record — or "field=val;…" string. ConditionField — field name to evaluate. Operator — =, !=, >, <, >=, <=, contains, startswith, endswith, isnull, isnotnull (default =). CompareValue — value to compare against (not used by isnull/isnotnull). TargetField — field name to set. TrueValue — value written when condition is true. FalseValue — value written when condition is false (optional; field left unchanged when absent). Outputs: Record (updated copy), Applied (bool), TargetField, NewValue.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Record.CopyField
member.summary: Copies the value of SourceField to TargetField within a record. Parameters: Record — or "field=val;…" string. SourceField — field name to read from. TargetField — field name to write to. Outputs: Record (updated copy), CopiedValue, TargetField.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Record.IncrementField
member.summary: Adds a numeric Step to a field value, with optional min/max clamping. Parameters: Record — or "field=val;…" string (optional). FieldName — numeric field to increment. Step — amount to add; can be negative for decrement (default 1). Clamp — (optional) "min:max" string to clamp the result. Outputs: Record (updated), OldValue, NewValue. Returns the new numeric value.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Record.SetFieldValue
member.summary: Sets a named field in a record to a literal value or a {Token}-interpolated string. Tokens are resolved from the Record dictionary or individual parameters. Parameters: FieldName — name of the field to set. Value — new value; may contain {OtherField} tokens. Record — or "field=val;…" string (optional). Outputs: FieldName, Value (resolved), Record (updated copy).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Route.RouteOnCondition
member.summary:
Evaluates a list of condition→route mappings and returns the first matching route label. Parameters: Routes (semicolon-separated list of "expression=>routeLabel" pairs), DefaultRoute (string, returned when no condition matches). Plus any context values referenced by the condition expressions as named parameters.
Example Routes value: "Status==Active=>ActiveQueue;Status==Pending=>WaitQueue" Conditions support literal equality comparison only in this implementation. For advanced expression evaluation integrate with .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Route.SplitIntoBuckets
member.summary: Splits a numeric Value into buckets and returns the bucket label. Parameters: Value (numeric), Buckets (semicolon-separated "min-max=label" or "max=label"), DefaultBucket (label when no bucket matches, default "Other"). Example Buckets: "0-25=Low;26-75=Medium;76-100=High"

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Route.ThrottleRule
member.summary: Limits the rate of rule activation per WindowSeconds sliding window. When the call count exceeds MaxCalls within the window, returns false. Requires an for persistent counter state. Parameters: StateKey (string scope key), MaxCalls (int), WindowSeconds (int). Optional IRuleStateStore (injected) or uses the static default store.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Schema.EnforceNotNull
member.summary: Enforces that a set of named fields are non-null and non-empty in the input record. Parameters: Record — IDictionary<string,object> OR a "field=value;…" semicolon-separated string. Fields — comma-separated list of field names to validate. Returns true when every specified field is present and non-empty. Outputs: NullFields (comma-separated list of offending fields).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Schema.ValidateSchemaFields
member.summary: Validates that an input record contains all required fields and, optionally, that each field value can be parsed as the declared type. Parameters: Record — IDictionary<string,object> OR a semicolon-separated "field=value;…" string. RequiredFields — comma-separated list of field names that must be present. FieldTypes — (optional) semicolon-separated "field:type" pairs. Supported types: int, double, decimal, bool, datetime, guid, string. Returns true when the record passes all checks. Outputs: MissingFields, InvalidFields, Errors (list).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Security.HashValue
member.summary: Hashes Value using the specified algorithm. Parameters: Value (string), Algorithm ("SHA256"|"SHA512"|"MD5", default "SHA256"). Optional Salt (string appended before hashing), Encoding ("hex"|"base64", default "hex").

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Security.MaskValue
member.summary: Masks Value to hide sensitive characters. Parameters: Value (string). Optional Mode ("last4"|"first4"|"middle"|"all", default "last4"), MaskChar (single char, default '*'). Examples: "1234567890" → "last4" = "******7890" / "first4" = "1234******" / "middle" = "12****90".

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Security.TokenizeValue
member.summary:
Replaces Value with a deterministic, reversible token scoped by VaultKey. Tokens are stored in an in-process vault keyed by VaultKey.
Parameters:
Value (string) — original sensitive value.
VaultKey (string) — logical vault/scope identifier (e.g. "creditCard", "ssn").
Operation ("tokenize"|"detokenize", default "tokenize").
Token (string) — required when Operation is "detokenize".
Note: the default vault is in-process only and is not persistent. Replace with a persistent implementation for production use.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Transform.PadOrTruncate
member.summary: Pads or truncates Value to exactly Length characters. Parameters: Value (string), Length (int string). Optional Align ("left"|"right", default "right"), PadChar (single char string, default " "). If the value exceeds Length it is truncated from the right.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Transform.ReplacePattern
member.summary: Replaces all occurrences of Pattern in Value with Replacement. Parameters: Value (string), Pattern (regex string), Replacement (string). Optional IgnoreCase (bool string, default "false").

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Transform.SplitAndTake
member.summary: Splits Value by Delimiter and returns the element at Index (0-based). Parameters: Value (string), Delimiter (string), Index (int string). Optional TrimParts (bool string, default "true").

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Transform.TrimAndNormalize
member.summary: Trims whitespace from Value and optionally changes casing. Parameters: Value (string), optional CaseMode ("upper"|"lower"|"title"|"none", default "none").

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Validate.ValidCreditCard
member.summary: Validates a credit card number using the Luhn algorithm and detects card type. Accepts numbers with or without spaces / dashes. Parameters: Value — card number string. Outputs: Result (bool), CardType (Visa|Mastercard|Amex|Discover|Unknown).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Validate.ValidEmail
member.summary: Validates an e-mail address using , which covers RFC 5321/5322 syntax checking. Parameters: Value — the address to validate. Returns true when the address is well-formed.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Validate.ValidPhone
member.summary: Validates a phone number. Parameters: Value — phone string to validate. Format — E164 | US | Any (default Any). Returns true when the number matches the requested format.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Validate.ValidPostalCode
member.summary: Validates postal/ZIP codes. Parameters: Value — the postal code string. Country — US | CA | UK | DE | FR | generic (default US).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.BuiltinRules.Validate.ValidSSN
member.summary: Validates a US Social Security Number in XXX-XX-XXXX format. The pattern rejects all-zero groups, the 666 prefix, and the 900-999 range (ITIN prefixes). Parameters: Value — the SSN string to validate. Returns true when the SSN is structurally valid.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Rules.RuleRegistry
member.summary:
Discovers and implementations from assemblies already scanned by AssemblyHandler and wires them into a / pair.
Mirrors the pattern used by PipelinePluginRegistry — all extension loading flows through IDMEEditor.assemblyHandler.ConfigEditor so external plug-in assemblies are automatically picked up with zero extra wiring.
Typical usage:
var registry = new RuleRegistry(editor); registry.Discover(); // scan already-loaded assemblies var engine = registry.Engine; var factory = registry.ParserFactory;

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.BeepAudit
member.summary: Production backed by the shared . Created by AddBeepAudit when is true. When disabled, callers receive instead.
member.remarks:
The class is split across three partial files:
.Core — fields, ctor, entry point.
.Query — query / purge / verify (Phase 02 stubs).
.Lifetime — flush semantics.
member.summary: Lifetime / shutdown half of . Delegates to the shared so audit and log events drain together.

Query / purge / integrity surface of . Phase 10 wires every method to the late-bound query engine and purge service supplied by the DI extension. When the audit feature is disabled the methods short-circuit; when the dependencies are missing they throw an explanatory .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.BeepAuditOptions
member.summary: Operator-facing configuration for the Beep audit-trail feature. Audit is lossless by policy so defaults differ from logging: , hash chain on, no samplers, budget breaches default to rather than deleting old records.
member.remarks: Phase 01 consumed only the toggle and budget fields. Phase 02 introduced the typed sink / redactor / enricher collections. Phase 04 adds , , and for the budget enforcer / sweeper integration. Audit deliberately exposes no sampler collection because audit events are lossless.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Bridges.FormsAuditBridge
member.summary: Adapts a forms-level stream into the unified pipeline. The bridge is invoked by the helper or by callers that subscribe to commit hooks directly.
member.remarks: The bridge is **append-only** — it does not write back into the legacy . The legacy store keeps its own life-cycle and the unified pipeline gains a parallel, signed copy suitable for compliance reporting and tamper-evident replay. Failures inside are swallowed so a misbehaving sink can never break the legacy commit path.

member.summary: Implements by forwarding each into the unified pipeline. Lets operators consolidate proxy-tier auditing alongside forms-, schema-, and auth-tier auditing without disturbing the existing .
member.remarks: The bridge is **non-blocking** — swallows every exception so it satisfies the strict contract ("must not throw"). Use a (or simple chain) to keep the legacy file sink alongside the bridge during rollout, then drop the legacy sink once the unified pipeline is trusted.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Export.AuditExporter
member.summary: Materializes the result of an as an export bundle (payload file + signed manifest). Sealed and partial — the format-specific writers live in .Ndjson, .Json, and .Csv.
member.remarks: The exporter never writes directly to operator storage — it hands the payload back as a array plus a matching . Callers persist the pair atomically so the manifest cannot drift away from the payload it certifies.
member.summary: CSV renderer for . Emits a flat row per audit event with stable column ordering. Field changes and property bags are JSON-encoded into single columns so the CSV stays rectangular while still preserving the full payload for downstream tools that can re-parse the embedded JSON.

JSON renderer for . Produces a single document { "events": [...] } containing every event in canonical form. The matching ships in a sibling .manifest.json file so payload and manifest can rotate independently.

NDJSON renderer for . Wraps each audit event in a thin so the canonical line format matches what the file/SQLite sinks emit at write time. The output is therefore directly verifiable against the chain hashes already present in the store.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Export.AuditExporter.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Query.IAuditQueryEngine,TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Export.ManifestSigner)

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Export.ExportManifest
member.summary: Tamper-evident manifest produced alongside every audit export. Carries the SHA-256 of the export payload, the count of events, the time window covered, the chains touched, and an HMAC of the canonical manifest payload signed with the same key material as the audit hash chain (Phase 08). Verifying an export means re-hashing the payload, recomputing the HMAC, and comparing.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Export.ManifestSigner.ComputePayloadSha256(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Computes the SHA-256 (hex) of the supplied payload bytes. Exposed so the exporter can stamp the manifest before the HMAC is computed.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.CanonicalJsonSerializer
member.summary: Deterministic JSON serializer used as the input to the audit hash chain. Properties are emitted in fixed alphabetical order, dates use round-trip ISO-8601 format, enums use their string name, and dictionaries are sorted by key. The same logical event therefore always produces the same byte sequence — a non-negotiable requirement for hash-chain reproducibility across processes, platforms, and .NET versions.
member.remarks: We do not delegate to because its property ordering depends on reflection metadata cache state and numeric formatting can differ subtly between runtimes. A small hand-written writer is the simplest way to keep the chain stable across .NET 8 / 9 / 10 and across operating systems.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.CanonicalJsonSerializer.SerializeForHash(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Models.AuditEvent)
member.summary: Serializes for chain hashing, excluding the chain fields that the signer fills in (, , ).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.EnvironmentKeyMaterialProvider
member.summary: Reads the HMAC secret from an environment variable (default BEEP_AUDIT_HMAC_SECRET). Returns a documented fallback secret only when the variable is missing — the fallback is intentionally weak so misconfiguration is visible without breaking development workflows.
member.remarks: Production hosts should set the environment variable to at least 32 bytes of random material (e.g. openssl rand -hex 32) and rotate per environment. The fallback secret is logged once at startup by Phase 11 self-observability.

member.summary: Fallback secret used only when the environment variable is missing. Documented so operators can recognise it in tests.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.EnvironmentKeyMaterialProvider.UsingProductionSecret
member.summary: Returns true if the environment variable is set and non-empty (i.e. the production secret is in use). Useful for the Phase 11 startup probe.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.HashChainSigner
member.summary: HMAC-SHA256 hash-chain signer. Sealed and partial — split into .Core (lifecycle), .Sign (Sequence/PrevHash/Hash assignment), and .Verify (post-hoc replay).
member.remarks: Sign operations are serialized per ChainId via a small per-chain mutex map so concurrent producers cannot race on sequence assignment. The signer caches the active anchor for each chain to avoid hitting on every event; the store is still updated synchronously after each successful sign so a crash never loses the chain head.
member.summary: Re-seal half of . Re-assigns Sequence, PrevHash, and Hash to the supplied chain after a GDPR-style purge has removed events out of the middle. Keeps the in-memory chain state and anchor store consistent with the new tail so subsequent calls continue without producing a sequence gap.
Signing half of . Owns the per-event Sequence/PrevHash/Hash assignment under a per-chain mutex so concurrent producers cannot race.
Verification half of . Replays the hash chain across an event sequence and reports the first divergence found. Used by the runbook entry-points and any periodic background check.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.HashChainSigner.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.IKeyMaterialProvider,TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.IChainAnchorStore)
member.summary: Creates a signer over the supplied key/anchor providers.

member.summary: Re-numbers and re-hashes every surviving event in (in the order supplied) for the chain identified by .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.IAuditEventReader
member.summary: Source from which the verifier loads a previously persisted chain. File-, SQLite-, and Cosmos-backed implementations all satisfy this contract; the verifier itself is storage-agnostic.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.IAuditEventReader.ReadChain(System.String)
member.summary: Streams every event for in strict Sequence order (1, 2, 3, …). Implementations should yield to keep memory bounded for large chains.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.IChainAnchorStore
member.summary: Persists the per-chain "last sequence + last hash" anchor across process restarts. The signer reads the anchor on first use of a chain id and writes back after every successful sign so the chain stays continuous through crashes and graceful restarts.
member.remarks: Implementations must be thread-safe and atomic at the per-chain level (a partial write that loses a hash would silently corrupt the chain). The default uses a tmp + fsync + rename pattern to guarantee atomicity on every supported filesystem.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.IChainAnchorStore.TryRead(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the anchor for , or null if none.

member.summary: Persists atomically.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.IChainAnchorStore.ReadAll
member.summary: Returns a snapshot of every known anchor.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.IHashChainSigner
member.summary: Assigns the tamper-evident chain fields (Sequence, PrevHash, Hash) to an before it is enqueued onto the audit pipeline. Verifies a chain after the fact via .
member.remarks: Sign operations are single-threaded per ChainId so the Sequence numbers stay strictly monotonic even when many producers audit concurrently. The signer reads its initial state from the configured on first use of a chain and writes the new anchor after every sign.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.IHashChainSigner.Sign(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Models.AuditEvent)
member.summary: Assigns , , and on in place. Returns the same instance for fluent chaining.

member.summary: Recomputes the hash chain over the supplied event sequence and returns the first divergence (if any). Pass an to also verify continuity with the persisted anchor.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.IHashChainSigner.Reseal(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Models.AuditEvent})
member.summary: Re-numbers and re-hashes in the supplied order for . Used by the GDPR purge service after deleting events out of the middle of a chain. The signer's in-memory chain state and the configured are updated to the new tail so subsequent calls continue without producing a sequence gap.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.IKeyMaterialProvider
member.summary: Source of the HMAC secret used to seal the audit hash chain. Kept abstract so different hosts can plug in their own secret store: an environment variable on Linux, DPAPI on Windows, the macOS Keychain, Azure Key Vault, etc.
member.remarks: Implementations must never write the secret to disk and must never include it in any log envelope. The default reads from a configurable environment variable so the operator can rotate without redeploying. Tests should use with a fixed string.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.JsonChainAnchorStore
member.summary: File-backed persisting every known chain anchor in a single small JSON file. Atomic writes use the standard tmp + fsync + rename dance so a crash mid-write either keeps the prior file intact or commits the new one — never a half-formed state.
member.remarks: The file is rewritten in full on every ; that is acceptable because the payload is only a few hundred bytes per chain. SQLite-backed and KeyVault-backed implementations can replace this when the anchor count grows large enough to matter (Phase 13).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.SealedLogPolicy
member.summary: Marks a freshly rotated audit file as read-only so accidental in-place edits become visible. On Linux/macOS the policy additionally drops the file's mode to 0440 (owner+group read, no write) for stricter compliance footprints.
member.remarks: Wire to FileRollingSink.Rolled in the DI extension so every closed audit file is sealed automatically. The policy never throws — failure to chmod or set the read-only attribute is swallowed because a sealed-log breach is a configuration concern, not a hot-path concern.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.SealedLogPolicy.OnSealed
member.summary: Optional callback invoked after a successful seal. Useful for Phase 11 self-observability so operators can confirm rotated files were sealed end-to-end.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Integrity.SealedLogPolicy.OnFailed
member.summary: Optional callback invoked when sealing fails. Receives the file path and the surfaced exception. Callers should treat this as a high-priority operator alert.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Purge.ConfirmTokenPurgePolicy
member.summary: Default . Authorizes the purge when the caller supplies the same confirmation token that the operator configured on BeepAuditOptions.PurgeConfirmationToken.
member.remarks: The check uses an ordinal comparison; tokens are intended to be long random strings managed via the same secret store as the HMAC chain key. When no token is configured the policy denies every call so an oblivious caller cannot purge by accident.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Purge.ConfirmTokenPurgePolicy.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a policy keyed by .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Purge.GdprPurgeService
member.summary: GDPR-style purge service. Removes audit events that match a privacy request, then re-seals every affected hash chain so the integrity verifier (Phase 08) keeps reporting a clean chain after the deletion.
member.remarks:
Split into four partials:
.Core — fields, ctor, gating, summary emission.
.User — .
.Entity — .
.ResealChain — chain replay + signer hand-off.
Every successful purge writes a synthetic event to the dedicated "purge" chain so the act of purging is itself auditable and tamper-evident.
member.summary: Entity-scoped purge half of .
Chain re-seal half of . Reads the surviving events from the store, hands them off to the signer for renumbering / re-hashing, then writes them back so the integrity verifier reports a clean chain.
User-scoped purge half of .

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Purge.GdprPurgeService.PurgeChainId
member.summary: Logical chain id used for synthetic purge events.
Creates a purge service over the supplied storage + chain primitives.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Purge.GdprPurgeService.IsAvailable
member.summary: Returns true when at least one purge store is registered.

member.summary: Removes every audit event whose + matches the supplied tuple and re-seals every affected chain.

member.summary: Removes every audit event whose matches and re-seals every affected chain. Writes a synthetic Purge event so the operation is itself auditable.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Purge.IAuditPurgeStore
member.summary: Storage capability that the GDPR purge service consumes (Phase 10). A store must be able to delete events by predicate, enumerate the remaining events of a chain, and re-write the chain fields in place after the signer recomputes them.
member.remarks: Only stores that can guarantee atomic delete + rewrite semantics (SQLite WAL is the v1 reference implementation) should advertise this capability. NDJSON file purge is a Phase 13 follow-up because rewriting a sealed/gzipped audit file requires cooperation with the rotation/seal policy.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Purge.IAuditPurgeStore.Name

member.summary: Deletes every audit row matching the supplied entity / record pair. may be null to purge every record of the entity.

member.summary: Returns every remaining event of in strict Sequence order so the re-seal pass can recompute the chain hashes from the first surviving record onward.

member.summary: Persists the recomputed chain fields (, , ) for every event in . Implementations should run the updates inside a single transaction so a crash either commits the new chain or leaves the prior chain intact.

member.summary: Operator gate for GDPR-style purge calls. The purge service requires an implementation to authorize every removal so a misconfigured caller cannot accidentally erase audit history.
member.remarks: The default matches the supplied confirmation token against the value configured on BeepAuditOptions.PurgeConfirmationToken. Hosts that need stricter governance (Active Directory group, signed Azure AD access token, etc.) implement their own policy and inject it into the DI container.

member.remarks: Performance is bounded by IO; the engine reads files newest-first (mtime descending) and stops as soon as matches accumulate. For production deployments operators are strongly advised to add a alongside the file sink so the SQLite engine handles ad-hoc queries.

member.summary: Storage-agnostic query surface used by (Phase 10) to translate an into a concrete result set. SQLite, file-scan, and composite engines all satisfy this contract; the audit recorder is engine-agnostic.
member.remarks: Engines must be safe for concurrent callers, must honor cancellation, and must respect (0 = unbounded). Implementations should sort the result set according to + so a host can render without an extra LINQ pass.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Query.NdjsonAuditDeserializer
member.summary: Reads a single NDJSON envelope line written by NdjsonSerializer (Phase 03) and reconstructs the embedded . Used by the file-scan query engine and the GDPR purge re-seal path so we can verify and rewrite chain hashes from disk without spinning up a SQLite sink.
member.remarks: The deserializer is strict about field shape but tolerant of missing / unknown fields so older log files (pre-Phase 08, no chain fields) round-trip cleanly with Sequence = 0, Hash = null. Properties are projected to ; numeric / bool / null primitives are kept as-is so equality comparisons in behave the way operators expect.

The sink already implements the contract directly; the wrapper exists so DI composition stays uniform across engines and so a future SQLite query implementation that opens a dedicated read-only connection can swap in without touching call sites.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.BeepServiceAuditExtensions
member.summary: Registration helpers for the Beep audit-trail feature.
member.remarks: Phase 01 wired the contract and a null implementation. Phase 02 constructs the real backed by the shared when is true. The pipeline is registered as a keyed singleton so the DI container owns its lifetime and disposes it on shutdown (which drains the queue and disposes every sink). Audit pipelines never use samplers because audit events are lossless by policy.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.BeepServiceAuditExtensions.AuditPipelineKey

member.returns: The same service collection for fluent chaining.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.BeepServiceBlazorExtensions
member.summary: Blazor WebAssembly registration helpers. They use the IndexedDB sink exclusively because the WASM sandbox cannot write to a real filesystem. The browser quota is shared with the page, so budgets are deliberately small ( / ).
member.remarks: The host project is responsible for registering an implementation (typically a thin wrapper around IJSRuntime.InvokeAsync) before calling these helpers. The helpers will resolve the bridge from the service collection at registration time so the pipeline can build its sink.

member.summary: Registers the Beep audit feature for Blazor WASM. Audit is opt-in on this host because IDB quota is shared with the page; callers must call this method explicitly.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.BeepServiceDesktopExtensions
member.summary: Desktop registration helpers (WinForms / WPF / Console / Avalonia). They lock the v1 storage budgets from , point file sinks at the per-user LocalApplicationData path resolved by , and then defer to / so every downstream wiring (sinks, enrichers, redactors, retention, diagnostics) keeps a single canonical entry point.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.BeepServiceDiagnosticsExtensions
member.summary: Phase 11 diagnostic registration helpers. Builds the , attaches it (plus ) to a , and optionally schedules the periodic metrics snapshot service. Kept in its own file so the logging / audit registration helpers stay focused on their primary concerns.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.BeepServiceDiagnosticsExtensions.AttachDiagnostics(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryPipeline,TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.IBudgetEnforcer)

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.BeepServiceLoggingExtensions
member.summary: Registration helpers for the Beep logging feature.
member.remarks: Phase 01 wired the contract and a null implementation. Phase 02 constructs the real backed by the shared when is true. The pipeline is registered as a keyed singleton so the DI container owns its lifetime and disposes it on shutdown (which drains the queue and disposes every sink).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.BeepServiceLoggingExtensions.LoggingPipelineKey
member.summary: Service key used to disambiguate the logging pipeline from the audit pipeline when both are registered in the same container.

member.summary: Registers in the service collection. When is false (the default) the registration resolves to and no pipeline is created.
member.returns: The same service collection for fluent chaining.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.BeepServiceMauiExtensions
member.summary: .NET MAUI registration helpers (Android / iOS / Mac Catalyst / Windows). Mobile flash storage is constrained, so the v1 budgets from are deliberately modest and rotation is more aggressive than on desktop.
member.remarks:
The library never references Microsoft.Maui.Storage directly so the core stays cross-target. Callers pass a delegate that returns Microsoft.Maui.Storage.FileSystem.AppDataDirectory:
services.AddBeepLoggingForMaui(() => Microsoft.Maui.Storage.FileSystem.AppDataDirectory, "MyApp");

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.BeepServiceMauiExtensions.AddBeepLoggingForMaui(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Func{System.String},System.String,System.Action{TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions})
member.summary: Registers the Beep logging feature with MAUI-friendly defaults backed by .

member.summary: Registers the Beep audit feature with MAUI-friendly defaults.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.BeepServiceRetentionExtensions
member.summary: Registration helpers for the Phase 04 retention sweeper and budget enforcer. These are kept distinct from the logging / audit registrations so non-hosted callers never pay for a background timer they did not ask for.
member.remarks: The enforcer is registered as a singleton so multiple call sites (file sinks, audit producers, the hosted service, ad-hoc admin tools) share the same scope registry. Calling more than once is a no-op after the first registration.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.BeepServiceRetentionExtensions.AddBeepBudgetEnforcer(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary: Registers as a singleton () without scheduling the hosted sweeper. Use when callers want to enforce on demand (e.g. in unit tests, on app shutdown, or from a CLI).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.BeepServiceRetentionExtensions.AddBeepRetentionSweeper(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})
member.summary: Registers and schedules on the host's IHostedService pipeline.
member.param:
Cadence between sweeps. Defaults to
when omitted.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.BeepServiceWebExtensions
member.summary: ASP.NET Core / server-side registration helpers. They lock the v1 web budgets from , point file sinks at {contentRoot}/logs (or an explicit path the caller supplies for read-only container deploys), and use a larger queue capacity than the desktop preset because web hosts typically run higher-throughput pipelines.
member.remarks:
The library deliberately does not reference Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting so it builds against pure netN TFMs. Callers that have an IWebHostEnvironment pass env.ContentRootPath explicitly:
services.AddBeepLoggingForWeb(builder.Environment.ContentRootPath, "MyApp");

member.summary: Registers the Beep logging feature with web-friendly defaults.

App name for the per-user fallback path.
