member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Examples.AuditDistributedExample
member.summary: End-to-end sample showing how a distributed-tier subsystem (resharding planner, transaction coordinator, plan-change service) wires AddBeepAuditForDesktop and forwards events through . The bridge keeps the call sites strongly typed so they do not have to build instances by hand.
member.remarks:
Demonstrates:
DI registration with a stricter audit chain segment.
Three distinct distributed event flows (reshard, tx, plan-change) recorded under category .
An explicit failure path that emits with a reason.
Final flush + integrity verify.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Examples.AuditDistributedExample.SampleAppName
member.summary: Suggested entry-point name for the sample.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Examples.AuditDistributedExample.RunAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Builds a configured , runs the distributed workload, and verifies chain integrity at the end of the session.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Examples.AuditDistributedExample.ExecuteAsync(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.Bridges.DistributedAuditBridge,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Drives a representative distributed workload through the bridge: a successful shard split, a successful global commit, a successful plan change, and finally a failed shard split that surfaces with a reason.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Examples.AuditFormsExample
member.summary: End-to-end sample showing how a FormsManager-style host registers AddBeepAuditForDesktop and emits per-record audit events through the unified pipeline. Mirrors the behaviour the FormsAuditBridge uses to forward legacy AuditEntry instances.
member.remarks:
Demonstrates:
DI registration with desktop audit defaults.
Hash-chain enabled (default for desktop preset).
Insert / Update / Delete events with field-level change-sets keyed to a record.
End-of-session flush followed by integrity verification.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Examples.AuditFormsExample.SampleAppName
member.summary: Suggested entry-point name for the sample.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Examples.AuditFormsExample.RunAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Builds a configured , runs the representative workload, flushes the pipeline, and verifies the chain integrity before disposing the host.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Examples.AuditFormsExample.ExecuteAsync(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Audit.IBeepAudit,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Emits a typical Forms session: an Insert into the HR_EMP block, then an Update on the same record, finally a Delete. Each event carries a field-change set so verifiers can reconstruct the record state over time.

Demonstrates the four elements every desktop sample app should show:
DI registration with platform defaults.
A redaction preset attached to the options.
A correlation scope wrapping a unit of work.
A periodic flush followed by clean shutdown.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLog
member.summary: Production backed by the shared . Created by AddBeepLogging when is true. When disabled, callers receive instead.
member.remarks:
The class is split across three partial files:
.Core — fields, ctor, entry point.
.Levels — Trace/Debug/Info/Warn/Error/Critical wrappers.
.Lifetime — flush / dispose semantics.
member.summary: Convenience-level wrappers for . Each method short-circuits when is false or the configured minimum level filters the call out.
Lifetime / shutdown half of . Delegates to the shared so logs and audit events drain together.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLog.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions,TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryPipeline)
member.summary: Creates a new logger bound to the supplied pipeline.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions
member.summary: Operator-facing configuration for the Beep logging feature. All disk-bound options carry a hard cap so the feature is safe to ship in storage-constrained environments.
member.remarks: Phase 01 consumed only the toggle and budget fields. Phase 02 introduced the typed sink / redactor / enricher / sampler collections that the shared telemetry pipeline reads on construction. Phase 04 adds the per-directory , , and POCOs consumed by the and the optional .

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.Enabled
member.summary: When false (the default) the registration extension binds the null logger so the feature has zero runtime cost.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.MinLevel
member.summary: Minimum severity processed by the pipeline.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.QueueCapacity
member.summary: Maximum envelopes that may sit in the bounded queue.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.BackpressureMode
member.summary: Behavior when the queue is full. Logs default to so a noisy code path never blocks the producer.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.FlushInterval
member.summary: How often the batch writer drains the queue.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.ShutdownTimeout
member.summary: How long FlushAsync waits during clean shutdown.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.StorageBudgetBytes
member.summary: Hard cap on the total bytes the logging feature may consume on disk. Honored by the budget enforcer (Phase 04). Set to 0 to disable the cap (not recommended on mobile or browser hosts).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.Sinks
member.summary: Pluggable telemetry sinks. Populated by extension methods.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.Redactors
member.summary: Pluggable redactors. Populated by extension methods.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.Enrichers
member.summary: Pluggable enrichers. Populated by extension methods.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.Samplers
member.summary: Pluggable samplers. Populated by extension methods.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.Deduper
member.summary: Optional message deduper. null disables the dedup stage so every envelope continues straight to the rate limiter / queue.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.RateLimiter
member.summary: Optional rate limiter. null disables the rate-limit stage so every envelope continues straight to the queue.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.Rotation
member.summary: Rotation policy applied to file-based sinks.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.Retention
member.summary: Retention policy applied by the budget sweeper.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.Budget
member.summary: Storage budget applied by the sweeper. Defaults to because logs are disposable.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.EnableRetentionSweeper
member.summary: When true, the registration helper schedules on the host's IHostedService pipeline. Off by default so non-hosted callers (console tools, ad-hoc tests) do not pay for a timer they do not need.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.SweepInterval
member.summary: Cadence of the retention sweeper when enabled.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.ReplaceDMLogger
member.summary: When true (the default once the feature is enabled) the registration helper replaces any existing IDMLogger registration with a bridge that forwards every legacy WriteLog/LogError/LogWarning call into the unified pipeline. Set to false to keep the original DMLogger alongside .

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.RegisterMicrosoftLoggerProvider
member.summary:
When true the registration helper installs MicrosoftLoggerProvider as an
so frameworks already using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging (ASP.NET Core, EF, gRPC, hosted services) flow into the unified pipeline without duplicate plumbing.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.EnableMetricsSnapshot
member.summary: When true, the registration helper schedules on the host's IHostedService pipeline. Off by default so non-hosted callers (console tools, ad-hoc tests) do not pay for a timer.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.MetricsSnapshotInterval
member.summary: Cadence used by the optional metrics snapshot service.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.MetricsSnapshotFile
member.summary: When set, periodic snapshots are written to this file path (rendered as ). Leave null to disable file output.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.MetricsSnapshotFormat
member.summary: Format used when writing snapshots to disk.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLoggingOptions.EmitMetricsSnapshotAsSelfEvent
member.summary: When true, periodic snapshots are also emitted as BeepTelemetry.Self.Snapshot events through the pipeline.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLogScopeExtensions
member.summary: Convenience extension methods that let callers open an ambient directly off an reference. The log instance itself is not stored on the scope — the scope is purely an async-local frame — so disposing the returned handle never affects the log.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLogScopeExtensions.Scope(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.IBeepLog,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: Opens a with the supplied name and optional tags. Returns a no-op handle when the log is null, disabled, or the name is empty so callers can guard with using unconditionally.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.Bridges.DMLoggerToBeepLogBridge
member.summary: Adapts the legacy surface onto an instance so existing call sites (lg.WriteLog(...), lg.LogError(...)) flow through the unified telemetry pipeline without code changes.
member.remarks: Phase 09 wires this bridge in place of the original TheTechIdea.Beep.Logger.DMLogger registration when and are both true. When the unified logger is disabled the original DMLogger is left in place so the baseline behavior is unchanged. The bridge raises for every routed message so existing UI subscribers (status bars, toast hosts) continue to work.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.Bridges.DMLoggerToBeepLogBridge.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.IBeepLog)
member.summary: Creates a new bridge that forwards to the supplied beep logger.
member.param: Target beep logger; must not be null.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.Bridges.DMLoggerToBeepLogBridge.RaisePropertyChanged(System.String)
member.summary: Reserved for symmetry with the legacy logger which exposed ; the bridge has no observable state so the helper simply raises the event for the named property when invoked by a future caller.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.Bridges.MicrosoftLoggerAdapter
member.summary: Per-category MEL adapter that forwards every call to . Maps the MEL log level to , materialises the formatted message once, and lifts MEL state into the property bag when it implements of .
member.remarks: opens a so MEL scopes (HTTP request id, EF command id, etc.) automatically participate in the Beep correlation/trace ids that downstream enrichers stamp onto every envelope.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.Bridges.MicrosoftLoggerProvider
member.summary: that funnels every call into the supplied . Lets ASP.NET Core, EF Core, gRPC, hosted services, and any other framework using MEL flow into the Beep telemetry pipeline without duplicate plumbing.
member.remarks: Each unique category string passed to is cached; the wrapper itself is cheap (one per category) so the provider scales to the typical few-hundred-category footprint of a large application. Disposal is a no-op because the underlying pipeline is owned by the DI container, not by the provider.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.Bridges.MicrosoftLoggerProvider.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.IBeepLog)
member.summary: Creates a provider that forwards to the supplied beep logger.
member.param: Target beep logger; must not be null.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.Bridges.MicrosoftLoggerScope
member.summary: Bridges onto so MEL scopes (HTTP request id, EF command id, ASP.NET Core {RequestPath}) automatically flow into the Beep correlation/trace stack.
member.remarks: Returned handles always succeed and never throw; if the scope state is empty or unrecognised a no-op handle is returned. The scope name defaults to "mel.scope" when no better name can be derived from the state.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.NullBeepLog
member.summary: Default used when the logging feature is disabled or before Phase 02 ships the production pipeline. Every method is a fast no-op so callers never have to null-check the dependency.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BackpressureMode
member.summary: Behavior selected by the telemetry pipeline when its bounded queue is full. Logs default to ; audit defaults to because audit events are lossless by policy.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BackpressureMode.DropOldest
member.summary: New writes evict the oldest queued envelope. The dropped count is surfaced through PipelineMetrics. Suited for lossy logs.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BackpressureMode.Block
member.summary: New writes block until the queue has free capacity. Suited for audit and other lossless flows. The pipeline may escalate to if the block exceeds shutdown timeout.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BackpressureMode.FailFast
member.summary: New writes throw immediately when the queue is full. The producer decides how to recover (typically by aborting the user action).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BatchWriter
member.summary: Long-running drain loop owned by . Reads envelopes off in batches and fans them out to every registered .
member.remarks: One instance per pipeline. The drain loop runs on a dedicated so producers never block on sink IO. Per-sink exceptions are caught here so a single failing sink can never starve the others or crash the pipeline.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BatchWriter.SinkErrorCount
member.summary: Total per-sink exceptions caught since startup.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BatchWriter.FlushAsync(System.TimeSpan,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Drains any remaining envelopes and asks every sink to flush. Bounded by the supplied .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BoundedChannelQueue
member.summary: Cross-platform bounded queue used as the producer / consumer hand-off inside . Wraps with a selectable and a drop counter so the pipeline can surface overflow through metrics (Phase 11).
member.remarks: One queue is created per . Logs and audit envelopes share the same queue so they retain producer order. The underlying channel is configured with SingleReader = true to allow the runtime to pick the fast path; multiple producers are supported.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BoundedChannelQueue.Reader
member.summary: Reader exposed to the .

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BoundedChannelQueue.Capacity
member.summary: Configured capacity (used by metrics).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BoundedChannelQueue.Mode
member.summary: Configured backpressure mode.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BoundedChannelQueue.DroppedCount
member.summary: Total envelopes dropped due to backpressure since startup.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BoundedChannelQueue.CurrentDepth
member.summary: Best-effort current depth. The underlying channel exposes on .NET 6+; we treat it as a hint only because consumers and producers may race the read.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BoundedChannelQueue.TryEnqueue(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryEnvelope)
member.summary: Synchronous, non-blocking enqueue used by the logging hot path. Returns false when the envelope was dropped (mode-dependent) or could not be written.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BoundedChannelQueue.EnqueueAsync(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryEnvelope,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Async enqueue used by the audit producer. Honors by awaiting capacity and honors by throwing.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.BoundedChannelQueue.Complete
member.summary: Marks the channel complete so the drain loop can exit after consuming the remaining items.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.ActivityScopeEnricher
member.summary: Surfaces the current on each envelope: scope name, start timestamp and any operator-supplied scope tags. Tags are written under the scope.<tagKey> namespace to keep them visually distinct from producer-supplied properties.
member.remarks: This enricher is a no-op when no scope is active. It does not touch trace/correlation ids — that is 's job — so the two enrichers compose without writing the same key twice.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.BeepActivity
member.summary: Lightweight activity record pushed onto . Mirrors the W3C trace context shape so we can hand off to a real (and any OTel exporter) later without translating identifiers.
member.remarks: Identifiers are stored as lowercase hex strings — 32 chars for and 16 chars for / — to match the W3C TraceContext format. Tags are an optional bag of scope-local properties surfaced by .

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.BeepActivity.Name
member.summary: Caller-supplied scope name (e.g. "Order.Submit").

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.BeepActivity.TraceId
member.summary: Lowercase hex 16-byte trace id (W3C TraceContext).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.BeepActivity.SpanId
member.summary: Lowercase hex 8-byte span id (W3C TraceContext).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.BeepActivity.ParentSpanId
member.summary: Span id of the parent activity, when nested.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.BeepActivity.StartUtc
member.summary: UTC start timestamp captured when the scope opened.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.BeepActivity.Tags
member.summary: Optional scope-local tags surfaced by enrichers.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.BeepActivityScope
member.summary: Async-local stack backing for . Kept in its own partial so the public surface is not littered with stack-management plumbing.
member.remarks: We store a reference per async-local slot rather than pushing/popping a primitive value directly because only flows the reference itself — mutating the stack does not trigger callbacks but does propagate visibly to peers on the same logical context. Pop logic tolerates mismatched/out-of-order disposal so a faulty caller can never corrupt the stack for the rest of the host.
member.summary: Ambient scope stack used by enrichers to attach trace/correlation ids to every produced inside a using (BeepActivityScope.Begin(...)) block. Backed by an stack so the context flows across await boundaries.
member.remarks: When exists at time the new scope inherits its and treats the activity's as its parent. This keeps Beep telemetry correlated with any framework already producing OTel spans (ASP.NET Core, gRPC, HttpClient, etc.).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.BeepActivityScope.ResetCurrentForTests
member.summary: Test/diagnostic helper that resets the current async-local stack. Production code never calls this.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.BeepActivityScope.Begin(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: Opens a new scope. Dispose the returned handle to pop the scope. Returns a no-op handle when is null/empty so callers can safely guard with using regardless of whether the operation actually deserves a scope.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.BeepActivityScope.Current
member.summary: Returns the current activity, or null if no scope is open on this async-local context.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.BeepActivityScope.IsEmpty
member.summary: True when no scope is currently open on this context.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.CorrelationEnricher
member.summary: Stamps every envelope with a correlationId. Reuses the producer- supplied when present, then the current trace id, and only generates a fresh GUID-N when no scope or upstream id exists.
member.remarks: Always writes the chosen value back to so downstream enrichers and sinks see a single canonical field, and mirrors it into for sinks that flatten the property bag (e.g. the NDJSON file sink).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.EnrichmentProperties
member.summary: Single source of truth for the property keys written by built-in enrichers. Centralizing the string constants prevents typo-driven drift between enrichers and downstream sink schemas / queries.
member.remarks: Keys are camelCase for OTel compatibility — most exporters already expect this casing for the trace/correlation triplet.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.EnvironmentEnricher
member.summary: Stamps environment metadata onto every envelope: deployment name (e.g. "prod" / "staging"), region, application version and Beep app-repo name. Values are captured at construction time so the hot path stays allocation-free.
member.remarks: All four fields are optional. Empty strings are skipped to avoid littering the property bag with blank entries when a deployment does not set them.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.EnvironmentEnricher.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates an environment enricher with the supplied static metadata.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.IdGenerators
member.summary: W3C TraceContext-compatible id generators. Trace ids are 16 random bytes encoded as 32 lowercase hex characters; span ids are 8 random bytes encoded as 16 lowercase hex characters. Both are non-zero by construction so they round-trip cleanly through OTel exporters that reject the all-zero sentinel.
member.remarks: Uses rather than because GUIDs do not match the W3C byte length and version bits would have to be stripped anyway.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.IdGenerators.NewTraceId
member.summary: Returns a fresh 32-char lowercase hex trace id.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.IdGenerators.NewSpanId
member.summary: Returns a fresh 16-char lowercase hex span id.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.IdGenerators.NewCorrelationId
member.summary: Returns a fresh GUID-N (32 hex chars, no dashes). Used as a human-friendly correlation id when no scope is active.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.MachineProcessEnricher
member.summary: Stamps machine, process and thread identity onto every envelope. Reads and the current process metadata once at construction time so the hot path is just dictionary writes — no call per event.
member.remarks: Cached values intentionally do not refresh; in long-lived hosts the machine name and process id are immutable for the lifetime of the process. Thread id is captured per call because telemetry crosses thread-pool worker boundaries freely.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.MachineProcessEnricher.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a machine/process enricher with cached identity.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.TenantEnricher
member.summary: Stamps a tenant identifier onto every envelope. The value is resolved through a caller-supplied delegate so the enricher itself has no dependency on ; the Phase 09 bridges wire concrete resolvers (e.g. one that reads BeepService.AppRepoName).
member.remarks: The resolver is invoked on the producer thread and must therefore be cheap and side-effect free. Throwing resolvers are swallowed; the enricher never fails the envelope.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.TenantEnricher.#ctor(System.Func{System.String})
member.summary: Creates a tenant enricher with the supplied resolver.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.TraceEnricher
member.summary:
Stamps trace context (traceId, spanId, parentSpanId) onto every envelope. Source preference order:
The producer-supplied .
when an OTel-compatible ambient activity exists (covers ASP.NET Core, gRPC, HttpClient, EF, etc.).
.
When all three are empty the enricher is a no-op so envelopes outside any traced operation are not tagged with synthetic ids.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.UserEnricher
member.summary: Stamps userId and userName onto every envelope using caller-supplied resolvers. Either resolver may be omitted; the enricher writes only the fields it has values for.
member.remarks: Resolvers run on the producer thread. To stay cross-platform the enricher does not touch or any other OS-specific principal — those bindings live in the Phase 09 bridges.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Context.UserEnricher.#ctor(System.Func{System.String},System.Func{System.String})
member.summary: Creates a user enricher with optional id and name resolvers.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.IMessageDeduper
member.summary: Pre-queue stage that collapses near-identical log envelopes into a single representative envelope plus a periodic summary. Audit envelopes always bypass this stage by pipeline contract.
member.remarks: The pipeline supplies an emitter delegate via ; the deduper invokes it whenever a window expires and a synthetic summary needs to enter the queue. This keeps the deduper free of any pipeline coupling — making it trivially testable.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.IMessageDeduper.Name
member.summary: Stable name for diagnostics.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.IMessageDeduper.SuppressedCount
member.summary: Total envelopes suppressed since startup.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.IMessageDeduper.Bind(System.Action{TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryEnvelope})
member.summary: Wires the synthetic-summary emitter. Called once by the pipeline at construction. Implementations may store the delegate or wrap it in a no-op when null is supplied.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.IMessageDeduper.TryAccept(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryEnvelope)
member.summary: Returns true when the envelope should continue downstream or false when it has been folded into an existing window. May synchronously call the bound emitter to flush expired summaries before deciding on the current envelope.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.IMessageDeduper.DrainExpired
member.summary: Forces emission of every expired window summary. Called by the pipeline drain loop and on shutdown so no suppressed envelope is silently lost.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.MessageTemplateNormalizer
member.summary: Reduces a free-form log message to a stable template by replacing the parts that vary per call (numbers, GUIDs, quoted literals) with placeholder tokens. Two messages whose only difference is variable content normalize to the same template, which is what uses as its identity key.
member.remarks: Single-pass over the input string with no allocations beyond one per call. Designed for the producer hot path: roughly two pointer compares and a copy per character.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.MessageTemplateNormalizer.Normalize(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the normalized template, or the original on null/empty.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.WindowedDeduper
member.summary: Bounded, time-windowed message deduper. Collapses identical (level, category, normalized-template) envelopes within a configurable window into a single forwarded envelope plus a post-window summary carrying the suppressed count.
member.remarks: The class is split across two partial files: .Core exposes the public surface and accepts envelopes; .Window owns eviction and summary emission.
member.summary: Eviction and summary-emission half of . Holds the per-key tracking record and the helpers that walk the LRU list when entries expire or when the key cap is hit.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.WindowedDeduper.DefaultWindow
member.summary: Default window when none is supplied.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.WindowedDeduper.DefaultMaxKeys
member.summary: Default maximum tracked keys when none is supplied.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.WindowedDeduper.DedupCountProperty
member.summary: Property key carrying the number of suppressed envelopes in a summary.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.WindowedDeduper.DedupTemplateProperty
member.summary: Property key carrying the original normalized template.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.WindowedDeduper.#ctor(System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Func{System.DateTime})
member.summary: Creates a deduper with the supplied window and key cap.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.WindowedDeduper.Window
member.summary: Configured window length.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Dedup.WindowedDeduper.MaxKeys
member.summary: Configured maximum tracked keys.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Diagnostics.HealthAggregator
member.summary: Rolls a list of sinks up into a single health verdict plus a per-sink breakdown. Used by when composing a snapshot and by when writing the periodic file.
member.remarks: The aggregator is allocation-free for the common path of zero sinks; for non-empty inputs it returns a tiny POCO list. It does not cache results — the caller is expected to throttle invocation (the snapshot writer already runs on its own cadence).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Diagnostics.HealthAggregator.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.ITelemetrySink})

member.summary: True when every sink reports itself healthy.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Diagnostics.HealthAggregator.Probe
member.summary: Returns one entry per registered sink.

member.summary: Optional richer health surface for sinks that track their own success / error timestamps and counters. Sinks that only expose the bare flag are still supported: falls back to for them.

member.summary: Frozen point-in-time snapshot returned by . The shape is intentionally flat and JSON-friendly so the can serialize it without bespoke conversions.
member.remarks: Counters are monotonic since startup; gauges are point-in-time. Drop counters are split by stage (sampler / dedup / rate-limit / queue-full / sink-error) so operators can see which stage is shedding load.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Diagnostics.PerfHarness
member.summary: Throughput / drop / latency probe for the . Fires envelopes for and reports queue depth, drop counts, and end-to-end latency observed at a downstream .
member.remarks:
Designed for two scenarios:
CI smoke: assert that documented per-platform floor numbers are met on the build agent.
Local triage: drop into a console app to characterize a given sink set on the operator's hardware.
The harness intentionally avoids any third-party benchmarking library so it can run inside the engine assembly itself.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Diagnostics.PerfHarness.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryPipeline,TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.MemorySink)
member.summary: Creates a harness that submits envelopes through and observes them via . The observer must be one of the pipeline's sinks; the harness uses it to compute end-to-end latency.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Diagnostics.PerfHarness.TargetEventsPerSec
member.summary: Target submission rate (envelopes per second).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Diagnostics.PerfHarness.Duration
member.summary: Total run duration.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Diagnostics.PerfHarness.ProducerCount
member.summary: Number of producer tasks that share the load.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Diagnostics.PerfHarness.Category
member.summary: Logical category attached to every emitted envelope.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Diagnostics.PeriodicMetricsSnapshotHostedService
member.summary: Opt-in that periodically captures a and either emits it as a self-event, writes it to a single rolling file, or both. The service is kept off by default so production hosts pay nothing until an operator opts in to long-running observability.
member.remarks:
Notes:
The service ticks on a single timer; an in-flight snapshot is never re-entered.
Disk writes go through a temp+rename so a crash mid-write never produces a half-written snapshot.
Self-event emission is rate-controlled by so flooding cannot occur even with a 1-second interval.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Diagnostics.PipelineDiagnosticsHooks
member.summary:
Subscribes + to existing pipeline events:
→ sweeper / budget metrics + self event.
→ sink-error metric + self event.
Hooks are detachable via the returned so the pipeline can clean up during shutdown without leaving dangling subscriptions on the singleton enforcer.
Creates a new hook bag and immediately attaches every subscription.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Diagnostics.PipelineMetrics
member.summary: Self-observability counters and gauges owned by a single instance. Designed to be free of external dependencies so it can be constructed eagerly during pipeline initialization and read concurrently from any thread.
member.remarks:
Split into four partial files:
.Core — fields, constructor, public name, dispose-state.
.Counters — Increment* helpers using .
.Gauges — point-in-time getters and setters.
.Snapshot — frozen assembly.
One instance per pipeline so logging and audit metrics never collide. The reference is supplied at construction so the snapshot can include per-sink health without re-walking the sink list every call.
member.summary: Monotonic counter increments for . Every method is allocation-free and safe to call from the hot path; readers see eventually-consistent values via the matching *.Read properties.
Point-in-time getters and write-ables for . Distinguished from counters because gauges are not monotonic — the queue depth oscillates and the flush-latency value reflects the most recent batch.
Snapshot assembly for . The snapshot is computed under no lock — every value is read via or via a provider, so callers see an eventually-consistent view that never tears.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Diagnostics.SelfEventCategory
member.summary: Reserved category names used by the self-observability stage so downstream filters can isolate diagnostic noise from application telemetry.
member.remarks: All self events flow through the same TelemetryPipeline as regular logs, but they bypass the deduper / rate-limiter / sampler stages because the already enforces its own per-(eventType, key) dedupe window. Operators can route these events to a separate sink (or filter them out entirely) by matching on .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Diagnostics.SelfEventEmitter
member.summary: Single chokepoint for emitting self-observability envelopes (drop spike, sink unhealthy, budget breach, chain divergence, …). Enforces a per-(category, key) dedup window so a failure that fires once per millisecond cannot itself flood the pipeline.
member.remarks: The emitter writes envelopes through an injected delegate so it stays decoupled from internals; the pipeline binds the delegate to its synthetic-enqueue path so self events bypass the deduper / rate-limiter / sampler stages (those have already had a chance to run on the originating envelopes — running them again would double-account).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Diagnostics.SelfEventEmitter.DefaultWindow
member.summary: Default dedup window (1 minute) per (category, key).

Creates a new emitter. is invoked for every accepted self event (after the dedup window has cleared); is an optional operator hook that fires for every event the emitter accepts (regardless of where it routes it).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.IEnricher
member.summary: Pipeline stage that augments each with cross-cutting context (correlation ids, machine name, user id, activity span info, etc.). Forward-declared in Phase 02; concrete enrichers ship in Phase 06.
member.remarks: Enrichers run on the producer thread (before enqueue) so they must be cheap, allocation-light, and never block on IO. Implementations mutate the supplied envelope in place rather than returning a copy.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.IEnricher.Name
member.summary: Stable enricher name for diagnostics and ordering.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.IEnricher.Enrich(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryEnvelope)
member.summary: Augments the envelope. Must not throw on null envelope.Properties — initialize when needed.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.IRedactor
member.summary: Pipeline stage that removes or masks sensitive content (PII, secrets, connection strings) from each . Forward-declared in Phase 02; concrete redactors ship in Phase 05.
member.remarks: Redactors run after enrichers and before sampling/enqueue so all downstream stages and sinks see redacted content only. Implementations must edit the envelope in place to keep the hot path allocation-free.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.IRedactor.Name
member.summary: Stable redactor name for diagnostics and ordering.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.IRedactor.Redact(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryEnvelope)
member.summary: Redacts sensitive data from the supplied envelope.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.ISampler
member.summary: Pipeline stage that decides whether a log envelope is forwarded or dropped. Audit envelopes always bypass sampling. Forward-declared in Phase 02; concrete samplers ship in Phase 07.
member.remarks: Samplers run after enrichment and redaction but before enqueue so a dropped envelope never consumes queue capacity or sink work. Returning false drops the envelope; returning true keeps it.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.ISampler.Name
member.summary: Stable sampler name for diagnostics and ordering.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.ISampler.ShouldSample(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryEnvelope)
member.summary: Returns true when the envelope should continue through the pipeline. Implementations must be deterministic across the same envelope to keep sampling reproducible.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.ITelemetrySink
member.summary: Output stage for the unified telemetry pipeline. A sink receives a pre-batched, fully-enriched, fully-redacted slice of envelopes and is responsible for persisting or forwarding them.
member.remarks: Implementations must be safe to call from a single dedicated worker thread (the pipeline drains serially per sink) and must surface failure through so the pipeline can react. Per-call exceptions are caught by the pipeline; sinks should not rely on the pipeline to retry.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.ITelemetrySink.Name
member.summary: Stable, human-readable sink identifier (e.g. file, sqlite).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.ITelemetrySink.IsHealthy
member.summary: Snapshot of sink health. The pipeline reads this between batches and may emit a self-observability event (Phase 11) when it flips from true to false.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.ITelemetrySink.WriteBatchAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryEnvelope},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Persists or forwards a batch of envelopes. Implementations may throw; the pipeline isolates the failure to this sink.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.ITelemetrySink.FlushAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Flushes any sink-side buffer (e.g. fsync the underlying writer). Called from FlushAsync on the pipeline.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.NdjsonSerializer
member.summary: UTF-8 newline-delimited-JSON writer used by file and SQLite sinks to render instances. Built on so it works on every supported .NET target without per-OS branches.
member.remarks: Exception payloads are flattened to a small property bag (type, message, stack) rather than reflected through the full type tree. This keeps output compact, avoids reentrancy on logger frames, and sidesteps serializer cycles on common framework exceptions.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.NdjsonSerializer.SerializeLine(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryEnvelope)
member.summary: Serializes a single envelope to UTF-8 bytes terminated with \n. Returns an array sized exactly to the produced payload to keep the file writer allocation pattern predictable.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.NdjsonSerializer.SerializeText(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryEnvelope)
member.summary: Serializes a single envelope as a UTF-8 string (no trailing newline). Useful for sinks that store one envelope per row (SQLite, IndexedDB) instead of per file line.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.PlatformPaths
member.summary: Single source of truth for "where can we safely write on this OS / host?". Returns directories using only portable BCL APIs so the same code works on Windows, Linux, macOS, MAUI, and (with the Phase 12 override) Blazor.
member.remarks: Blazor WebAssembly cannot write to a real filesystem; in that target Phase 12 swaps the active provider for an IndexedDB-backed shim and the path returned here becomes a virtual identifier consumed by the JS interop sink. All other targets receive a real, creatable path.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.PlatformPaths.DefaultAppName
member.summary: Default app folder name when callers do not pass one.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.PlatformPaths.AppDataRoot(System.String)
member.summary:
Per-user, per-app local data root. Uses
with so the directory is created on demand by the BCL.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.PlatformPaths.LogsDir(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Logs directory for the supplied app name. Defaults to {AppDataRoot}/logs. Created on demand.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.PlatformPaths.AuditDir(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Audit directory for the supplied app name. Defaults to {AppDataRoot}/audit. Created on demand.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.PlatformPaths.SanitizeFileName(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns a sanitized file-name fragment from safe to use across every supported filesystem. Replaces any invalid character with _.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Presets.PlatformBudgets
member.summary: Locked v1 default storage budgets per host platform. Centralized here so the Phase 12 registration helpers all pick from a single source of truth and operators have one place to look up the recommended caps.
member.remarks: Logs and audit have separate caps because audit is lossless by policy and typically needs a longer retention window. The Blazor targets are deliberately small because IndexedDB quota is shared with the page; the MAUI targets are small because mobile storage is constrained and apps that exceed user-visible caps may be killed by the OS.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.RateLimit.IRateLimiter
member.summary: Pre-queue stage that enforces a per-key throughput cap on log envelopes. Audit envelopes always bypass this stage by pipeline contract.
member.remarks: The rate limiter is the final pre-queue gate. Implementations are expected to be thread-safe and to update internal counters under a lock no broader than per-key.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.RateLimit.IRateLimiter.Name
member.summary: Stable name for diagnostics.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.RateLimit.IRateLimiter.DroppedCount
member.summary: Total envelopes rejected since startup.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.RateLimit.IRateLimiter.Bind(System.Action{TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryEnvelope})
member.summary: Wires the synthetic-summary emitter so the limiter can publish periodic "[rate-limited]" envelopes describing recent drops. Called once by the pipeline at construction.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.RateLimit.IRateLimiter.TryAcquire(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryEnvelope)
member.summary: Returns true when the envelope fits inside the bucket for its key (and consumes one token); returns false when the bucket is empty.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.RateLimit.IRateLimiter.DrainSummaries
member.summary: Forces emission of any pending rate-limit summary. Called by the pipeline drain loop and on shutdown.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.RateLimit.TokenBucketRateLimiter
member.summary: Per-key token-bucket rate limiter. Refills at , capped at ; once the bucket for a key empties, subsequent envelopes are rejected until enough time has passed for a fresh token.
member.remarks: Tokens are tracked as so the refill math stays exact for sub-second arrivals. A periodic summary envelope is emitted (default every 30s) describing the per-key drops accrued since the previous summary, so operators can see "category X has been throttled" without having to parse every dropped event.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.RateLimit.TokenBucketRateLimiter.DefaultSummaryInterval
member.summary: Default summary cadence if none is supplied.

member.summary: Property key carrying the per-summary drop count.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.RateLimit.TokenBucketRateLimiter.DropKeyProperty
member.summary: Property key carrying the rate-limit key (e.g. category).

member.summary: Creates a token-bucket limiter.
member.param: Sustained rate per second per key (positive).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.RateLimit.TokenBucketRateLimiter.RefillPerSecond
member.summary: Configured refill rate per second per key.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.RateLimit.TokenBucketRateLimiter.Burst
member.summary: Configured burst capacity per key.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.CompositeRedactor
member.summary: Bundles several s into a single ordered chain. Lets operators pre-build a named preset (e.g. "AuditStrict") and pass it in any place a single redactor is expected.
member.remarks: Inner redactors run in registration order. Exceptions thrown by one redactor never short-circuit the chain — they are swallowed so a faulty plugin cannot disable the entire scrubbing layer. The pipeline-level guard in still wraps each call, so the only state that crosses the boundary is the envelope itself.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.CompositeRedactor.#ctor(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.IRedactor})
member.summary: Creates a composite redactor with the supplied chain.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.CompositeRedactor.Inner
member.summary: Inner redactors, in execution order.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.ConnectionStringRedactor
member.summary: Scrubs the credential keys most commonly leaked in connection strings: password, pwd, account_key, secret, shared_access_key, shared_access_signature, apikey, api_key, token. Compatible with both ADO.NET-style connection strings and URI query strings.
member.remarks: Mirrors and extends the legacy redactor pattern so callers can switch to the unified pipeline without changing observed behavior.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.ConnectionStringRedactor.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext)
member.summary: Creates a connection-string redactor. Defaults to ; switch to in audit so analysts can detect credential reuse without recovering the value.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.CreditCardRedactor
member.summary: Detects 13–19 digit runs (with optional spaces or dashes) that pass the Luhn check and replaces them per the configured . The Luhn pre-filter keeps random ID strings of similar length from being mis-flagged.
member.remarks: Whitespace and dashes are stripped before validation, but the surrounding non-digit characters are preserved in the replacement boundary so log readers still see where the value sat in the message.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.CreditCardRedactor.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext)
member.summary: Creates a Luhn-checked credit card redactor.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.DefaultRedactionPresets
member.summary: Curated bundles for the two most common deployments. Operators can drop a preset directly into BeepLoggingOptions.Redactors or BeepAuditOptions.Redactors instead of hand-wiring individual redactors.
member.remarks: Presets are factories rather than singletons because each one binds a specific (mode + salt). Callers who want the same preset with a custom salt should pass it in.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.DefaultRedactionPresets.Off
member.summary: Empty preset. Useful as an explicit "I deliberately disabled redaction" marker.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.DefaultRedactionPresets.LogsBalanced(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext)
member.summary: Recommended preset for application logs: connection strings, JWTs, and the password/token/secret keyword family, all in mode for readability.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.DefaultRedactionPresets.AuditStrict(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext)
member.summary: Strict preset for audit: extends with PII detectors (credit card, email) and a structured-field redactor for common PII property keys, hashed so analysts can still join records by stable digest.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.EmailRedactor
member.summary: Redacts RFC-5322-ish email addresses. Designed to favour false negatives over false positives so legitimate technical strings (file paths, urls without user-info) are left intact.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.EmailRedactor.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext)
member.summary: Creates an email redactor.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.JwtRedactor
member.summary: Detects standard three-segment JWTs (eyJ...........) and redacts the entire token. Catches the dominant Bearer-token shape without relying on header keys, so it works regardless of whether the token was logged behind Authorization:, token=, or embedded inline.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.JwtRedactor.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext)
member.summary: Creates a JWT redactor.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.KeyValueRedactor
member.summary: Redacts key=value pairs whose key matches an allowlist of sensitive names. Operates on the message body; structured properties are handled by .
member.remarks: Recognises both unquoted (token=abc) and quoted (token='abc def', token="abc def") values. Stops at the next whitespace, semicolon or comma when unquoted.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.KeyValueRedactor.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a key=value redactor for the supplied sensitive keys. Empty/null keys are filtered out.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.KeywordRedactor
member.summary: Redacts whole-word matches against a fixed keyword list. Useful for fixed secret tokens, internal product code names, or known passwords that should never appear in logs.
member.remarks: Matching is case-insensitive and word-boundary anchored to avoid over-matching (e.g. "sun" will not redact "sunday"). Patterns are compiled once and combined into a single alternation so runtime cost is roughly one regex per envelope regardless of keyword count.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.KeywordRedactor.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a keyword redactor. Empty/null keywords are ignored. If every keyword filters out, the redactor becomes a no-op.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.ProxyRedactorAdapter
member.summary: Bridge that lets the unified telemetry pipeline reuse the legacy (internal to the engine) without duplicating its compiled regex set. New code should prefer the dedicated , , family; this adapter exists so existing proxy callers can opt into the pipeline incrementally.
member.remarks: The underlying always replaces matches with the literal token [REDACTED]; this adapter ignores the supplied beyond surfacing it for diagnostics. Use one of the typed redactors when you need or .

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.ProxyRedactorAdapter.#ctor
member.summary: Creates an adapter around .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext
member.summary: Configuration shared by every : the transformation , the literal mask token and the salt used to derive deterministic hashes when is .
member.remarks: Built-in redactors accept a context in their constructor. Operators may reuse a single context across multiple redactors (typical) or hand each redactor its own context to mix modes (e.g. Mask for connection strings, Hash for PII keys). The context is effectively immutable after construction; mutating it at runtime is not thread-safe.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext.DefaultReplacementToken
member.summary: Default literal token written by .

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext.DefaultHashSalt
member.summary: Default per-environment hash salt. Operators should override.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext.Mode
member.summary: Mode applied to every match.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext.ReplacementToken
member.summary: Literal substring written when is . Built-in redactors may prepend a type-specific prefix, e.g. [REDACTED:JWT].

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext.HashSalt
member.summary: Salt mixed into digests so two environments produce different hashes for the same input. Should be set per-environment via configuration.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext.WithMode(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionMode)
member.summary: Returns a shallow copy with the requested mode override. Useful for preset builders that need a stricter mode for a single redactor.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext.WithToken(System.String)
member.summary: Returns a shallow copy with the requested replacement token.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionHelpers
member.summary: Shared transformation primitives used by every built-in redactor. Centralizing , and guarantees consistent behavior across redactors and is the single place to change replacement formatting later.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionHelpers.Mask(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext,System.String)
member.summary: Returns the literal mask token for the supplied context. Optional yields tokens like [REDACTED:JWT] when the redactor wants to advertise the kind of secret that was scrubbed.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionHelpers.Hash(System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext)
member.summary: Returns a SHA-256 hex digest of mixed with 's salt. The result is lowercase and 64 hex characters long. Returns an empty string for null/empty input so downstream serializers do not write a literal hash of an empty string.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionHelpers.Transform(System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext,System.String)
member.summary: Applies the context's to a captured secret, returning the appropriate replacement string. Returns null for so callers can strip the property entirely.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionMode
member.summary: How a redactor transforms a matched secret or PII fragment.

item.description: replaces the value with the redactor's ReplacementToken (defaults to ***). Cheapest and most readable for log streams.
replaces the value with a SHA-256 hex digest of the value mixed with . Used by audit so analysts can join records by stable hash without learning the raw value.
removes the value entirely (sets the matched property to null or strips the matched substring from the message). Use only when zero leakage is required.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionMode.Mask
member.summary: Replace the value with a static replacement token.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionMode.Hash
member.summary: Replace the value with a salted SHA-256 hex digest.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionMode.Drop
member.summary: Strip the value entirely.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RegexRedactor
member.summary: Generic operator-supplied regex redactor. The matched substring is replaced according to the configured : the entire Match.Value becomes the mask token, hash digest, or is dropped depending on .
member.remarks: Use this for environment-specific patterns (internal account ids, custom token formats). Built-in shapes (email, JWT, etc.) ship as dedicated redactors so operators do not have to maintain their own regex copies.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RegexRedactor.#ctor(System.String,System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a regex redactor. is compiled once and reused for the lifetime of the redactor.
member.param: Redaction mode + replacement controls. Defaults applied when null.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.StructuredFieldRedactor
member.summary: Redacts entries in by key. Operates on the structured property bag instead of the message body, which is the typical pathway for application-supplied PII (e.g. "Email", "Phone", "Ssn").
member.remarks: removes the key entirely. The other modes coerce the existing value via before transforming.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.StructuredFieldRedactor.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Redaction.RedactionContext,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a redactor for the supplied property keys. Key match is case-insensitive (Ordinal).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.BudgetBreachAction
member.summary: Action taken when a directory exceeds its cap.
member.remarks: Logs default to because individual log lines are disposable. Audit defaults to because deleting old audit records may breach compliance and the operator must explicitly opt into purges.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.BudgetBreachAction.DeleteOldest
member.summary: Sweeper deletes oldest files first until the directory is back under budget. Default for logs.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.BudgetBreachAction.BlockNewWrites
member.summary: Producers begin failing fast (audit) until the directory drops back under budget through external means. Default for audit; safe for compliance scenarios.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.BudgetBreachAction.EmitOnly
member.summary: No mutation, just emit the breach event for self-observability (Phase 11) so operators can react manually.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.BudgetSweepResult
member.summary: Outcome of one sweep of a single . Returned by and raised through for self-observability.

member.summary: Scope name (mirrors ).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.BudgetSweepResult.Directory

member.summary: Total bytes in the directory after the sweep.

member.summary: True if the scope is currently in BlockNewWrites state — producers should fail-fast until the next successful sweep clears the flag.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.BudgetSweepResult.LastError
member.summary: Most recent error message, or null.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.DefaultBudgetEnforcer
member.summary: Compress half of . Subscribes to and gzip-compresses every freshly rotated file when the scope's flag is on, then runs an immediate enforcement pass for the directory so a sudden rotation cannot push the directory past the budget unnoticed.
Default implementation. Manages a dictionary of instances keyed by directory and orchestrates compress / sweep work for each scope.
member.remarks:
Split into three partial files:
.Core — fields, ctor, scope registry, dispose.
.Sweep — age / count / budget enforcement loop.
.Compress — gzip-on-rotate handler hooked from sinks.
All disk IO runs on the calling task; the drives a periodic invocation of . Sinks may also trigger work synchronously through .
member.summary: Sweep half of . Applies, in order, the age cap, the file-count cap, and the byte budget for each registered scope.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.DefaultBudgetEnforcer.FindScope(System.String)
member.summary: Resolves the scope registered for a given directory, or null.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.EnforcerScope
member.summary: One unit of work for — a single directory whose telemetry files are governed by the supplied rotation, retention, and budget policies.
member.remarks: A scope is the contract between a sink (which writes files) and the enforcer (which sweeps them). The enforcer never invents scopes; callers register one per directory they care about.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.EnforcerScope.Name
member.summary: Logical name used in diagnostics events. Defaults to the last directory segment if the caller does not supply one.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.EnforcerScope.Directory
member.summary: Absolute directory the scope governs.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.EnforcerScope.FilePattern
member.summary: File-name search pattern (e.g. "beep-*.ndjson*") that matches all files this scope should consider. The trailing * covers both raw and gzipped siblings.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.EnforcerScope.Rotation
member.summary: Rotation policy hint (used by sinks, not the sweeper).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.EnforcerScope.Retention
member.summary: Retention policy applied by the sweeper.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.EnforcerScope.Budget
member.summary: Storage budget enforced by the sweeper.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.EnforcerScope.LastSweptUtc
member.summary: Last time this scope was successfully swept (UTC).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.EnforcerScope.ResolveName
member.summary: Returns the resolved display name (falls back to directory leaf).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.IBudgetEnforcer
member.summary: Owns the on-disk lifecycle of telemetry files: compresses on rotate, sweeps by age / count / budget, and surfaces budget breaches. Implementations must be thread-safe; the hosted service and ad-hoc callers may invoke them concurrently.
member.remarks: One enforcer instance typically governs many directories. Sinks (e.g. ) call once at construction so the enforcer can subscribe to their events and compress / enforce without a polling loop.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.IBudgetEnforcer.RegisterScope(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.EnforcerScope)
member.summary: Registers a directory to sweep on the next pass.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.IBudgetEnforcer.UnregisterScope(System.String)
member.summary: Removes a previously registered scope.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.IBudgetEnforcer.AttachSink(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.FileRollingSink,TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.EnforcerScope)
member.summary: Subscribes the enforcer to a sink's rotation event so that gzip compression and an immediate enforcement pass run as soon as a file rolls.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.IBudgetEnforcer.Scopes
member.summary: Snapshot of the registered scopes.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.IBudgetEnforcer.EnforceAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sweeps a single scope (age, count, then budget). Idempotent; safe to call any time.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.IBudgetEnforcer.EnforceAllAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sweeps every registered scope. Errors are isolated per scope and surfaced through .

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.IBudgetEnforcer.CompressIfNeededAsync(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.RolledFile,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Compresses a freshly rolled file when the scope's budget has on. Safe to call repeatedly for the same file (no-op once compressed).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.IBudgetEnforcer.IsBlocked(System.String)
member.summary: True when the supplied is currently in state. Audit producers should call this and fail-fast when it returns true.

member.name: E:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.IBudgetEnforcer.Swept
member.summary: Raised after every sweep (success or breach).

member.summary: Per-directory retention rules consumed by the budget enforcer. Either or both knobs may be active; the enforcer applies them in the order documented in .

member.summary: Default file count cap (30 files).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.RetentionPolicy.DefaultMaxAgeDays

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.RetentionPolicy.MaxAgeDays

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.RetentionSweeperHostedService
member.summary: Long-running background service that periodically calls on every registered scope. Strictly opt-in: registration helpers add this service only when the operator turns on the retention sweeper.
member.remarks: The service uses for a low-allocation cadence and tolerates individual scope failures: a thrown exception is swallowed (the enforcer reports it through ) so a single bad directory cannot take the host process down.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.RetentionSweeperHostedService.DefaultSweepInterval
member.summary: Default cadence between sweeps (5 minutes).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.RetentionSweeperHostedService.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.IBudgetEnforcer,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.RetentionSweeperHostedService.SweepInterval
member.summary: Configured cadence between sweeps.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.RotationPolicy
member.summary: Operator-facing knobs that tell a rolling sink when to close the current file and start a new one. The sink itself owns the decision; this policy is a data-only POCO so it can be persisted, diffed, and shipped through configuration.
member.remarks: Phase 03 hard-coded equivalent values inline in ; Phase 04 surfaces them through this policy so callers can configure them at registration time.

member.summary: Default rolling threshold (5 MB).

member.summary: Default wall-clock roll interval (24 hours).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.RotationPolicy.MaxFileBytes
member.summary: Maximum bytes per file before rotation. Set to a non-positive value to disable size-based rotation.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.RotationPolicy.RollInterval

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.RotationPolicy.MaxEventsPerFile
member.summary: Optional cap on envelope count per file. Sinks that do not track per-file counts (SQLite) ignore this knob.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.RotationPolicy.Clone

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.StorageBudget.DefaultMaxTotalBytes

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.StorageBudget.MaxTotalBytes
member.summary: Maximum total bytes for the directory. Set to a non-positive value to disable the cap (not recommended on mobile / browser hosts).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.StorageBudget.CompressOnRotate
member.summary: When true (the default for logs) the enforcer compresses every .ndjson sibling that has just rolled. Disable on CPU-constrained hosts (MAUI / Blazor) where the gzip cost outweighs the disk savings.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.StorageBudget.OnBreach
member.summary: What the enforcer does when the directory exceeds . See for the available behaviors.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Retention.StorageBudget.Clone
member.summary: Convenience copy used by the registration helpers.

member.summary: Pinning helper that always returns true. Useful in tests and as an explicit "I deliberately disabled sampling" marker in configuration code.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sampling.AlwaysSampler.Instance
member.summary: Shared singleton instance.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sampling.CategorySampler
member.summary: Applies a per-category keep rate. Envelopes whose matches the configured category (case-insensitive Ordinal) are sampled; all others pass through.
member.remarks: Use to throttle a single noisy component (e.g. a tight inner loop tagged "HotLoop") without affecting the rest of the application's logging volume.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sampling.CategorySampler.#ctor(System.String,System.Double)
member.summary: Creates a category sampler.
member.param: Category name to throttle (must be non-empty).
Keep rate in [0, 1]. Clamped if outside.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sampling.CategorySampler.Category
member.summary: The throttled category.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sampling.CategorySampler.Rate
member.summary: The keep rate applied to matching envelopes.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sampling.LevelSampler
member.summary: Drops a configurable fraction of envelopes at or below a threshold level; envelopes more severe than the threshold are always kept. Use to thin out chatty Trace/Debug traffic without ever dropping warnings or errors.
member.remarks: Decision is deterministic per correlationId/traceId so either every envelope of a given trace is sampled in or none is (avoids sparse, half-correlated traces). Audit envelopes never reach any sampler — see the pipeline guard.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sampling.LevelSampler.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Logging.BeepLogLevel,System.Double)
member.summary: Creates a level sampler. is clamped to [0, 1]; 1.0 is a no-op (everything kept) and 0.0 drops all envelopes at/below the threshold.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sampling.LevelSampler.Threshold
member.summary: The level at or below which sampling applies.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sampling.LevelSampler.Rate
member.summary: The keep rate applied to envelopes at/below the threshold.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sampling.NeverSampler
member.summary: Pinning helper that always returns false. Useful for blackholing a category temporarily without removing the rest of the pipeline configuration.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sampling.NeverSampler.Instance
member.summary: Shared singleton instance.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sampling.SamplingDecision
member.summary: Shared rate-comparison primitive used by every built-in sampler. Deterministic when an envelope carries a non-empty correlation/trace id (a single trace stays whole or wholly dropped); falls back to a thread-local otherwise.
member.remarks: The deterministic path uses FNV-1a over the correlation id rather than because the latter is salted per-process on modern .NET — that would make sampling decisions non-reproducible across host restarts and break replay-style debugging.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sampling.SamplingDecision.Keep(TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryEnvelope,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns true for envelopes that should be kept given the supplied (clamped to [0, 1]).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.CompositeSink
member.summary: Fan-out sink that forwards every batch to a fixed list of inner sinks. Per-inner exceptions are caught and reflected through so a single failing destination cannot take the rest of the pipeline down with it.
member.remarks: Composite isolation is layered on top of the per-sink try/catch in . The still sees the composite as one sink, so a partial failure inside the composite does not increment its sink-error counter; instead the failure surfaces here through and the per-sink unhealthy bit.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.CompositeSink.#ctor(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.ITelemetrySink})
member.summary: Creates a composite that forwards to .

member.summary: Most recent inner failure message, or null.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.CompositeSink.Inner
member.summary: Inner sinks in the order they were registered.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.FileRollingSink
member.summary: Cross-platform NDJSON file sink that rolls by size and / or wall clock. Phase 03 owns write + roll only; compression and retention run from Phase 04 by subscribing to .
member.remarks:
Split into three partial files:
.Core — fields, ctor, dispose, public state.
.Write — batch append + flush to disk.
.Roll — rotation policy + event.
Only one open file handle is held at a time per sink instance, so the sink is safe for hosts with strict file-descriptor budgets (mobile / containers).
member.summary: Phase 11 health-probe surface for . Exposes the existing healthy / last-error / written-count fields plus per-write timestamps so can report richer self-observability data without changing the core write path.
Rotation half of . Decides when to roll, performs the swap, and surfaces the closed file via the event so Phase 04 can compress it and enforce retention.
Append + flush half of . All disk IO runs under _writeGate so a single sink instance may be safely shared by the pipeline drain loop and ad-hoc flush calls.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.MemorySink
member.summary: In-process ring buffer sink. Stores up to envelopes; oldest entries are evicted when the cap is exceeded. Useful for unit tests and as a low-storage fallback on hosts where disk is unavailable.
member.remarks: The buffer is backed by a + an atomic counter so producers (the pipeline drain task) and readers (test code calling ) never lock. The trade-off is that a transient over-shoot of one or two items above is possible during heavy concurrent writes; the cap is treated as a soft target.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.Platform.BlazorIndexedDbSink
member.summary: Telemetry sink that persists envelopes inside the browser's IndexedDB store via a host-supplied . The sink itself is portable C# so it builds on every TFM; the JS interop is delegated to the host package wwwroot/beep-telemetry/beep-indexeddb.js.
member.remarks:
Split into three partial files:
.Core — fields, ctor, public state, dispose.
.Write — batch serialization + IDB put + budget pruning.
.Health — surface.
member.summary: surface for . Tracks last success / error timestamps and consecutive failures so the reports a meaningful status when IndexedDB throws (typically QuotaExceededError on tight browsers).
Write + flush + budget-pruning logic for . The sink converts each envelope into one NDJSON line (so the same canonical shape used by survives) and hands the batch to the in a single call.

member.summary: Host-supplied JS-interop seam for the . The core library never references Microsoft.JSInterop directly so the Telemetry pipeline keeps building on every TFM (net8 / net9 / net10) and non-Blazor hosts pay nothing.
member.remarks: Implementations live in the Blazor host project and wrap calls like IJSRuntime.InvokeAsync("beepTelemetry.put", …) to the bundled wwwroot/beep-telemetry/beep-indexeddb.js helper. Every method must be safe to call concurrently from the batch-writer drain thread.
