member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.Platform.MauiAppDataSink
member.summary: Factory that produces a tuned for MAUI hosts (Android / iOS / Mac Catalyst / Windows). Implemented as a static factory rather than a subclass because is sealed (its internal write path holds an exclusive file handle and cannot be safely inherited).
member.remarks:
MAUI quirks the defaults compensate for:
Smaller MaxFileBytes (1 MB) so flush latency stays low on slow flash storage.
Shorter rotation interval (15 min) so the OS gets a chance to evict / index closed files between rolls.
The library never references Microsoft.Maui.Storage directly so the core stays cross-target. Callers pass a delegate that returns FileSystem.AppDataDirectory at startup.

member.summary: Creates a rooted at {appDataDirectoryProvider()}/{appName}/{subfolder}.

Per-app folder under the OS app-data root.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.RedactingSinkDecorator
member.summary: Wraps an inner with a per-sink redactor stack. Used when a single sink (e.g. an audit SQLite store) requires stricter redaction than the global pipeline chain.
member.remarks: Each batch is cloned before redaction so the in-place mutations made by the sink-local redactors do not leak back to other fan-out sinks in the same write. Cloning cost is paid once per envelope per decorated sink — comparable to a property-bag copy — so this should only be used where the stricter rules really are needed.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.SqliteSink
member.summary: Cross-platform SQLite sink for the unified telemetry pipeline. Backed by Microsoft.Data.Sqlite so the same code runs on Windows, Linux, macOS, and MAUI without an OS-specific provider.
member.remarks:
Split into four partial files:
.Core — fields, ctor, connection lifecycle, health.
.Schema — PRAGMAs, CREATE TABLE / INDEX bootstrap.
.Write — batched insert in a single transaction.
.Query — diagnostic read helpers. Full query API lands in Phase 10.
One open connection per sink instance keeps the file-handle and memory cost predictable; concurrent writers are serialized through _writeGate. WAL mode is enabled so the database survives process kill mid-batch.
member.summary: Phase 11 health-probe surface for . Pulls last-success / last-error timestamps from the existing write path without holding the write gate so the probe can run from any thread.

Phase 10 purge partial of . Implements so the GDPR purge service can run over a SQLite-backed audit store. Every operation is performed inside a single transaction and uses the same write gate as inserts so concurrent producers stay correct.

Read-side helpers for . Phase 03 ships the minimum needed for diagnostics (row counts, oldest timestamp); the typed audit query API (QueryAsync(AuditQuery)) lands in Phase 10 and will reuse the same connection + gate.
Phase 10 read partial of . Translates an into a parameterized SQL statement over the telemetry table and re-hydrates audit events from the stored audit_json column.
member.remarks: All filter fields with their own column (timestamp, user, entity, record key) are translated to native predicates so the partial indexes from Phase 03 stay effective. Fields that only exist inside the JSON envelope (category, operation, outcome, tenant, chain id, properties) are filtered post-hydration via — the query still pushes a reasonable upper bound on rows fetched (Take * 4) to keep result-set size bounded for huge datasets.
member.summary: Phase 10 log-read partial of . Translates a into a parameterized SQL statement over the telemetry table where kind = 0 and rehydrates instances. Audit rows are excluded so a single sink can serve both engines without bleeding rows from one channel into the other.
Schema bootstrap half of . Idempotent PRAGMA + CREATE statements applied once on first open of the connection.
member.remarks: Schema v1 stores both logs and audit in a single denormalized telemetry table. Audit-only fields (audit_user, audit_entity, audit_record_key) are populated only when kind = 1 (Audit) and indexed via partial indexes so log inserts pay no index cost for fields they do not use. Phase 08 adds chain_id, seq, prev_hash, hash through an ALTER TABLE migration tracked in the meta table.
member.summary: Insert + flush half of . Each call wraps the entire batch in a single transaction so the database round-trip cost is O(1) per batch, not O(envelope).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.SqliteSink.SyncNormal
member.summary: SQLite synchronous=NORMAL — high throughput, recoverable via WAL.

member.summary: SQLite synchronous=FULL — strict durability for audit-sensitive deployments.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.Test.FlakySink
member.summary: Deterministic fault-injection sink. Throws an on every Nth call to ; all other calls succeed and forward the batch to an inner sink (or simply count it when no inner sink is supplied).
member.remarks:
Use this sink to verify pipeline robustness:
Logs: producer never observes the failure (drop is silent).
Audit: failure surfaces through and the sink-error self event from Phase 11.
The failure cadence is fully deterministic (FailEvery) so tests do not depend on RNG state.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.Test.FlakySink.#ctor(System.Int32,TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.ITelemetrySink,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a flaky sink that fails on every th call. is optional; when supplied the successful batches are forwarded to it (typically a or ).

member.summary: Total batches the sink intentionally failed.

member.summary: Total envelopes successfully forwarded to the inner sink.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.Test.FullDiskSink
member.summary: Storage-exhaustion fault injection sink. Tracks an estimated byte count from length plus a fixed per-envelope overhead, and once is reached every subsequent throws an with a "disk full" message.
member.remarks:
Use this sink to verify:
The pipeline marks the sink unhealthy and emits the BeepTelemetry.Self.Sink error event.
The retention sweeper / budget enforcer recovers space and clears the unhealthy state once is called.
The byte estimate intentionally over-counts so that small test budgets trigger predictably.

member.remarks:
Split into two partial files:
.Core — fields, constructor, write path, lifecycle.

The buffer uses a + atomic counter so tests can call from any thread without a lock and without freezing the producer drain task.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.Sinks.Test.SlowSink
member.summary: Latency-injection sink. Awaits before each successful call. Useful to stress the bounded queue: with a slow sink, the producer side will hit the back-pressure path (drop or block) before the writer drains.
member.remarks: The sink uses rather than so it does not hold the underlying writer thread. Tests can stop the simulated latency early by cancelling the supplied .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryEnvelope
member.summary: Cloning support for . Used by the per-sink redaction path so a sink-local mutation cannot leak into other sinks running in the same fan-out.

Single envelope type carried from producer (BeepLog / BeepAudit) all the way through the queue, enrichers, redactors, samplers and sinks. One envelope class lets logs and audit share a single bounded queue without re-ordering relative to each other.
member.remarks: Mutable on purpose: enrichers (Phase 06) and redactors (Phase 05) edit the envelope in-place to avoid per-event allocations on the hot path. Sinks must treat the envelope as read-only once they receive it.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryEnvelope.Clone
member.summary: Returns a shallow envelope copy with the property bag duplicated. The reference and instance are shared because both are treated as immutable past envelope creation.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryKind
member.summary: Discriminator carried on every so the pipeline (and individual sinks) can tell logs from audit and apply the correct policy. Audit envelopes bypass samplers and never use DropOldest backpressure.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryKind.Log
member.summary: Diagnostic log entry (lossy by default).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryKind.Audit
member.summary: Audit-trail event (lossless by policy).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryKind.Self
member.summary: Pipeline self-observability event (e.g. drop counter, sink error). Reserved for Phase 11; Phase 02 does not emit these.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryPipeline
member.summary: Shared, opt-in telemetry orchestrator that backs both and . The pipeline holds the bounded queue, the enricher / redactor / sampler stages, the fan-out batch writer, and the lifetime management.
member.remarks:
The class is split across three partial files:
.Core — fields, ctor, stage wiring, Submit entry points.
.Drain — stage application before enqueue.
.Flush — graceful shutdown / FlushAsync.
Construction allocates exactly one queue and one drain task. When no sinks are registered the pipeline still accepts envelopes (they are dropped at dispatch) so callers can pre-wire before sinks land.
member.summary: Stage application + enqueue half of . Logging producers call from the synchronous hot path; audit producers call so semantics are honored.
Lifetime / flush half of . Owns the cooperative shutdown contract callers see through and .

member.name: E:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryPipeline.SinkErrored
member.summary: Raised once per per-sink exception caught by the batch writer. Phase 11 hooks subscribe here to feed and .
Creates a new pipeline. Caller owns the lifetime; dispose during host shutdown via .

member.summary: Synchronous submit used by . Applies enrichers, redactors, samplers (in that order) before attempting a non-blocking enqueue. Returns false when the envelope was dropped (filtered out or queue overflow).

member.summary: Asynchronous submit used by . Applies enrichers and redactors but always bypasses samplers, then awaits queue capacity per the configured backpressure mode.

member.summary: Drains the queue and asks every sink to flush within the supplied . Safe to call repeatedly.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Services.Telemetry.TelemetryPipeline.DisposeAsync
member.summary: Disposes the pipeline. Completes the queue, cancels the drain loop, awaits its shutdown, then disposes every sink.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.WebAPI.WebAPIDataSource
member.summary: Connection and transaction skeleton implementations.
Core partial containing only state (properties/fields), constructor and disposal logic. All IDataSource method implementations are split into separate partial skeleton files.
Data retrieval & CRUD operations using helpers.
HTTP convenience methods for common Web API calls (GET, etc.)
Query & scalar interface stubs.
Scripting & entity creation interface stubs (no implementation per requirements).
Structure and metadata related methods.
IWebAPIDataSource specific members (skeleton implementation).
